1Kings1:32 And king David said
1Kings1:33 And the king said unto them
1Kings1:34 and let Zadok the priest and Nathan the prophet anoint him there king over Israel; and blow ye the trumpet
1Kings1:35 Then ye shall come up after him
1Kings1:36 And Benaiah the son of Jehoiada answered the king
1Kings1:37 As Jehovah hath been with my lord the king
1Kings1:38 So Zadok the priest
1Kings1:39 And Zadok the priest took the horn of oil out of the Tent
1Kings1:40 And all the people came up after him
1Kings1:41 And Adonijah and all the guests that were with him heard it as they had made an end of eating. And when Joab heard the sound of the trumpet
1Kings1:42 While he yet spake
1Kings1:43 And Jonathan answered and said to Adonijah
1Kings1:44 and the king hath sent with him Zadok the priest
1Kings1:45 and Zadok the priest and Nathan the prophet have anointed him king in Gihon; and they are come up from thence rejoicing
1Kings1:46 And also Solomon sitteth on the throne of the kingdom.
1Kings1:47 And moreover the kings servants came to bless our lord king David
1Kings1:48 And also thus said the king
1Kings1:49 And all the guests of Adonijah were afraid
1Kings1:50 And Adonijah feared because of Solomon; and he arose
1Kings1:51 And it was told Solomon
1Kings1:52 And Solomon said
1Kings1:53 So king Solomon sent
1Kings2:1 Now the days of David drew nigh that he should die; and he charged Solomon his son
1Kings2:2 I am going the way of all the earth: be thou strong therefore
1Kings2:3 and keep the charge of Jehovah thy God
1Kings2:4 That Jehovah may establish his word which he spake concerning me
1Kings2:5 Moreover thou knowest also what Joab the son of Zeruiah did unto me
1Kings2:6 Do therefore according to thy wisdom
1Kings2:7 But show kindness unto the sons of Barzillai the Gileadite
1Kings2:8 And
1Kings2:9 Now therefore hold him not guiltless
1Kings2:10 And David slept with his fathers
1Kings2:11 And the days that David reigned over Israel were forty years; seven years reigned he in Hebron
1Kings2:12 And Solomon sat upon the throne of David his father; and his kingdom was established greatly.
1Kings2:13 Then Adonijah the son of Haggith came to Bath-sheba the mother of Solomon. And she said
1Kings2:14 He said moreover
1Kings2:15 And he said
1Kings2:16 And now I ask one petition of thee; deny me not. And she said unto him
1Kings2:17 And he said
1Kings2:18 And Bath-sheba said
1Kings2:19 Bath-sheba therefore went unto king Solomon
1Kings2:20 Then she said
1Kings2:21 And she said
1Kings2:22 And king Solomon answered and said unto his mother
1Kings2:23 Then king Solomon sware by Jehovah
1Kings2:24 Now therefore as Jehovah liveth
1Kings2:25 And king Solomon sent by Benaiah the son of Jehoiada; and he fell upon him
1Kings2:26 And unto Abiathar the priest said the king
1Kings2:27 So Solomon thrust out Abiathar from being priest unto Jehovah
1Kings2:28 And the tidings came to Joab; for Joab had turned after Adonijah
1Kings2:29 And it was told king Solomon
1Kings2:30 And Benaiah came to the Tent of Jehovah
1Kings2:31 And the king said unto him
1Kings2:32 And Jehovah will return his blood upon his own head
1Kings2:33 So shall their blood return upon the head of Joab
1Kings2:34 Then Benaiah the son of Jehoiada went up
1Kings2:35 And the king put Benaiah the son of Jehoiada in his room over the host; and Zadok the priest did the king put in the room of Abiathar.
1Kings2:36 And the king sent and called for Shimei
1Kings2:37 For on the day thou goest out
1Kings2:38 And Shimei said unto the king
1Kings2:39 And it came to pass at the end of three years
1Kings2:40 And Shimei arose
1Kings2:41 And it was told Solomon that Shimei had gone from Jerusalem to Gath
1Kings2:42 And the king sent and called for Shimei
1Kings2:43 Why then hast thou not kept the oath of Jehovah
1Kings2:44 The king said moreover to Shimei
1Kings2:45 But king Solomon shall be blessed
1Kings2:46 So the king commanded Benaiah the son of Jehoiada; and he went out
1Kings3:1 And Solomon made affinity with Pharaoh king of Egypt
1Kings3:2 Only the people sacrificed in the high places
1Kings3:3 And Solomon loved Jehovah
1Kings3:4 And the king went to Gibeon to sacrifice there; for that was the great high place: a thousand burnt-offerings did Solomon offer upon that altar.
1Kings3:5 In Gibeon Jehovah appeared to Solomon in a dream by night; and God said
1Kings3:6 And Solomon said
1Kings3:7 And now
1Kings3:8 And thy servant is in the midst of thy people which thou hast chosen
1Kings3:9 Give thy servant therefore an understanding heart to judge thy people
1Kings3:10 And the speech pleased the Lord
1Kings3:11 And God said unto him
1Kings3:12 behold
1Kings3:13 And I have also given thee that which thou hast not asked
1Kings3:14 And if thou wilt walk in my ways
1Kings3:15 And Solomon awoke; and
1Kings3:16 Then there came two women that were harlots
1Kings3:17 And the one woman said
1Kings3:18 And it came to pass the third day after I was delivered
1Kings3:19 And this womans child died in the night
1Kings3:20 And she arose at midnight
1Kings3:21 And when I rose in the morning to give my child suck
1Kings3:22 And the other woman said
1Kings3:23 Then said the king
1Kings3:24 And the king said
1Kings3:25 And the king said
1Kings3:26 Then spake the woman whose the living child was unto the king
1Kings3:27 Then the king answered and said
1Kings3:28 And all Israel heard of the judgment which the king had judged; and they feared the king: for they saw that the wisdom of God was in him
1Kings4:1 And king Solomon was king over all Israel.
1Kings4:2 And these were the princes whom he had: Azariah the son of Zadok
1Kings4:3 Elihoreph and Ahijah
1Kings4:4 and Benaiah the son of Jehoiada was over the host; and Zadok and Abiathar were priests;
1Kings4:5 and Azariah the son of Nathan was over the officers; and Zabud the son of Nathan was chief minister
1Kings4:6 and Ahishar was over the household; and Adoniram the son of Abda was over the men subject to taskwork.
1Kings4:7 And Solomon had twelve officers over all Israel
1Kings4:8 And these are their names: Ben-hur
1Kings4:9 Ben-deker
1Kings4:10 Ben-hesed
1Kings4:11 Ben-abinadab
1Kings4:12 Baana the son of Ahilud
1Kings4:13 Ben-geber
1Kings4:14 Ahinadab the son of Iddo
1Kings4:15 Ahimaaz
1Kings4:16 Baana the son of Hushai
1Kings4:17 Jehoshaphat the son of Paruah
1Kings4:18 Shimei the son of Ela
1Kings4:19 Geber the son of Uri
1Kings4:20 Judah and Israel were many as the sand which is by the sea in multitude
1Kings4:21 And Solomon ruled over all the kingdoms from the River unto the land of the Philistines
1Kings4:22 And Solomons provision for one day was thirty measures of fine flour
1Kings4:23 ten fat oxen
1Kings4:24 For he had dominion over all the region on this side the River
1Kings4:25 And Judah and Israel dwelt safely
1Kings4:26 And Solomon had forty thousand stalls of horses for his chariots
1Kings4:27 And those officers provided victuals for king Solomon
1Kings4:28 Barley also and straw for the horses and swift steeds brought they unto the place where the officers were
1Kings4:29 And God gave Solomon wisdom and understanding exceeding much
1Kings4:30 And Solomons wisdom excelled the wisdom of all the children of the east
1Kings4:31 For he was wiser than all men; than Ethan the Ezrahite
1Kings4:32 And he spake three thousand proverbs; and his songs were a thousand and five.
1Kings4:33 And he spake of trees
1Kings4:34 And there came of all peoples to hear the wisdom of Solomon
1Kings5:1 And Hiram king of Tyre sent his servants unto Solomon; for he had heard that they had anointed him king in the room of his father: for Hiram was ever a lover of David.
1Kings5:2 And Solomon sent to Hiram
1Kings5:3 Thou knowest how that David my father could not build a house for the name of Jehovah his God for the wars which were about him on every side
1Kings5:4 But now Jehovah my God hath given me rest on every side; there is neither adversary
1Kings5:5 And
1Kings5:6 Now therefore command thou that they cut me cedar-trees out of Lebanon; and my servants shall be with thy servants; and I will give thee hire for thy servants according to all that thou shalt say: for thou knowest that there is not among us any that knoweth how to cut timber like unto the Sidonians.
1Kings5:7 And it came to pass
1Kings5:8 And Hiram sent to Solomon
1Kings5:9 My servants shall bring them down from Lebanon unto the sea; and I will make them into rafts to go by sea unto the place that thou shalt appoint me
1Kings5:10 So Hiram gave Solomon timber of cedar and timber of fir according to all his desire.
1Kings5:11 And Solomon gave Hiram twenty thousand measures of wheat for food to his household
1Kings5:12 And Jehovah gave Solomon wisdom
1Kings5:13 And king Solomon raised a levy out of all Israel; and the levy was thirty thousand men.
1Kings5:14 And he sent them to Lebanon
1Kings5:15 And Solomon had threescore and ten thousand that bare burdens
1Kings5:16 besides Solomons chief officers that were over the work
1Kings5:17 And the king commanded
1Kings5:18 And Solomons builders and Hirams builders and the Gebalites did fashion them
1Kings6:1 And it came to pass in the four hundred and eightieth year after the children of Israel were come out of the land of Egypt
1Kings6:2 And the house which king Solomon built for Jehovah
1Kings6:3 And the porch before the temple of the house
1Kings6:4 And for the house he made windows of fixed lattice-work.
1Kings6:5 And against the wall of the house he built stories round about
1Kings6:6 The nethermost story was five cubits broad
1Kings6:7 And the house
1Kings6:8 The door for the middle side-chambers was in the right side of the house: and they went up by winding stairs into the middle story
1Kings6:9 So he built the house
1Kings6:10 And he built the stories against all the house
1Kings6:11 And the word of Jehovah came to Solomon
1Kings6:12 Concerning this house which thou art building
1Kings6:13 And I will dwell among the children of Israel
1Kings6:14 So Solomon built the house
1Kings6:15 And he built the walls of the house within with boards of cedar: from the floor of the house unto the walls of the ceiling
1Kings6:16 And he built twenty cubits on the hinder part of the house with boards of cedar from the floor unto the walls of the ceiling: he built them for it within
1Kings6:17 And the house
1Kings6:18 And there was cedar on the house within
1Kings6:19 And he prepared an oracle in the midst of the house within
1Kings6:20 And within the oracle was a space of twenty cubits in length
1Kings6:21 So Solomon overlaid the house within with pure gold: and he drew chains of gold across before the oracle; and he overlaid it with gold.
1Kings6:22 And the whole house he overlaid with gold
1Kings6:23 And in the oracle he made two cherubim of olive-wood
1Kings6:24 And five cubits was the one wing of the cherub
1Kings6:25 And the other cherub was ten cubits: both the cherubim were of one measure and one form.
1Kings6:26 The height of the one cherub was ten cubits
1Kings6:27 And he set the cherubim within the inner house; and the wings of the cherubim were stretched forth
1Kings6:28 And he overlaid the cherubim with gold.
1Kings6:29 And he carved all the walls of the house round about with carved figures of cherubim and palm-trees and open flowers
1Kings6:30 And the floor of the house he overlaid with gold
1Kings6:31 And for the entrance of the oracle he made doors of olive-wood: the lintel and door-posts were a fifth part of the wall.
1Kings6:32 So he made two doors of olive-wood; and he carved upon them carvings of cherubim and palm-trees and open flowers
1Kings6:33 So also made he for the entrance of the temple door-posts of olive-wood
1Kings6:34 and two doors of fir-wood: the two leaves of the one door were folding
1Kings6:35 And he carved thereon cherubim and palm-trees and open flowers; and he overlaid them with gold fitted upon the graven work.
1Kings6:36 And he built the inner court with three courses of hewn stone
1Kings6:37 In the fourth year was the foundation of the house of Jehovah laid
1Kings6:38 And in the eleventh year
1Kings7:1 And Solomon was building his own house thirteen years
1Kings7:2 For he built the house of the forest of Lebanon; the length thereof was a hundred cubits
1Kings7:3 And it was covered with cedar above over the forty and five beams
1Kings7:4 And there were beams in three rows
1Kings7:5 And all the doors and posts were made square with beams: and window was over against window in three ranks.
1Kings7:6 And he made the porch of pillars; the length thereof was fifty cubits
1Kings7:7 And he made the porch of the throne where he was to judge
1Kings7:8 And his house where he was to dwell
1Kings7:9 All these were of costly stones
1Kings7:10 And the foundation was of costly stones
1Kings7:11 And above were costly stones
1Kings7:12 And the great court round about had three courses of hewn stone
1Kings7:13 And king Solomon sent and fetched Hiram out of Tyre.
1Kings7:14 He was the son of a widow of the tribe of Naphtali
1Kings7:15 For he fashioned the two pillars of brass
1Kings7:16 And he made two capitals of molten brass
1Kings7:17 There were nets of checker-work
1Kings7:18 So he made the pillars; and there were two rows round about upon the one network
1Kings7:19 And the capitals that were upon the top of the pillars in the porch were of lily-work
1Kings7:20 And there were capitals above also upon the two pillars
1Kings7:21 And he set up the pillars at the porch of the temple: and he set up the right pillar
1Kings7:22 And upon the top of the pillars was lily-work: so was the work of the pillars finished.
1Kings7:23 And he made the molten sea of ten cubits from brim to brim
1Kings7:24 And under the brim of it round about there were knops which did compass it
1Kings7:25 It stood upon twelve oxen
1Kings7:26 And it was a handbreadth thick: and the brim thereof was wrought like the brim of a cup
1Kings7:27 And he made the ten bases of brass; four cubits was the length of one base
1Kings7:28 And the work of the bases was on this manner: they had panels; and there were panels between the ledges;
1Kings7:29 and on the panels that were between the ledges were lions
1Kings7:30 And every base had four brazen wheels
1Kings7:31 And the mouth of it within the capital and above was a cubit: and the mouth thereof was round after the work of a pedestal
1Kings7:32 And the four wheels were underneath the panels; and the axletrees of the wheels were in the base: and the height of a wheel was a cubit and half a cubit.
1Kings7:33 And the work of the wheels was like the work of a chariot wheel: their axletrees
1Kings7:34 And there were four undersetters at the four corners of each base: the undersetters thereof were of the base itself.
1Kings7:35 And in the top of the base was there a round compass half a cubit high; and on the top of the base the stays thereof and the panels thereof were of the same.
1Kings7:36 And on the plates of the stays thereof
1Kings7:37 After this manner he made the ten bases: all of them had one casting
1Kings7:38 And he made ten lavers of brass: one laver contained forty baths; and every laver was four cubits; and upon very one of the ten bases one laver.
1Kings7:39 And he set the bases
1Kings7:40 And Hiram made the lavers
1Kings7:41 the two pillars
1Kings7:42 and the four hundred pomegranates for the two networks; two rows of pomegranates for each network
1Kings7:43 and the ten bases
1Kings7:44 and the one sea
1Kings7:45 and the pots
1Kings7:46 In the plain of the Jordan did the king cast them
1Kings7:47 And Solomon left all the vessels unweighed
1Kings7:48 And Solomon made all the vessels that were in the house of Jehovah: the golden altar
1Kings7:49 and the candlesticks
1Kings7:50 and the cups
1Kings7:51 Thus all the work that king Solomon wrought in the house of Jehovah was finished. And Solomon brought in the things which David his father had dedicated
1Kings8:1 Then Solomon assembled the elders of Israel
1Kings8:2 And all the men of Israel assembled themselves unto king Solomon at the feast
1Kings8:3 And all the elders of Israel came
1Kings8:4 And they brought up the ark of Jehovah
1Kings8:5 And king Solomon and all the congregation of Israel
1Kings8:6 And the priests brought in the ark of the covenant of Jehovah unto its place
1Kings8:7 For the cherubim spread forth their wings over the place of the ark
1Kings8:8 And the staves were so long that the ends of the staves were seen from the holy place before the oracle; but they were not seen without: and there they are unto this day.
1Kings8:9 There was nothing in the ark save the two tables of stone which Moses put there at Horeb
1Kings8:10 And it came to pass
1Kings8:11 so that the priests could not stand to minister by reason of the cloud; for the glory of Jehovah filled the house of Jehovah.
1Kings8:12 Then spake Solomon
1Kings8:13 I have surely built thee a house of habitation
1Kings8:14 And the king turned his face about
1Kings8:15 And he said
1Kings8:16 Since the day that I brought forth my people Israel out of Egypt
1Kings8:17 Now it was in the heart of David my father to build a house for the name of Jehovah
1Kings8:18 But Jehovah said unto David my father
1Kings8:19 nevertheless thou shalt not build the house; but thy son that shall come forth out of thy loins
1Kings8:20 And Jehovah hath established his word that he spake; for I am risen up in the room of David my father
1Kings8:21 And there have I set a place for the ark
1Kings8:22 And Solomon stood before the altar of Jehovah in the presence of all the assembly of Israel
1Kings8:23 and he said
1Kings8:24 who hast kept with thy servant David my father that which thou didst promise him: yea
1Kings8:25 Now therefore
1Kings8:26 Now therefore
1Kings8:27 But will God in very deed dwell on the earth? behold
1Kings8:28 Yet have thou respect unto the prayer of thy servant
1Kings8:29 that thine eyes may be open toward this house night and day
1Kings8:30 And hearken thou to the supplication of thy servant
1Kings8:31 If a man sin against his neighbor
1Kings8:32 then hear thou in heaven
1Kings8:33 When thy people Israel are smitten down before the enemy
1Kings8:34 then hear thou in heaven
1Kings8:35 When heaven is shut up
1Kings8:36 then hear thou in heaven
1Kings8:37 If there be in the land famine
1Kings8:38 what prayer and supplication soever be made by any man
1Kings8:39 then hear thou in heaven thy dwelling-place
1Kings8:40 that they may fear thee all the days that they live in the land which thou gavest unto our fathers.
1Kings8:41 Moreover concerning the foreigner
1Kings8:42 (for they shall hear of thy great name
1Kings8:43 hear thou in heaven thy dwelling-place
1Kings8:44 If thy people go out to battle against their enemy
1Kings8:45 then hear thou in heaven their prayer and their supplication
1Kings8:46 If they sin against thee (for there is no man that sinneth not)
1Kings8:47 yet if they shall bethink themselves in the land whither they are carried captive
1Kings8:48 if they return unto thee with all their heart and with all their soul in the land of their enemies
1Kings8:49 then hear thou their prayer and their supplication in heaven thy dwelling-place
1Kings8:50 and forgive thy people who have sinned against thee
1Kings8:51 (for they are thy people
1Kings8:52 that thine eyes may be open unto the supplication of thy servant
1Kings8:53 For thou didst separate them from among all the peoples of the earth
1Kings8:54 And it was so
1Kings8:55 And he stood
1Kings8:56 Blessed be Jehovah
1Kings8:57 Jehovah our God be with us
1Kings8:58 that he may incline our hearts unto him
1Kings8:59 And let these my words
1Kings8:60 that all the peoples of the earth may know that Jehovah
1Kings8:61 Let your heart therefore be perfect with Jehovah our God
1Kings8:62 And the king
1Kings8:63 And Solomon offered for the sacrifice of peace-offerings
1Kings8:64 The same day did the king hallow the middle of the court that was before the house of Jehovah; for there he offered the burnt-offering
1Kings8:65 So Solomon held the feast at that time
1Kings8:66 On the eighth day he sent the people away; and they blessed the king
1Kings9:1 And it came to pass
1Kings9:2 that Jehovah appeared to Solomon the second time
1Kings9:3 And Jehovah said unto him
1Kings9:4 And as for thee
1Kings9:5 then I will establish the throne of thy kingdom over Israel for ever
1Kings9:6 But if ye shall turn away from following me
1Kings9:7 then will I cut off Israel out of the land which I have given them; and this house
1Kings9:8 And though this house is so high
1Kings9:9 and they shall answer
1Kings9:10 And it came to pass at the end of twenty years
1Kings9:11 (now Hiram the king of Tyre had furnished Solomon with cedar-trees and fir-trees
1Kings9:12 And Hiram came out from Tyre to see the cities which Solomon had given him; and they pleased him not.
1Kings9:13 And he said
1Kings9:14 And Hiram sent to the king sixscore talents of gold.
1Kings9:15 And this is the reason of the levy which king Solomon raised
1Kings9:16 Pharaoh king of Egypt had gone up
1Kings9:17 And Solomon built Gezer
1Kings9:18 and Baalath
1Kings9:19 and all the store-cities that Solomon had
1Kings9:20 As for all the people that were left of the Amorites
1Kings9:21 their children that were left after them in the land
1Kings9:22 But of the children of Israel did Solomon make no bondservants; but they were the men of war
1Kings9:23 These were the chief officers that were over Solomons work
1Kings9:24 But Pharaohs daughter came up out of the city of David unto her house which Solomon had built for her: then did he build Millo.
1Kings9:25 And three times a year did Solomon offer burnt-offerings and peace-offerings upon the altar which he built unto Jehovah
1Kings9:26 And king Solomon made a navy of ships in Ezion-geber
1Kings9:27 And Hiram sent in the navy his servants
1Kings9:28 And they came to Ophir
1Kings10:1 And when the queen of Sheba heard of the fame of Solomon concerning the name of Jehovah
1Kings10:2 And she came to Jerusalem with a very great train
1Kings10:3 And Solomon told her all her questions: there was not anything hid from the king which he told her not.
1Kings10:4 And when the queen of Sheba had seen all the wisdom of Solomon
1Kings10:5 and the food of his table
1Kings10:6 And she said to the king
1Kings10:7 Howbeit I believed not the words
1Kings10:8 Happy are thy men
1Kings10:9 Blessed be Jehovah thy God
1Kings10:10 And she gave the king a hundred and twenty talents of gold
1Kings10:11 And the navy also of Hiram
1Kings10:12 And the king made of the almug-trees pillars for the house of Jehovah
1Kings10:13 And king Solomon gave to the queen of Sheba all her desire
1Kings10:14 Now the weight of gold that came to Solomon in one year was six hundred threescore and six talents of gold
1Kings10:15 besides that which the traders brought
1Kings10:16 And king Solomon made two hundred bucklers of beaten gold; six hundred shekels of gold went to one buckler.
1Kings10:17 And he made three hundred shields of beaten gold; three pounds of gold went to one shield: and the king put them in the house of the forest of Lebanon.
1Kings10:18 Moreover the king made a great throne of ivory
1Kings10:19 There were six steps to the throne
1Kings10:20 And twelve lions stood there on the one side and on the other upon the six steps: there was not the like made in any kingdom.
1Kings10:21 And all king Solomons drinking vessels were of gold
1Kings10:22 For the king had at sea a navy of Tarshish with the navy of Hiram: once every three years came the navy of Tarshish
1Kings10:23 So king Solomon exceeded all the kings of the earth in riches and in wisdom.
1Kings10:24 And all the earth sought the presence of Solomon
1Kings10:25 And they brought every man his tribute
1Kings10:26 And Solomon gathered together chariots and horsemen: and he had a thousand and four hundred chariots
1Kings10:27 And the king made silver to be in Jerusalem as stones
1Kings10:28 And the horses which Solomon had were brought out of Egypt; and the kings merchants received them in droves
1Kings10:29 And a chariot came up and went out of Egypt for six hundred shekels of silver
1Kings11:1 Now king Solomon loved many foreign women
1Kings11:2 of the nations concerning which Jehovah said unto the children of Israel
1Kings11:3 And he had seven hundred wives
1Kings11:4 For it came to pass
1Kings11:5 For Solomon went after Ashtoreth the goddess of the Sidonians
1Kings11:6 And Solomon did that which was evil in the sight of Jehovah
1Kings11:7 Then did Solomon build a high place for Chemosh the abomination of Moab
1Kings11:8 And so did he for all his foreign wives
1Kings11:9 And Jehovah was angry with Solomon
1Kings11:10 and had commanded him concerning this thing
1Kings11:11 Wherefore Jehovah said unto Solomon
1Kings11:12 Notwithstanding in thy days I will not do it
1Kings11:13 Howbeit I will not rend away all the kingdom; but I will give one tribe to thy son
1Kings11:14 And Jehovah raised up an adversary unto Solomon
1Kings11:15 For it came to pass
1Kings11:16 (for Joab and all Israel remained there six months
1Kings11:17 that Hadad fled
1Kings11:18 And they arose out of Midian
1Kings11:19 And Hadad found great favor in the sight of Pharaoh
1Kings11:20 And the sister of Tahpenes bare him Genubath his son
1Kings11:21 And when Hadad heard in Egypt that David slept with his fathers
1Kings11:22 Then Pharaoh said unto him
1Kings11:23 And God raised up another adversary unto him
1Kings11:24 And he gathered men unto him
1Kings11:25 And he was an adversary to Israel all the days of Solomon
1Kings11:26 And Jeroboam the son of Nebat
1Kings11:27 And this was the reason why he lifted up his hand against the king: Solomon built Millo
1Kings11:28 And the man Jeroboam was a mighty man of valor; and Solomon saw the young man that he was industrious
1Kings11:29 And it came to pass at that time
1Kings11:30 And Ahijah laid hold of the new garment that was on him
1Kings11:31 And he said to Jeroboam
1Kings11:32 (but he shall have one tribe
1Kings11:33 because that they have forsaken me
1Kings11:34 Howbeit I will not take the whole kingdom out of his hand; but I will make him prince all the days of his life

2Kings15:31 Now the rest of the acts of Pekah
2Kings15:32 In the second year of Pekah the son of Remaliah king of Israel began Jotham the son of Uzziah king of Judah to reign.
2Kings15:33 Five and twenty years old was he when he began to reign; and he reigned sixteen years in Jerusalem: and his mothers name was Jerusha the daughter of Zadok.
2Kings15:34 And he did that which was right in the eyes of Jehovah; he did according to all that his father Uzziah had done.
2Kings15:35 Howbeit the high places were not taken away: the people still sacrificed and burned incense in the high places. He built the upper gate of the house of Jehovah.
2Kings15:36 Now the rest of the acts of Jotham
2Kings15:37 In those days Jehovah began to send against Judah Rezin the king of Syria
2Kings15:38 And Jotham slept with his fathers
2Kings16:1 In the seventeenth year of Pekah the son of Remaliah Ahaz the son of Jotham king of Judah began to reign.
2Kings16:2 Twenty years old was Ahaz when he began to reign; and he reigned sixteen years in Jerusalem: and he did not that which was right in the eyes of Jehovah his God
2Kings16:3 But he walked in the way of the kings of Israel
2Kings16:4 And he sacrificed and burnt incense in the high places
2Kings16:5 Then Rezin king of Syria and Pekah son of Remaliah king of Israel came up to Jerusalem to war: and they besieged Ahaz
2Kings16:6 At that time Rezin king of Syria recovered Elath to Syria
2Kings16:7 So Ahaz sent messengers to Tiglath-pileser king of Assyria
2Kings16:8 And Ahaz took the silver and gold that was found in the house of Jehovah
2Kings16:9 And the king of Assyria hearkened unto him; and the king of Assyria went up against Damascus
2Kings16:10 And king Ahaz went to Damascus to meet Tiglath-pileser king of Assyria
2Kings16:11 And Urijah the priest built an altar: according to all that king Ahaz had sent from Damascus
2Kings16:12 And when the king was come from Damascus
2Kings16:13 And he burnt his burnt-offering and his meal-offering
2Kings16:14 And the brazen altar
2Kings16:15 And king Ahaz commanded Urijah the priest
2Kings16:16 Thus did Urijah the priest
2Kings16:17 And king Ahaz cut off the panels of the bases
2Kings16:18 And the covered way for the sabbath that they had built in the house
2Kings16:19 Now the rest of the acts of Ahaz which he did
2Kings16:20 And Ahaz slept with his fathers
2Kings17:1 In the twelfth year of Ahaz king of Judah began Hoshea the son of Elah to reign in Samaria over Israel
2Kings17:2 And he did that which was evil in the sight of Jehovah
2Kings17:3 Against him came up Shalmaneser king of Assyria; and Hoshea became his servant
2Kings17:4 And the king of Assyria found conspiracy in Hoshea; for he had sent messengers to So king of Egypt
2Kings17:5 Then the king of Assyria came up throughout all the land
2Kings17:6 In the ninth year of Hoshea the king of Assyria took Samaria
2Kings17:7 And it was so
2Kings17:8 and walked in the statutes of the nations
2Kings17:9 And the children of Israel did secretly things that were not right against Jehovah their God: and they built them high places in all their cities
2Kings17:10 and they set them up pillars and Asherim upon every high hill
2Kings17:11 and there they burnt incense in all the high places
2Kings17:12 and they served idols
2Kings17:13 Yet Jehovah testified unto Israel
2Kings17:14 Notwithstanding
2Kings17:15 And they rejected his statutes
2Kings17:16 And they forsook all the commandments of Jehovah their God
2Kings17:17 And they caused their sons and their daughters to pass through the fire
2Kings17:18 Therefore Jehovah was very angry with Israel
2Kings17:19 Also Judah kept not the commandments of Jehovah their God
2Kings17:20 And Jehovah rejected all the seed of Israel
2Kings17:21 For he rent Israel from the house of David; and they made Jeroboam the son of Nebat king: and Jeroboam drove Israel from following Jehovah
2Kings17:22 And the children of Israel walked in all the sins of Jeroboam which he did; they departed not from them;
2Kings17:23 until Jehovah removed Israel out of his sight
2Kings17:24 And the king of Assyria brought men from Babylon
2Kings17:25 And so it was
2Kings17:26 Wherefore they spake to the king of Assyria
2Kings17:27 Then the king of Assyria commanded
2Kings17:28 So one of the priests whom they had carried away from Samaria came and dwelt in Beth-el
2Kings17:29 Howbeit every nation made gods of their own
2Kings17:30 And the men of Babylon made Succoth-benoth
2Kings17:31 and the Avvites made Nibhaz and Tartak; and the Sepharvites burnt their children in the fire to Adrammelech and Anammelech
2Kings17:32 So they feared Jehovah
2Kings17:33 They feared Jehovah
2Kings17:34 Unto this day they do after the former manner: they fear not Jehovah
2Kings17:35 with whom Jehovah had made a covenant
2Kings17:36 but Jehovah
2Kings17:37 and the statutes and the ordinances
2Kings17:38 and the covenant that I have made with you ye shall not forget; neither shall ye fear other gods:
2Kings17:39 but Jehovah your God shall ye fear; and he will deliver you out of the hand of all your enemies.
2Kings17:40 Howbeit they did not hearken
2Kings17:41 So these nations feared Jehovah
2Kings18:1 Now it came to pass in the third year of Hoshea son of Elah king of Israel
2Kings18:2 Twenty and five years old was he when he began to reign; and he reigned twenty and nine years in Jerusalem: and his mothers name was Abi the daughter of Zechariah.
2Kings18:3 And he did that which was right in the eyes of Jehovah
2Kings18:4 He removed the high places
2Kings18:5 He trusted in Jehovah
2Kings18:6 For he clave to Jehovah; he departed not from following him
2Kings18:7 And Jehovah was with him; whithersoever he went forth he prospered: and he rebelled against the king of Assyria
2Kings18:8 He smote the Philistines unto Gaza and the borders thereof
2Kings18:9 And it came to pass in the fourth year of king Hezekiah
2Kings18:10 And at the end of three years they took it: in the sixth year of Hezekiah
2Kings18:11 And the king of Assyria carried Israel away unto Assyria
2Kings18:12 because they obeyed not the voice of Jehovah their God
2Kings18:13 Now in the fourteenth year of king Hezekiah did Sennacherib king of Assyria come up against all the fortified cities of Judah
2Kings18:14 And Hezekiah king of Judah sent to the king of Assyria to Lachish
2Kings18:15 And Hezekiah gave him all the silver that was found in the house of Jehovah

2Kings18:18 And when they had called to the king

2Kings18:21 Now

2Kings18:23 Now therefore

2Kings18:27 But Rabshakeh said unto them
2Kings18:28 Then Rabshakeh stood

2Kings18:30 neither let Hezekiah make you trust in Jehovah
2Kings18:31 Hearken not to Hezekiah: for thus saith the king of Assyria
2Kings18:32 Until I come and take you away to a land like your own land

2Kings19:16 Incline thine ear
2Kings19:17 Of a truth

2Kings19:21 This is the word that Jehovah hath spoken concerning him: The virgin daughter of Zion hath despised thee and laughed thee to scorn; the daughter of Jerusalem hath shaken her head at thee.
2Kings19:22 Whom hast thou defied and blasphemed? and against whom hast thou exalted thy voice and lifted up thine eyes on high? even against the Holy One of Israel.
2Kings19:23 By thy messengers thou hast defied the Lord
2Kings19:24 I have digged and drunk strange waters
2Kings19:25 Hast thou not heard how I have done it long ago
2Kings19:26 Therefore their inhabitants were of small power
2Kings19:27 But I know thy sitting down
2Kings19:28 Because of thy raging against me
2Kings19:29 And this shall be the sign unto thee: Ye shall eat this year that which groweth of itself
2Kings19:30 And the remnant that is escaped of the house of Judah shall again take root downward
2Kings19:31 For out of Jerusalem shall go forth a remnant

2Kings19:34 For I will defend this city to save it

2Kings21:1 Manasseh was twelve years old when he began to reign; and he reigned five and fifty years in Jerusalem: and his mothers name was Hephzibah.
2Kings21:2 And he did that which was evil in the sight of Jehovah
2Kings21:3 For he built again the high places which Hezekiah his father had destroyed; and he reared up altars for Baal
2Kings21:4 And he built altars in the house of Jehovah
2Kings21:5 And he built altars for all the host of heaven in the two courts of the house of Jehovah.
2Kings21:6 And he made his son to pass through the fire
2Kings21:7 And he set the graven image of Asherah
2Kings21:8 neither will I cause the feet of Israel to wander any more out of the land which I gave their fathers
2Kings21:9 But they hearkened not: and Manasseh seduced them to do that which is evil more than did the nations whom Jehovah destroyed before the children of Israel.
2Kings21:10 And Jehovah spake by his servants the prophets
2Kings21:11 Because Manasseh king of Judah hath done these abominations
2Kings21:12 therefore thus saith Jehovah
2Kings21:13 And I will stretch over Jerusalem the line of Samaria
2Kings21:14 And I will cast off the remnant of mine inheritance
2Kings21:15 because they have done that which is evil in my sight
2Kings21:16 Moreover Manasseh shed innocent blood very much
2Kings21:17 Now the rest of the acts of Manasseh
2Kings21:18 And Manasseh slept with his fathers
2Kings21:19 Amon was twenty and two years old when he began to reign; and he reigned two years in Jerusalem: and his mothers name was Meshullemeth the daughter of Haruz of Jotbah.
2Kings21:20 And he did that which was evil in the sight of Jehovah
2Kings21:21 And he walked in all the way that his father walked in
2Kings21:22 and he forsook Jehovah
2Kings21:23 And the servants of Amon conspired against him
2Kings21:24 But the people of the land slew all them that had conspired against king Amon; and the people of the land made Josiah his son king in his stead.
2Kings21:25 Now the rest of the acts of Amon which he did
2Kings21:26 And he was buried in his sepulchre in the garden of Uzza: and Josiah his son reigned in his stead.

2Kings23:30 And his servants carried him in a chariot dead from Megiddo
2Kings23:31 Jehoahaz was twenty and three years old when he began to reign; and he reigned three months in Jerusalem: and his mothers name was Hamutal the daughter of Jeremiah of Libnah.

2Kings23:36 Jehoiakim was twenty and five years old when he began to reign; and he reigned eleven years in Jerusalem: and his mothers name was Zebidah the daughter of Pedaiah of Rumah.

2Chronicles32:5 And he took courage
2Chronicles32:6 And he set captains of war over the people
2Chronicles32:7 Be strong and of good courage
2Chronicles32:8 with him is an arm of flesh; but with us is Jehovah our God to help us

2Chronicles32:11 Doth not Hezekiah persuade you

2Chronicles32:23 And many brought gifts unto Jehovah to Jerusalem

2Chronicles32:25 But Hezekiah rendered not again according to the benefit done unto him; for his heart was lifted up: therefore there was wrath upon him
2Chronicles32:26 Notwithstanding Hezekiah humbled himself for the pride of his heart
2Chronicles32:27 And Hezekiah had exceeding much riches and honor: and he provided him treasuries for silver
2Chronicles32:28 store-houses also for the increase of grain and new wine and oil; and stalls for all manner of beasts
2Chronicles32:29 Moreover he provided him cities
2Chronicles32:30 This same Hezekiah also stopped the upper spring of the waters of Gihon
2Chronicles32:31 Howbeit in the business of the ambassadors of the princes of Babylon
2Chronicles32:32 Now the rest of the acts of Hezekiah

2Chronicles33:1 Manasseh was twelve years old when he began to reign; and he reigned fifty and five years in Jerusalem.
2Chronicles33:2 And he did that which was evil in the sight of Jehovah
2Chronicles33:3 For he built again the high places which Hezekiah his father had broken down; and he reared up altars for the Baalim
2Chronicles33:4 And he built altars in the house of Jehovah
2Chronicles33:5 And he built altars for all the host of heaven in the two courts of the house of Jehovah.

2Chronicles33:7 And he set the graven image of the idol

2Chronicles33:12 And when he was in distress

2Chronicles33:24 And his servants conspired against him
2Chronicles33:25 But the people of the land slew all them that had conspired against king Amon; and the people of the land made Josiah his son king in his stead.

Esther1:1 Now it came to pass in the days of Ahasuerus (this is Ahasuerus who reigned from India even unto Ethiopia
Esther1:2 that in those days
Esther1:3 in the third year of his reign
Esther1:4 when he showed the riches of his glorious kingdom and the honor of his excellent majesty many days
Esther1:5 And when these days were fulfilled
Esther1:6 There were hangings of white cloth
Esther1:7 And they gave them drink in vessels of gold (the vessels being diverse one from another)
Esther1:8 And the drinking was according to the law; none could compel: for so the king had appointed to all the officers of his house
Esther1:9 Also Vashti the queen made a feast for the women in the royal house which belonged to king Ahasuerus.
Esther1:10 On the seventh day
Esther1:11 to bring Vashti the queen before the king with the crown royal
Esther1:12 But the queen Vashti refused to come at the kings commandment by the chamberlains: therefore was the king very wroth
Esther1:13 Then the king said to the wise men
Esther1:14 and the next unto him were Carshena
Esther1:15 What shall we do unto the queen Vashti according to law
Esther1:16 And Memucan answered before the king and the princes
Esther1:17 For this deed of the queen will come abroad unto all women
Esther1:18 And this day will the princesses of Persia and Media who have heard of the deed of the queen say the like unto all the kings princes. So will there arise much contempt and wrath.
Esther1:19 If it please the king
Esther1:20 And when the kings decree which he shall make shall be published throughout all his kingdom (for it is great)
Esther1:21 And the saying pleased the king and the princes; and the king did according to the word of Memucan:
Esther1:22 for he sent letters into all the kings provinces

Esther4:6 So Hathach went forth to Mordecai unto the broad place of the city
Esther4:7 And Mordecai told him of all that had happened unto him
Esther4:8 Also he gave him the copy of the writing of the decree that was given out in Shushan to destroy them
Esther4:9 And Hathach came and told Esther the words of Mordecai.
Esther4:10 Then Esther spake unto Hathach
Esther4:11 All the kings servants

Esther4:14 For if thou altogether holdest thy peace at this time
Esther4:15 Then Esther bade them return answer unto Mordecai
Esther4:16 Go
Esther4:17 So Mordecai went his way
Esther5:1 Now it came to pass on the third day
Esther5:2 And it was so

Esther6:12 And Mordecai came again to the kings gate. But Haman hasted to his house
Esther6:13 And Haman recounted unto Zeresh his wife and all his friends everything that had befallen him. Then said his wise men and Zeresh his wife unto him
Esther6:14 While they were yet talking with him
Esther7:1 So the king and Haman came to banquet with Esther the queen.
Esther7:2 And the king said again unto Esther on the second day at the banquet of wine
Esther7:3 Then Esther the queen answered and said
Esther7:4 for we are sold
Esther7:5 Then spake the king Ahasuerus and said unto Esther the queen
Esther7:6 And Esther said
Esther7:7 And the king arose in his wrath from the banquet of wine and went into the palace garden: and Haman stood up to make request for his life to Esther the queen; for he saw that there was evil determined against him by the king.
Esther7:8 Then the king returned out of the palace garden into the place of the banquet of wine; and Haman was fallen upon the couch whereon Esther was. Then said the king
Esther7:9 Then said Harbonah
Esther7:10 So they hanged Haman on the gallows that he had prepared for Mordecai. Then was the kings wrath pacified.
Esther8:1 On that day did the king Ahasuerus give the house of Haman the Jews enemy unto Esther the queen. And Mordecai came before the king; for Esther had told what he was unto her.
Esther8:2 And the king took off his ring
Esther8:3 And Esther spake yet again before the king
Esther8:4 Then the king held out to Esther the golden sceptre. So Esther arose
Esther8:5 And she said
Esther8:6 for how can I endure to see the evil that shall come unto my people? or how can I endure to see the destruction of my kindred?
Esther8:7 Then the king Ahasuerus said unto Esther the queen and to Mordecai the Jew
Esther8:8 Write ye also to the Jews
Esther8:9 Then were the kings scribes called at that time
Esther8:10 And he wrote the name of king Ahasuerus
Esther8:11 wherein the king granted the Jews that were in every city to gather themselves together
Esther8:12 upon one day in all the provinces of king Ahasuerus
Esther8:13 A copy of the writing
Esther8:14 So the posts that rode upon swift steeds that were used in the kings service went out
Esther8:15 And Mordecai went forth from the presence of the king in royal apparel of blue and white
Esther8:16 The Jews had light and gladness
Esther8:17 And in every province
Esther9:1 Now in the twelfth month
Esther9:2 the Jews gathered themselves together in their cities throughout all the provinces of the king Ahasuerus
Esther9:3 And all the princes of the provinces
Esther9:4 For Mordecai was great in the kings house
Esther9:5 And the Jews smote all their enemies with the stroke of the sword
Esther9:6 And in Shushan the palace the Jews slew and destroyed five hundred men.

Esther9:8 and Poratha
Esther9:9 and Parmashta
Esther9:10 the ten sons of Haman the son of Hammedatha

Esther9:12 And the king said unto Esther the queen
Esther9:13 Then said Esther
Esther9:14 And the king commanded it so to be done: and a decree was given out in Shushan; and they hanged Hamans ten sons.

Esther9:16 And the other Jews that were in the kings provinces gathered themselves together
Esther9:17 This was done on the thirteenth day of the month Adar; and on the fourteenth day of the same they rested
Esther9:18 But the Jews that were in Shushan assembled together on the thirteenth day thereof
Esther9:19 Therefore do the Jews of the villages
Esther9:20 And Mordecai wrote these things
Esther9:21 to enjoin them that they should keep the fourteenth day of the month Adar
Esther9:22 as the days wherein the Jews had rest from their enemies
Esther9:23 And the Jews undertook to do as they had begun
Esther9:24 because Haman the son of Hammedatha
Esther9:25 but when the matter came before the king
Esther9:26 Wherefore they called these days Purim
Esther9:27 the Jews ordained
Esther9:28 and that these days should be remembered and kept throughout every generation

Esther9:30 And he sent letters unto all the Jews
Esther9:31 to confirm these days of Purim in their appointed times
Esther9:32 And the commandment of Esther confirmed these matters of Purim; and it was written in the book.

Job1:1 There was a man in the land of Uz
Job1:2 And there were born unto him seven sons and three daughters.
Job1:3 His substance also was seven thousand sheep
Job1:4 And his sons went and held a feast in the house of each one upon his day; and they sent and called for their three sisters to eat and to drink with them.
Job1:5 And it was so
Job1:6 Now it came to pass on the day when the sons of God came to present themselves before Jehovah
Job1:7 And Jehovah said unto Satan
Job1:8 And Jehovah said unto Satan
Job1:9 Then Satan answered Jehovah
Job1:10 Hast not thou made a hedge about him
Job1:11 But put forth thy hand now
Job1:12 And Jehovah said unto Satan
Job1:13 And it fell on a day when his sons and his daughters were eating and drinking wine in their eldest brothers house
Job1:14 that there came a messenger unto Job
Job1:15 and the Sabeans fell upon them
Job1:16 While he was yet speaking
Job1:17 While he was yet speaking
Job1:18 While he was yet speaking
Job1:19 and

Job2:12 And when they lifted up their eyes afar off

Job3:1 After this opened Job his mouth
Job3:2 And Job answered and said:
Job3:3 Let the day perish wherein I was born
Job3:4 Let that day be darkness; Let not God from above seek for it
Job3:5 Let darkness and the shadow of death claim it for their own; Let a cloud dwell upon it; Let all that maketh black the day terrify it.
Job3:6 As for that night
Job3:7 Lo
Job3:8 Let them curse it that curse the day
Job3:9 Let the stars of the twilight thereof be dark: Let it look for light
Job3:10 Because it shut not up the doors of my mothers womb
Job3:11 Why died I not from the womb? Why did I not give up the ghost when my mother bare me?
Job3:12 Why did the knees receive me? Or why the breast
Job3:13 For now should I have lain down and been quiet; I should have slept; then had I been at rest
Job3:14 With kings and counsellors of the earth
Job3:15 Or with princes that had gold
Job3:16 Or as a hidden untimely birth I had not been
Job3:17 There the wicked cease from troubling; And there the weary are at rest.
Job3:18 There the prisoners are at ease together; They hear not the voice of the taskmaster.
Job3:19 The small and the great are there: And the servant is free from his master.
Job3:20 Wherefore is light given to him that is in misery
Job3:21 Who long for death
Job3:22 Who rejoice exceedingly
Job3:23 Why is light given to a man whose way is hid
Job3:24 For my sighing cometh before I eat
Job3:25 For the thing which I fear cometh upon me
Job3:26 I am not at ease
Job4:1 Then answered Eliphaz the Temanite
Job4:2 If one assay to commune with thee
Job4:3 Behold
Job4:4 Thy words have upholden him that was falling
Job4:5 But now it is come unto thee
Job4:6 Is not thy fear of God thy confidence
Job4:7 Remember
Job4:8 According as I have seen
Job4:9 By the breath of God they perish
Job4:10 The roaring of the lion
Job4:11 The old lion perisheth for lack of prey
Job4:12 Now a thing was secretly brought to me
Job4:13 In thoughts from the visions of the night
Job4:14 Fear came upon me
Job4:15 Then a spirit passed before my face; The hair of my flesh stood up.
Job4:16 It stood still
Job4:17 Shall mortal man be more just than God? Shall a man be more pure than his Maker?
Job4:18 Behold
Job4:19 How much more them that dwell in houses of clay
Job4:20 Betwixt morning and evening they are destroyed: They perish for ever without any regarding it.
Job4:21 Is not their tent-cord plucked up within them? They die
Job5:1 Call now; is there any that will answer thee? And to which of the holy ones wilt thou turn?
Job5:2 For vexation killeth the foolish man
Job5:3 I have seen the foolish taking root: But suddenly I cursed his habitation.
Job5:4 His children are far from safety
Job5:5 Whose harvest the hungry eateth up
Job5:6 For affliction cometh not forth from the dust
Job5:7 But man is born unto trouble
Job5:8 But as for me
Job5:9 Who doeth great things and unsearchable
Job5:10 Who giveth rain upon the earth
Job5:11 So that he setteth up on high those that are low
Job5:12 He frustrateth the devices of the crafty
Job5:13 He taketh the wise in their own craftiness; And the counsel of the cunning is carried headlong.
Job5:14 They meet with darkness in the day-time
Job5:15 But he saveth from the sword of their mouth
Job5:16 So the poor hath hope
Job5:17 Behold
Job5:18 For he maketh sore
Job5:19 He will deliver thee in six troubles; Yea
Job5:20 In famine he will redeem thee from death; And in war from the power of the sword.
Job5:21 Thou shalt be hid from the scourge of the tongue; Neither shalt thou be afraid of destruction when it cometh.
Job5:22 At destruction and dearth thou shalt laugh; Neither shalt thou be afraid of the beasts of the earth.
Job5:23 For thou shalt be in league with the stones of the field; And the beasts of the field shall be at peace with thee.
Job5:24 And thou shalt know that thy tent is in peace; And thou shalt visit thy fold
Job5:25 Thou shalt know also that thy seed shall be great
Job5:26 Thou shalt come to thy grave in a full age
Job5:27 Lo this
Job6:1 Then Job answered and said
Job6:2 Oh that my vexation were but weighed
Job6:3 For now it would be heavier than the sand of the seas: Therefore have my words been rash.
Job6:4 For the arrows of the Almighty are within me
Job6:5 Doth the wild ass bray when he hath grass? Or loweth the ox over his fodder?
Job6:6 Can that which hath no savor be eaten without salt? Or is there any taste in the white of an egg?
Job6:7 My soul refuseth to touch them; They are as loathsome food to me.
Job6:8 Oh that I might have my request; And that God would grant me the thing that I long for!
Job6:9 Even that it would please God to crush me; That he would let loose his hand
Job6:10 And be it still my consolation
Job6:11 What is my strength
Job6:12 Is my strength the strength of stones? Or is my flesh of brass?
Job6:13 Is it not that I have no help in me
Job6:14 To him that is ready to faint kindness should be showed from his friend; Even to him that forsaketh the fear of the Almighty.
Job6:15 My brethren have dealt deceitfully as a brook
Job6:16 Which are black by reason of the ice
Job6:17 What time they wax warm
Job6:18 The caravans that travel by the way of them turn aside; They go up into the waste
Job6:19 The caravans of Tema looked
Job6:20 They were put to shame because they had hoped; They came thither
Job6:21 For now ye are nothing; Ye see a terror
Job6:22 Did I say
Job6:23 Or
Job6:24 Teach me
Job6:25 How forcible are words of uprightness! But your reproof
Job6:26 Do ye think to reprove words
Job6:27 Yea
Job6:28 Now therefore be pleased to look upon me; For surely I shall not lie to your face.
Job6:29 Return
Job6:30 Is there injustice on my tongue? Cannot my taste discern mischievous things?
Job7:1 Is there not a warfare to man upon earth? And are not his days like the days of a hireling?
Job7:2 As a servant that earnestly desireth the shadow
Job7:3 So am I made to possess months of misery
Job7:4 When I lie down
Job7:5 My flesh is clothed with worms and clods of dust; My skin closeth up
Job7:6 My days are swifter than a weavers shuttle
Job7:7 Oh remember that my life is a breath: Mine eye shall no more see good.
Job7:8 The eye of him that seeth me shall behold me no more; Thine eyes shall be upon me
Job7:9 As the cloud is consumed and vanisheth away
Job7:10 He shall return no more to his house
Job7:11 Therefore I will not refrain my mouth; I will speak in the anguish of my spirit; I will complain in the bitterness of my soul.
Job7:12 Am I a sea
Job7:13 When I say
Job7:14 Then thou scarest me with dreams
Job7:15 So that my soul chooseth strangling
Job7:16 I loathe my life; I would not live alway: Let me alone; for my days are vanity.

Job8:5 If thou wouldest seek diligently unto God
Job8:6 If thou wert pure and upright: Surely now he would awake for thee
Job8:7 And though thy beginning was small
Job8:8 For inquire
Job8:9 (For we are but of yesterday
Job8:10 Shall not they teach thee

Job8:12 Whilst it is yet in its greenness

Job8:14 Whose confidence shall break in sunder

Job9:1 Then Job answered and said
Job9:2 Of a truth I know that it is so: But how can man be just with God?
Job9:3 If he be pleased to contend with him
Job9:4 He is wise in heart
Job9:5 Him that removeth the mountains
Job9:6 That shaketh the earth out of its place
Job9:7 That commandeth the sun
Job9:8 That alone stretcheth out the heavens
Job9:9 That maketh the Bear
Job9:10 That doeth great things past finding out
Job9:11 Lo
Job9:12 Behold
Job9:13 God will not withdraw his anger; The helpers of Rahab do stoop under him.
Job9:14 How much less shall I answer him
Job9:15 Whom
Job9:16 If I had called
Job9:17 For he breaketh me with a tempest
Job9:18 He will not suffer me to take my breath
Job9:19 If we speak of strength
Job9:20 Though I be righteous
Job9:21 I am perfect; I regard not myself; I despise my life.
Job9:22 It is all one; therefore I say
Job9:23 If the scourge slay suddenly
Job9:24 The earth is given into the hand of the wicked; He covereth the faces of the judges thereof: If it be not he
Job9:25 Now my days are swifter than a post: They flee away
Job9:26 They are passed away as the swift ships; As the eagle that swoopeth on the prey.
Job9:27 If I say
Job9:28 I am afraid of all my sorrows
Job9:29 I shall be condemned; Why then do I labor in vain?
Job9:30 If I wash myself with snow water
Job9:31 Yet wilt thou plunge me in the ditch
Job9:32 For he is not a man
Job9:33 There is no umpire betwixt us
Job9:34 Let him take his rod away from me
Job9:35 Then would I speak
Job10:1 My soul is weary of my life; I will give free course to my complaint; I will speak in the bitterness of my soul.
Job10:2 I will say unto God
Job10:3 Is it good unto thee that thou shouldest oppress
Job10:4 Hast thou eyes of flesh? Or seest thou as man seeth?
Job10:5 Are thy days as the days of man
Job10:6 That thou inquirest after mine iniquity
Job10:7 Although thou knowest that I am not wicked
Job10:8 Thy hands have framed me and fashioned me Together round about; yet thou dost destroy me.
Job10:9 Remember
Job10:10 Hast thou not poured me out as milk
Job10:11 Thou hast clothed me with skin and flesh
Job10:12 Thou hast granted me life and lovingkindness; And thy visitation hath preserved my spirit.
Job10:13 Yet these things thou didst hide in thy heart; I know that this is with thee:
Job10:14 If I sin
Job10:15 If I be wicked
Job10:16 And if my head exalt itself
Job10:17 Thou renewest thy witnesses against me
Job10:18 Wherefore then hast thou brought me forth out of the womb? I had given up the ghost
Job10:19 I should have been as though I had not been; I should have been carried from the womb to the grave.
Job10:20 Are not my days few? cease then
Job10:21 Before I go whence I shall not return
Job10:22 The land dark as midnight
Job11:1 Then answered Zophar the Naamathite
Job11:2 Should not the multitude of words be answered? And should a man full of talk be justified?
Job11:3 Should thy boastings make men hold their peace? And when thou mockest
Job11:4 For thou sayest
Job11:5 But oh that God would speak
Job11:6 And that he would show thee the secrets of wisdom! For he is manifold in understanding. Know therefore that God exacteth of thee less than thine iniquity deserveth.
Job11:7 Canst thou by searching find out God? Canst thou find out the Almighty unto perfection?
Job11:8 It is high as heaven; what canst thou do? Deeper than Sheol; what canst thou know?
Job11:9 The measure thereof is longer than the earth
Job11:10 If he pass through
Job11:11 For he knoweth false men: He seeth iniquity also
Job11:12 But vain man is void of understanding
Job11:13 If thou set thy heart aright
Job11:14 If iniquity be in thy hand
Job11:15 Surely then shalt thou lift up thy face without spot; Yea
Job11:16 For thou shalt forget thy misery; Thou shalt remember it as waters that are passed away
Job11:17 And thy life shall be clearer than the noonday; Though there be darkness
Job11:18 And thou shalt be secure
Job11:19 Also thou shalt lie down
Job11:20 But the eyes of the wicked shall fail
Job12:1 Then Job answered and said
Job12:2 No doubt but ye are the people
Job12:3 But I have understanding as well as you; I am not inferior to you: Yea
Job12:4 I am as one that is a laughing-stock to his neighbor
Job12:5 In the thought of him that is at ease there is contempt for misfortune; It is ready for them whose foot slippeth.
Job12:6 The tents of robbers prosper
Job12:7 But ask now the beasts
Job12:8 Or speak to the earth
Job12:9 Who knoweth not in all these
Job12:10 In whose hand is the soul of every living thing
Job12:11 Doth not the ear try words
Job12:12 With aged men is wisdom
Job12:13 With God is wisdom and might; He hath counsel and understanding.

Job12:15 Behold

Job12:25 They grope in the dark without light; And he maketh them to stagger like a drunken man.
Job13:1 Lo
Job13:2 What ye know
Job13:3 Surely I would speak to the Almighty
Job13:4 But ye are forgers of lies; Ye are all physicians of no value.
Job13:5 Oh that ye would altogether hold your peace! And it would be your wisdom.
Job13:6 Hear now my reasoning
Job13:7 Will ye speak unrighteously for God
Job13:8 Will ye show partiality to him? Will ye contend for God?
Job13:9 Is it good that he should search you out? Or as one deceiveth a man
Job13:10 He will surely reprove you If ye do secretly show partiality.
Job13:11 Shall not his majesty make you afraid
Job13:12 Your memorable sayings are proverbs of ashes
Job13:13 Hold your peace
Job13:14 Wherefore should I take my flesh in my teeth
Job13:15 Behold
Job13:16 This also shall be my salvation
Job13:17 Hear diligently my speech
Job13:18 Behold now

Job13:21 Withdraw thy hand far from me; And let not thy terror make me afraid.
Job13:22 Then call thou
Job13:23 How many are mine iniquities and sins? Make me to know my transgression and my sin.
