Job14:1 Man
Job14:2 He cometh forth like a flower
Job14:3 And dost thou open thine eyes upon such a one
Job14:4 Who can bring a clean thing out of an unclean? not one.
Job14:5 Seeing his days are determined
Job14:6 Look away from him
Job14:7 For there is hope of a tree
Job14:8 Though the root thereof wax old in the earth
Job14:9 Yet through the scent of water it will bud
Job14:10 But man dieth
Job14:11 As the waters fail from the sea
Job14:12 So man lieth down and riseth not: Till the heavens be no more
Job14:13 Oh that thou wouldest hide me in Sheol
Job14:14 If a man die
Job14:15 Thou wouldest call
Job14:16 But now thou numberest my steps: Dost thou not watch over my sin?
Job14:17 My transgression is sealed up in a bag
Job14:18 But the mountain falling cometh to nought; And the rock is removed out of its place;
Job14:19 The waters wear the stones; The overflowings thereof wash away the dust of the earth: So thou destroyest the hope of man.
Job14:20 Thou prevailest for ever against him
Job14:21 His sons come to honor
Job14:22 But his flesh upon him hath pain
Job15:1 Then answered Eliphaz the Temanite
Job15:2 Should a wise man make answer with vain knowledge
Job15:3 Should he reason with unprofitable talk
Job15:4 Yea
Job15:5 For thine iniquity teacheth thy mouth
Job15:6 Thine own mouth condemneth thee
Job15:7 Art thou the first man that was born? Or wast thou brought forth before the hills?
Job15:8 Hast thou heard the secret counsel of God? And dost thou limit wisdom to thyself?
Job15:9 What knowest thou
Job15:10 With us are both the gray-headed and the very aged men
Job15:11 Are the consolations of God too small for thee
Job15:12 Why doth thy heart carry thee away? And why do thine eyes flash
Job15:13 That against God thou turnest thy spirit
Job15:14 What is man
Job15:15 Behold
Job15:16 How much less one that is abominable and corrupt
Job15:17 I will show thee
Job15:18 (Which wise men have told From their fathers
Job15:19 Unto whom alone the land was given
Job15:20 The wicked man travaileth with pain all his days
Job15:21 A sound of terrors is in his ears; In prosperity the destroyer shall come upon him.
Job15:22 He believeth not that he shall return out of darkness
Job15:23 He wandereth abroad for bread
Job15:24 Distress and anguish make him afraid; They prevail against him
Job15:25 Because he hath stretched out his hand against God
Job15:26 He runneth upon him with a stiff neck
Job15:27 Because he hath covered his face with his fatness
Job15:28 And he hath dwelt in desolate cities
Job15:29 He shall not be rich
Job15:30 He shall not depart out of darkness; The flame shall dry up his branches
Job15:31 Let him not trust in vanity
Job15:32 It shall be accomplished before his time
Job15:33 He shall shake off his unripe grape as the vine
Job15:34 For the company of the godless shall be barren
Job15:35 They conceive mischief
Job16:1 Then Job answered and said
Job16:2 I have heard many such things: Miserable comforters are ye all.
Job16:3 Shall vain words have an end? Or what provoketh thee that thou answerest?
Job16:4 I also could speak as ye do; If your soul were in my souls stead
Job16:5 But I would strengthen you with my mouth
Job16:6 Though I speak
Job16:7 But now he hath made me weary: Thou hast made desolate all my company.
Job16:8 And thou hast laid fast hold on me
Job16:9 He hath torn me in his wrath
Job16:10 They have gaped upon me with their mouth; They have smitten me upon the cheek reproachfully: They gather themselves together against me.
Job16:11 God delivereth me to the ungodly
Job16:12 I was at ease
Job16:13 His archers compass me round about; He cleaveth my reins asunder
Job16:14 He breaketh me with breach upon breach; He runneth upon me like a giant.
Job16:15 I have sewed sackcloth upon my skin
Job16:16 My face is red with weeping
Job16:17 Although there is no violence in my hands
Job16:18 O earth
Job16:19 Even now
Job16:20 My friends scoff at me: But mine eye poureth out tears unto God
Job16:21 That he would maintain the right of a man with God
Job16:22 For when a few years are come
Job17:1 My spirit is consumed
Job17:2 Surely there are mockers with me
Job17:3 Give now a pledge
Job17:4 For thou hast hid their heart from understanding: Therefore shalt thou not exalt them.
Job17:5 He that denounceth his friends for a prey
Job17:6 But he hath made me a byword of the people; And they spit in my face.
Job17:7 Mine eye also is dim by reason of sorrow
Job17:8 Upright men shall be astonished at this
Job17:9 Yet shall the righteous hold on his way
Job17:10 But as for you all
Job17:11 My days are past
Job17:12 They change the night into day: The light
Job17:13 If I look for Sheol as my house; If I have spread my couch in the darkness;
Job17:14 If I have said to corruption
Job17:15 Where then is my hope? And as for my hope
Job17:16 It shall go down to the bars of Sheol
Job18:1 Then answered Bildad the Shuhite
Job18:2 How long will ye hunt for words? Consider
Job18:3 Wherefore are we counted as beasts
Job18:4 Thou that tearest thyself in thine anger
Job18:5 Yea
Job18:6 The light shall be dark in his tent
Job18:7 The steps of his strength shall be straitened
Job18:8 For he is cast into a net by his own feet
Job18:9 A gin shall take him by the heel
Job18:10 A noose is hid for him in the ground
Job18:11 Terrors shall make him afraid on every side
Job18:12 His strength shall be hunger-bitten
Job18:13 The members of his body shall be devoured
Job18:14 He shall be rooted out of his tent where he trusteth; And he shall be brought to the king of terrors.
Job18:15 There shall dwell in his tent that which is none of his: Brimstone shall be scattered upon his habitation.
Job18:16 His roots shall be dried up beneath
Job18:17 His remembrance shall perish from the earth
Job18:18 He shall be driven from light into darkness
Job18:19 He shall have neither son nor sons son among his people
Job18:20 They that come after shall be astonished at his day
Job18:21 Surely such are the dwellings of the unrighteous
Job19:1 Then Job answered and said
Job19:2 How long will ye vex my soul
Job19:3 These ten times have ye reproached me: Ye are not ashamed that ye deal hardly with me.
Job19:4 And be it indeed that I have erred
Job19:5 If indeed ye will magnify yourselves against me
Job19:6 Know now that God hath subverted me in my cause
Job19:7 Behold
Job19:8 He hath walled up my way that I cannot pass
Job19:9 He hath stripped me of my glory
Job19:10 He hath broken me down on every side
Job19:11 He hath also kindled his wrath against me
Job19:12 His troops come on together
Job19:13 He hath put my brethren far from me
Job19:14 My kinsfolk have failed
Job19:15 They that dwell in my house
Job19:16 I call unto my servant
Job19:17 My breath is strange to my wife
Job19:18 Even young children despise me; If I arise
Job19:19 All my familiar friends abhor me
Job19:20 My bone cleaveth to my skin and to my flesh
Job19:21 Have pity upon me
Job19:22 Why do ye persecute me as God
Job19:23 Oh that my words were now written! Oh that they were inscribed in a book!
Job19:24 That with an iron pen and lead They were graven in the rock for ever!
Job19:25 But as for me I know that my Redeemer liveth
Job19:26 And after my skin
Job19:27 Whom I
Job19:28 If ye say
Job19:29 Be ye afraid of the sword: For wrath bringeth the punishments of the sword
Job20:1 Then answered Zophar the Naamathite
Job20:2 Therefore do my thoughts give answer to me
Job20:3 I have heard the reproof which putteth me to shame; And the spirit of my understanding answereth me.
Job20:4 Knowest thou not this of old time
Job20:5 That the triumphing of the wicked is short
Job20:6 Though his height mount up to the heavens
Job20:7 Yet he shall perish for ever like his own dung: They that have seen him shall say
Job20:8 He shall fly away as a dream
Job20:9 The eye which saw him shall see him no more; Neither shall his place any more behold him.
Job20:10 His children shall seek the favor of the poor
Job20:11 His bones are full of his youth
Job20:12 Though wickedness be sweet in his mouth
Job20:13 Though he spare it
Job20:14 Yet his food in his bowels is turned
Job20:15 He hath swallowed down riches
Job20:16 He shall suck the poison of asps: The vipers tongue shall slay him.
Job20:17 He shall not look upon the rivers
Job20:18 That which he labored for shall he restore
Job20:19 For he hath oppressed and forsaken the poor; He hath violently taken away a house
Job20:20 Because he knew no quietness within him
Job20:21 There was nothing left that he devoured not; Therefore his prosperity shall not endure.
Job20:22 In the fulness of his sufficiency he shall be in straits: The hand of every one that is in misery shall come upon him.
Job20:23 When he is about to fill his belly
Job20:24 He shall flee from the iron weapon
Job20:25 He draweth it forth
Job20:26 All darkness is laid up for his treasures: A fire not blown by man shall devour him; It shall consume that which is left in his tent.
Job20:27 The heavens shall reveal his iniquity
Job20:28 The increase of his house shall depart; His goods shall flow away in the day of his wrath.
Job20:29 This is the portion of a wicked man from God
Job21:1 Then Job answered and said
Job21:2 Hear diligently my speech; And let this be your consolations.
Job21:3 Suffer me
Job21:4 As for me
Job21:5 Mark me
Job21:6 Even when I remember I am troubled
Job21:7 Wherefore do the wicked live
Job21:8 Their seed is established with them in their sight
Job21:9 Their houses are safe from fear
Job21:10 Their bull gendereth
Job21:11 They send forth their little ones like a flock
Job21:12 They sing to the timbrel and harp
Job21:13 They spend their days in prosperity
Job21:14 And they say unto God
Job21:15 What is the Almighty
Job21:16 Lo
Job21:17 How oft is it that the lamp of the wicked is put out? That their calamity cometh upon them? That God distributeth sorrows in his anger?
Job21:18 That they are as stubble before the wind
Job21:19 Ye say
Job21:20 Let his own eyes see his destruction
Job21:21 For what careth he for his house after him
Job21:22 Shall any teach God knowledge
Job21:23 One dieth in his full strength
Job21:24 His pails are full of milk
Job21:25 And another dieth in bitterness of soul
Job21:26 They lie down alike in the dust
Job21:27 Behold
Job21:28 For ye say
Job21:29 Have ye not asked wayfaring men? And do ye not know their evidences
Job21:30 That the evil man is reserved to the day of calamity? That they are led forth to the day of wrath?
Job21:31 Who shall declare his way to his face? And who shall repay him what he hath done?
Job21:32 Yet shall he be borne to the grave
Job21:33 The clods of the valley shall be sweet unto him
Job21:34 How then comfort ye me in vain
Job22:1 Then answered Eliphaz the Temanite
Job22:2 Can a man be profitable unto God? Surely he that is wise is profitable unto himself.
Job22:3 Is it any pleasure to the Almighty
Job22:4 Is it for thy fear of him that he reproveth thee
Job22:5 Is not thy wickedness great? Neither is there any end to thine iniquities.
Job22:6 For thou hast taken pledges of thy brother for nought
Job22:7 Thou hast not given water to the weary to drink
Job22:8 But as for the mighty man
Job22:9 Thou hast sent widows away empty
Job22:10 Therefore snares are round about thee
Job22:11 Or darkness
Job22:12 Is not God in the height of heaven? And behold the height of the stars
Job22:13 And thou sayest
Job22:14 Thick clouds are a covering to him
Job22:15 Wilt thou keep the old way Which wicked men have trodden?
Job22:16 Who were snatched away before their time
Job22:17 Who said unto God
Job22:18 Yet he filled their houses with good things: But the counsel of the wicked is far from me.
Job22:19 The righteous see it
Job22:20 Saying
Job22:21 Acquaint now thyself with him
Job22:22 Receive
Job22:23 If thou return to the Almighty
Job22:24 And lay thou thy treasure in the dust
Job22:25 And the Almighty will be thy treasure
Job22:26 For then shalt thou delight thyself in the Almighty
Job22:27 Thou shalt make thy prayer unto him
Job22:28 Thou shalt also decree a thing
Job22:29 When they cast thee down
Job22:30 He will deliver even him that is not innocent: Yea
Job23:1 Then Job answered and said
Job23:2 Even to-day is my complaint rebellious: My stroke is heavier than my groaning.
Job23:3 Oh that I knew where I might find him! That I might come even to his seat!
Job23:4 I would set my cause in order before him
Job23:5 I would know the words which he would answer me
Job23:6 Would he contend with me in the greatness of his power? Nay; but he would give heed unto me.
Job23:7 There the upright might reason with him; So should I be delivered for ever from my judge.
Job23:8 Behold
Job23:9 On the left hand
Job23:10 But he knoweth the way that I take; When he hath tried me
Job23:11 My foot hath held fast to his steps; His way have I kept
Job23:12 I have not gone back from the commandment of his lips; I have treasured up the words of his mouth more than my necessary food.
Job23:13 But he is in one mind
Job23:14 For he performeth that which is appointed for me: And many such things are with him.
Job23:15 Therefore am I terrified at his presence; When I consider
Job23:16 For God hath made my heart faint
Job23:17 Because I was not cut off before the darkness
Job24:1 Why are times not laid up by the Almighty? And why do not they that know him see his days?
Job24:2 There are that remove the landmarks; They violently take away flocks
Job24:3 They drive away the ass of the fatherless; They take the widows ox for a pledge.
Job24:4 They turn the needy out of the way: The poor of the earth all hide themselves.
Job24:5 Behold
Job24:6 They cut their provender in the field; And they glean the vintage of the wicked.
Job24:7 They lie all night naked without clothing
Job24:8 They are wet with the showers of the mountains
Job24:9 There are that pluck the fatherless from the breast
Job24:10 So that they go about naked without clothing
Job24:11 They make oil within the walls of these men; They tread their winepresses
Job24:12 From out of the populous city men groan
Job24:13 These are of them that rebel against the light; They know not the ways thereof
Job24:14 The murderer riseth with the light; He killeth the poor and needy; And in the night he is as a thief.
Job24:15 The eye also of the adulterer waiteth for the twilight
Job24:16 In the dark they dig through houses: They shut themselves up in the day-time; They know not the light.
Job24:17 For the morning is to all of them as thick darkness; For they know the terrors of the thick darkness.
Job24:18 Swiftly they pass away upon the face of the waters; Their portion is cursed in the earth: They turn not into the way of the vineyards.
Job24:19 Drought and heat consume the snow waters: So doth Sheol those that have sinned.
Job24:20 The womb shall forget him; The worm shall feed sweetly on him; He shall be no more remembered; And unrighteousness shall be broken as a tree.
Job24:21 He devoureth the barren that beareth not
Job24:22 Yet God preserveth the mighty by his power: He riseth up that hath no assurance of life.
Job24:23 God giveth them to be in security
Job24:24 They are exalted; yet a little while
Job24:25 And if it be not so now
Job25:1 Then answered Bildad the Shuhite
Job25:2 Dominion and fear are with him; He maketh peace in his high places.
Job25:3 Is there any number of his armies? And upon whom doth not his light arise?
Job25:4 How then can man be just with God? Or how can he be clean that is born of a woman?
Job25:5 Behold
Job25:6 How much less man
Job26:1 Then Job answered and said
Job26:2 How hast thou helped him that is without power! How hast thou saved the arm that hath no strength!
Job26:3 How hast thou counselled him that hath no wisdom
Job26:4 To whom hast thou uttered words? And whose spirit came forth from thee?
Job26:5 They that are deceased tremble Beneath the waters and the inhabitants thereof.
Job26:6 Sheol is naked before God
Job26:7 He stretcheth out the north over empty space
Job26:8 He bindeth up the waters in his thick clouds; And the cloud is not rent under them.
Job26:9 He incloseth the face of his throne
Job26:10 He hath described a boundary upon the face of the waters
Job26:11 The pillars of heaven tremble And are astonished at his rebuke.
Job26:12 He stirreth up the sea with his power
Job26:13 By his Spirit the heavens are garnished; His hand hath pierced the swift serpent.
Job26:14 Lo
Job27:1 And Job again took up his parable
Job27:2 As God liveth
Job27:3 (For my life is yet whole in me
Job27:4 Surely my lips shall not speak unrighteousness
Job27:5 Far be it from me that I should justify you: Till I die I will not put away mine integrity from me.
Job27:6 My righteousness I hold fast
Job27:7 Let mine enemy be as the wicked
Job27:8 For what is the hope of the godless
Job27:9 Will God hear his cry
Job27:10 Will he delight himself in the Almighty
Job27:11 I will teach you concerning the hand of God; That which is with the Almighty will I not conceal.
Job27:12 Behold
Job27:13 This is the portion of a wicked man with God
Job27:14 If his children be multiplied
Job27:15 Those that remain of him shall be buried in death
Job27:16 Though he heap up silver as the dust
Job27:17 He may prepare it
Job27:18 He buildeth his house as the moth
Job27:19 He lieth down rich
Job27:20 Terrors overtake him like waters; A tempest stealeth him away in the night.
Job27:21 The east wind carrieth him away
Job27:22 For God shall hurl at him
Job27:23 Men shall clap their hands at him
Job28:1 Surely there is a mine for silver
Job28:2 Iron is taken out of the earth
Job28:3 Man setteth an end to darkness
Job28:4 He breaketh open a shaft away from where men sojourn; They are forgotten of the foot; They hang afar from men
Job28:5 As for the earth
Job28:6 The stones thereof are the place of sapphires
Job28:7 That path no bird of prey knoweth
Job28:8 The proud beasts have not trodden it
Job28:9 He putteth forth his hand upon the flinty rock; He overturneth the mountains by the roots.
Job28:10 He cutteth out channels among the rocks; And his eye seeth every precious thing.
Job28:11 He bindeth the streams that they trickle not; And the thing that is hid bringeth he forth to light.
Job28:12 But where shall wisdom be found? And where is the place of understanding?
Job28:13 Man knoweth not the price thereof; Neither is it found in the land of the living.
Job28:14 The deep saith
Job28:15 It cannot be gotten for gold
Job28:16 It cannot be valued with the gold of Ophir
Job28:17 Gold and glass cannot equal it
Job28:18 No mention shall be made of coral or of crystal: Yea
Job28:19 The topaz of Ethiopia shall not equal it
Job28:20 Whence then cometh wisdom? And where is the place of understanding?
Job28:21 Seeing it is hid from the eyes of all living
Job28:22 Destruction and Death say
Job28:23 God understandeth the way thereof
Job28:24 For he looketh to the ends of the earth
Job28:25 To make a weight for the wind: Yea
Job28:26 When he made a decree for the rain
Job28:27 Then did he see it
Job28:28 And unto man he said
Job29:1 And Job again took up his parable
Job29:2 Oh that I were as in the months of old
Job29:3 When his lamp shined upon my head
Job29:4 As I was in the ripeness of my days
Job29:5 When the Almighty was yet with me
Job29:6 When my steps were washed with butter
Job29:7 When I went forth to the gate unto the city
Job29:8 The young men saw me and hid themselves
Job29:9 The princes refrained from talking
Job29:10 The voice of the nobles was hushed
Job29:11 For when the ear heard me
Job29:12 Because I delivered the poor that cried
Job29:13 The blessing of him that was ready to perish came upon me; And I caused the widows heart to sing for joy.
Job29:14 I put on righteousness
Job29:15 I was eyes to the blind
Job29:16 I was a father to the needy: And the cause of him that I knew not I searched out.
Job29:17 And I brake the jaws of the unrighteous
Job29:18 Then I said
Job29:19 My root is spread out to the waters
Job29:20 My glory is fresh in me
Job29:21 Unto me men gave ear
Job29:22 After my words they spake not again; And my speech distilled upon them.
Job29:23 And they waited for me as for the rain; And they opened their mouth wide as for the latter rain.
Job29:24 I smiled on them
Job29:25 I chose out their way
Job30:1 But now they that are younger than I have me in derision
Job30:2 Yea
Job30:3 They are gaunt with want and famine; They gnaw the dry ground
Job30:4 They pluck salt-wort by the bushes; And the roots of the broom are their food.
Job30:5 They are driven forth from the midst of men; They cry after them as after a thief;
Job30:6 So that they dwell in frightful valleys
Job30:7 Among the bushes they bray; Under the nettles they are gathered together.
Job30:8 They are children of fools
Job30:9 And now I am become their song
Job30:10 They abhor me
Job30:11 For he hath loosed his cord
Job30:12 Upon my right hand rise the rabble; They thrust aside my feet
Job30:13 They mar my path
Job30:14 As through a wide breach they come: In the midst of the ruin they roll themselves upon me.
Job30:15 Terrors are turned upon me; They chase mine honor as the wind; And my welfare is passed away as a cloud.
Job30:16 And now my soul is poured out within me; Days of affliction have taken hold upon me.
Job30:17 In the night season my bones are pierced in me
Job30:18 By Gods great force is my garment disfigured; It bindeth me about as the collar of my coat.
Job30:19 He hath cast me into the mire
Job30:20 I cry unto thee
Job30:21 Thou art turned to be cruel to me; With the might of thy hand thou persecutest me.
Job30:22 Thou liftest me up to the wind
Job30:23 For I know that thou wilt bring me to death
Job30:24 Howbeit doth not one stretch out the hand in his fall? Or in his calamity therefore cry for help?
Job30:25 Did not I weep for him that was in trouble? Was not my soul grieved for the needy?
Job30:26 When I looked for good
Job30:27 My heart is troubled
Job30:28 I go mourning without the sun: I stand up in the assembly
Job30:29 I am a brother to jackals
Job30:30 My skin is black
Job30:31 Therefore is my harp turned to mourning
Job31:1 I made a covenant with mine eyes; How then should I look upon a virgin?
Job31:2 For what is the portion from God above
Job31:3 Is it not calamity to the unrighteous
Job31:4 Doth not he see my ways
Job31:5 If I have walked with falsehood
Job31:6 (Let me be weighed in an even balance
Job31:7 If my step hath turned out of the way
Job31:8 Then let me sow
Job31:9 If my heart hath been enticed unto a woman
Job31:10 Then let my wife grind unto another
Job31:11 For that were a heinous crime; Yea
Job31:12 For it is a fire that consumeth unto Destruction
Job31:13 If I have despised the cause of my man-servant or of my maid-servant
Job31:14 What then shall I do when God riseth up? And when he visiteth
Job31:15 Did not he that made me in the womb make him? And did not one fashion us in the womb?
Job31:16 If I have withheld the poor from their desire
Job31:17 Or have eaten my morsel alone
Job31:18 (Nay
Job31:19 If I have seen any perish for want of clothing
Job31:20 If his loins have not blessed me
Job31:21 If I have lifted up my hand against the fatherless
Job31:22 Then let my shoulder fall from the shoulder-blade
Job31:23 For calamity from God is a terror to me
Job31:24 If I have made gold my hope
Job31:25 If I have rejoiced because my wealth was great
Job31:26 If I have beheld the sun when it shined
Job31:27 And my heart hath been secretly enticed
Job31:28 This also were an iniquity to be punished by the judges; For I should have denied the God that is above.
Job31:29 If I have rejoiced at the destruction of him that hated me
Job31:30 (Yea
Job31:31 If the men of my tent have not said
Job31:32 (The sojourner hath not lodged in the street; But I have opened my doors to the traveller);
Job31:33 If like Adam I have covered my transgressions
Job31:34 Because I feared the great multitude
Job31:35 Oh that I had one to hear me! (Lo
Job31:36 Surely I would carry it upon my shoulder; I would bind it unto me as a crown:
Job31:37 I would declare unto him the number of my steps; As a prince would I go near unto him.
Job31:38 If my land crieth out against me
Job31:39 If I have eaten the fruits thereof without money
Job31:40 Let thistles grow instead of wheat
Job32:1 So these three men ceased to answer Job
Job32:2 Then was kindled the wrath of Elihu the son of Barachel the Buzite
Job32:3 Also against his three friends was his wrath kindled
Job32:4 Now Elihu had waited to speak unto Job
Job32:5 And when Elihu saw that there was no answer in the mouth of these three men
Job32:6 And Elihu the son of Barachel the Buzite answered and said
Job32:7 I said
Job32:8 But there is a spirit in man
Job32:9 It is not the great that are wise
Job32:10 Therefore I said
Job32:11 Behold
Job32:12 Yea
Job32:13 Beware lest ye say
Job32:14 For he hath not directed his words against me; Neither will I answer him with your speeches.
Job32:15 They are amazed
Job32:16 And shall I wait
Job32:17 I also will answer my part
Job32:18 For I am full of words; The spirit within me constraineth me.
Job32:19 Behold
Job32:20 I will speak
Job32:21 Let me not
Job32:22 For I know not to give flattering titles; Else would my Maker soon take me away.
Job33:1 Howbeit
Job33:2 Behold now
Job33:3 My words shall utter the uprightness of my heart; And that which my lips know they shall speak sincerely.
Job33:4 The Spirit of God hath made me
Job33:5 If thou canst
Job33:6 Behold
Job33:7 Behold
Job33:8 Surely thou hast spoken in my hearing
Job33:9 I am clean
Job33:10 Behold
Job33:11 He putteth my feet in the stocks
Job33:12 Behold
Job33:13 Why dost thou strive against him
Job33:14 For God speaketh once
Job33:15 In a dream
Job33:16 Then he openeth the ears of men
Job33:17 That he may withdraw man from his purpose
Job33:18 He keepeth back his soul from the pit
Job33:19 He is chastened also with pain upon his bed
Job33:20 So that his life abhorreth bread
Job33:21 His flesh is consumed away
Job33:22 Yea
Job33:23 If there be with him an angel
Job33:24 Then God is gracious unto him
Job33:25 His flesh shall be fresher than a childs; He returneth to the days of his youth.
Job33:26 He prayeth unto God
Job33:27 He singeth before men
Job33:28 He hath redeemed my soul from going into the pit
Job33:29 Lo
Job33:30 To bring back his soul from the pit
Job33:31 Mark well
Job33:32 If thou hast anything to say
Job33:33 If not
Job34:1 Moreover Elihu answered and said
Job34:2 Hear my words
Job34:3 For the ear trieth words
Job34:4 Let us choose for us that which is right: Let us know among ourselves what is good.
Job34:5 For Job hath said
Job34:6 Notwithstanding my right I am accounted a liar; My wound is incurable
Job34:7 What man is like Job
Job34:8 Who goeth in company with the workers of iniquity
Job34:9 For he hath said
Job34:10 Therefore hearken unto me
Job34:11 For the work of a man will he render unto him
Job34:12 Yea
Job34:13 Who gave him a charge over the earth? Or who hath disposed the whole world?
Job34:14 If he set his heart upon himself
Job34:15 All flesh shall perish together
Job34:16 If now thou hast understanding
Job34:17 Shall even one that hateth justice govern? And wilt thou condemn him that is righteous and mighty?-
Job34:18 Him that saith to a king
Job34:19 That respecteth not the persons of princes
Job34:20 In a moment they die
Job34:21 For his eyes are upon the ways of a man
Job34:22 There is no darkness
Job34:23 For he needeth not further to consider a man
Job34:24 He breaketh in pieces mighty men in ways past finding out
Job34:25 Therefore he taketh knowledge of their works; And he overturneth them in the night
Job34:26 He striketh them as wicked men In the open sight of others;
Job34:27 Because they turned aside from following him
Job34:28 So that they caused the cry of the poor to come unto him
Job34:29 When he giveth quietness
Job34:30 That the godless man reign not
Job34:31 For hath any said unto God
Job34:32 That which I see not teach thou me: If I have done iniquity
Job34:33 Shall his recompense be as thou wilt
Job34:34 Men of understanding will say unto me
Job34:35 Job speaketh without knowledge
Job34:36 Would that Job were tried unto the end
Job34:37 For he addeth rebellion unto his sin; He clappeth his hands among us
Job35:1 Moreover Elihu answered and said
Job35:2 Thinkest thou this to be thy right
Job35:3 That thou sayest
Job35:4 I will answer thee
Job35:5 Look unto the heavens
Job35:6 If thou hast sinned
Job35:7 If thou be righteous
Job35:8 Thy wickedness may hurt a man as thou art; And thy righteousness may profit a son of man.
Job35:9 By reason of the multitude of oppressions they cry out; They cry for help by reason of the arm of the mighty.
Job35:10 But none saith
Job35:11 Who teacheth us more than the beasts of the earth
Job35:12 There they cry
Job35:13 Surely God will not hear an empty cry
Job35:14 How much less when thou sayest thou beholdest him not
Job35:15 But now
Job35:16 Therefore doth Job open his mouth in vanity; He multiplieth words without knowledge.
Job36:1 Elihu also proceeded
Job36:2 Suffer me a little
Job36:3 I will fetch my knowledge from afar
Job36:4 For truly my words are not false: One that is perfect in knowledge is with thee.
Job36:5 Behold
Job36:6 He preserveth not the life of the wicked
Job36:7 He withdraweth not his eyes from the righteous: But with kings upon the throne He setteth them for ever
Job36:8 And if they be bound in fetters
Job36:9 Then he showeth them their work
Job36:10 He openeth also their ear to instruction
Job36:11 If they hearken and serve him
Job36:12 But if they hearken not
Job36:13 But they that are godless in heart lay up anger: They cry not for help when he bindeth them.
Job36:14 They die in youth
Job36:15 He delivereth the afflicted by their affliction
Job36:16 Yea
Job36:17 But thou art full of the judgment of the wicked: Judgment and justice take hold on thee.
Job36:18 For let not wrath stir thee up against chastisements; Neither let the greatness of the ransom turn thee aside.
Job36:19 Will thy cry avail
Job36:20 Desire not the night
Job36:21 Take heed
Job36:22 Behold
Job36:23 Who hath enjoined him his way? Or who can say
Job36:24 Remember that thou magnify his work
Job36:25 All men have looked thereon; Man beholdeth it afar off.
Job36:26 Behold
Job36:27 For he draweth up the drops of water
Job36:28 Which the skies pour down And drop upon man abundantly.
Job36:29 Yea
Job36:30 Behold
Job36:31 For by these he judgeth the peoples; He giveth food in abundance.
Job36:32 He covereth his hands with the lightning
Job36:33 The noise thereof telleth concerning him
Job37:1 Yea
Job37:2 Hear
Job37:3 He sendeth it forth under the whole heaven
Job37:4 After it a voice roareth; He thundereth with the voice of his majesty; And he restraineth not the lightnings when his voice is heard.
Job37:5 God thundereth marvellously with his voice; Great things doeth he
Job37:6 For he saith to the snow
Job37:7 He sealeth up the hand of every man
Job37:8 Then the beasts go into coverts
Job37:9 Out of the chamber of the south cometh the storm
Job37:10 By the breath of God ice is given; And the breadth of the waters is straitened.
Job37:11 Yea
Job37:12 And it is turned round about by his guidance
Job37:13 Whether it be for correction
Job37:14 Hearken unto this
Job37:15 Dost thou know how God layeth his charge upon them
Job37:16 Dost thou know the balancings of the clouds
Job37:17 How thy garments are warm
Job37:18 Canst thou with him spread out the sky
Job37:19 Teach us what we shall say unto him; For we cannot set our speech in order by reason of darkness.
Job37:20 Shall it be told him that I would speak? Or should a man wish that he were swallowed up?
Job37:21 And now men see not the light which is bright in the skies; But the wind passeth
Job37:22 Out of the north cometh golden splendor: God hath upon him terrible majesty.
Job37:23 Touching the Almighty
Job37:24 Men do therefore fear him: He regardeth not any that are wise of heart.
Job38:1 Then Jehovah answered Job out of the whirlwind
Job38:2 Who is this that darkeneth counsel By words without knowledge?
Job38:3 Gird up now thy loins like a man; For I will demand of thee
Job38:4 Where wast thou when I laid the foundations of the earth? Declare
Job38:5 Who determined the measures thereof
Job38:6 Whereupon were the foundations thereof fastened? Or who laid the corner-stone thereof
Job38:7 When the morning stars sang together
Job38:8 Or who shut up the sea with doors
Job38:9 When I made clouds the garment thereof
Job38:10 And marked out for it my bound
Job38:11 And said
Job38:12 Hast thou commanded the morning since thy days began
Job38:13 That it might take hold of the ends of the earth
Job38:14 It is changed as clay under the seal; And all things stand forth as a garment:
Job38:15 And from the wicked their light is withholden
Job38:16 Hast thou entered into the springs of the sea? Or hast thou walked in the recesses of the deep?
Job38:17 Have the gates of death been revealed unto thee? Or hast thou seen the gates of the shadow of death?
Job38:18 Hast thou comprehended the earth in its breadth? Declare
Job38:19 Where is the way to the dwelling of light? And as for darkness
Job38:20 That thou shouldest take it to the bound thereof
Job38:21 Doubtless
Job38:22 Hast thou entered the treasuries of the snow
Job38:23 Which I have reserved against the time of trouble
Job38:24 By what way is the light parted
Job38:25 Who hath cleft a channel for the waterflood
Job38:26 To cause it to rain on a land where no man is; On the wilderness
Job38:27 To satisfy the waste and desolate ground
Job38:28 Hath the rain a father? Or who hath begotten the drops of dew?
Job38:29 Out of whose womb came the ice? And the hoary frost of heaven
Job38:30 The waters hide themselves and become like stone
Job38:31 Canst thou bind the cluster of the Pleiades
Job38:32 Canst thou lead forth the Mazzaroth in their season? Or canst thou guide the Bear with her train?
Job38:33 Knowest thou the ordinances of the heavens? Canst thou establish the dominion thereof in the earth?
Job38:34 Canst thou lift up thy voice to the clouds
Job38:35 Canst thou send forth lightnings
Job38:36 Who hath put wisdom in the inward parts? Or who hath given understanding to the mind?
Job38:37 Who can number the clouds by wisdom? Or who can pour out the bottles of heaven
Job38:38 When the dust runneth into a mass
Job38:39 Canst thou hunt the prey for the lioness
Job38:40 When they couch in their dens
Job38:41 Who provideth for the raven his prey
Job39:1 Knowest thou the time when the wild goats of the rock bring forth? Or canst thou mark when the hinds do calve?
Job39:2 Canst thou number the months that they fulfil? Or knowest thou the time when they bring forth?
Job39:3 They bow themselves
Job39:4 Their young ones become strong
Job39:5 Who hath sent out the wild ass free? Or who hath loosed the bonds of the swift ass
Job39:6 Whose home I have made the wilderness
Job39:7 He scorneth the tumult of the city
Job39:8 The range of the mountains is his pasture
Job39:9 Will the wild-ox be content to serve thee? Or will he abide by thy crib?
Job39:10 Canst thou bind the wild-ox with his band in the furrow? Or will he harrow the valleys after thee?
Job39:11 Wilt thou trust him
Job39:12 Wilt thou confide in him
Job39:13 The wings of the ostrich wave proudly; But are they the pinions and plumage of love?
Job39:14 For she leaveth her eggs on the earth
Job39:15 And forgetteth that the foot may crush them
Job39:16 She dealeth hardly with her young ones
Job39:17 Because God hath deprived her of wisdom
Job39:18 What time she lifteth up herself on high
Job39:19 Hast thou given the horse his might? Hast thou clothed his neck with the quivering mane?
Job39:20 Hast thou made him to leap as a locust? The glory of his snorting is terrible.
Job39:21 He paweth in the valley
Job39:22 He mocketh at fear
Job39:23 The quiver rattleth against him
Job39:24 He swalloweth the ground with fierceness and rage; Neither believeth he that it is the voice of the trumpet.
Job39:25 As oft as the trumpet soundeth he saith
Job39:26 Is it by thy wisdom that the hawk soareth
Job39:27 Is it at thy command that the eagle mounteth up
Job39:28 On the cliff she dwelleth
Job39:29 From thence she spieth out the prey; Her eyes behold it afar off.
Job39:30 Her young ones also suck up blood: And where the slain are
Job40:1 Moreover Jehovah answered Job
Job40:2 Shall he that cavilleth contend with the Almighty? He that argueth with God
Job40:3 Then Job answered Jehovah
Job40:4 Behold
Job40:5 Once have I spoken
Job40:6 Then Jehovah answered Job out of the whirlwind
Job40:7 Gird up thy loins now like a man: I will demand of thee
Job40:8 Wilt thou even annul my judgment? Wilt thou condemn me
Job40:9 Or hast thou an arm like God? And canst thou thunder with a voice like him?
Job40:10 Deck thyself now with excellency and dignity; And array thyself with honor and majesty.
Job40:11 Pour forth the overflowings of thine anger; And look upon every one that is proud
Job40:12 Look on every one that is proud
Job40:13 Hide them in the dust together; Bind their faces in the hidden place.
Job40:14 Then will I also confess of thee That thine own right hand can save thee.
Job40:15 Behold now
Job40:16 Lo now
Job40:17 He moveth his tail like a cedar: The sinews of his thighs are knit together.
Job40:18 His bones are as tubes of brass; His limbs are like bars of iron.
Job40:19 He is the chief of the ways of God: He only that made him giveth him his sword.
Job40:20 Surely the mountains bring him forth food
Job40:21 He lieth under the lotus-trees
Job40:22 The lotus-trees cover him with their shade; The willows of the brook compass him about.
Job40:23 Behold
Job40:24 Shall any take him when he is on the watch
Job41:1 Canst thou draw out leviathan with a fishhook? Or press down his tongue with a cord?
Job41:2 Canst thou put a rope into his nose? Or pierce his jaw through with a hook?
Job41:3 Will he make many supplications unto thee? Or will he speak soft words unto thee?
Job41:4 Will he make a covenant with thee
Job41:5 Wilt thou play with him as with a bird? Or wilt thou bind him for thy maidens?
Job41:6 Will the bands of fishermen make traffic of him? Will they part him among the merchants?
Job41:7 Canst thou fill his skin with barbed irons
Job41:8 Lay thy hand upon him; Remember the battle
Job41:9 Behold
Job41:10 None is so fierce that he dare stir him up; Who then is he that can stand before me?
Job41:11 Who hath first given unto me
Job41:12 I will not keep silence concerning his limbs
Job41:13 Who can strip off his outer garment? Who shall come within his jaws?
Job41:14 Who can open the doors of his face? Round about his teeth is terror.
Job41:15 His strong scales are his pride
Job41:16 One is so near to another
Job41:17 They are joined one to another; They stick together
Job41:18 His sneezings flash forth light
Job41:19 Out of his mouth go burning torches
Job41:20 Out of his nostrils a smoke goeth
Job41:21 His breath kindleth coals
Job41:22 In his neck abideth strength
Job41:23 The flakes of his flesh are joined together: They are firm upon him; they cannot be moved.
Job41:24 His heart is as firm as a stone; Yea
Job41:25 When he raiseth himself up
Job41:26 If one lay at him with the sword
Job41:27 He counteth iron as straw
Job41:28 The arrow cannot make him flee: Sling-stones are turned with him into stubble.
Job41:29 Clubs are counted as stubble: He laugheth at the rushing of the javelin.
Job41:30 His underparts are like sharp potsherds: He spreadeth as it were a threshing-wain upon the mire.
Job41:31 He maketh the deep to boil like a pot: He maketh the sea like a pot of ointment.
Job41:32 He maketh a path to shine after him; One would think the deep to be hoary.
Job41:33 Upon earth there is not his like
Job41:34 He beholdeth everything that is high: He is king over all the sons of pride.
Job42:1 Then Job answered Jehovah
Job42:2 I know that thou canst do all things
Job42:3 Who is this that hideth counsel without knowledge? Therefore have I uttered that which I understood not
Job42:4 Hear
Job42:5 I had heard of thee by the hearing of the ear; But now mine eye seeth thee:
Job42:6 Wherefore I abhor myself
Job42:7 And it was so
Job42:8 Now therefore
Job42:9 So Eliphaz the Temanite and Bildad the Shuhite and Zophar the Naamathite went
Job42:10 And Jehovah turned the captivity of Job
Job42:11 Then came there unto him all his brethren
Job42:12 So Jehovah blessed the latter end of Job more than his beginning: And he had fourteen thousand sheep
Job42:13 He had also seven sons and three daughters.
Job42:14 And he called the name of the first
Job42:15 And in all the land were no women found so fair as the daughters of Job: and their father gave them inheritance among their brethren.
Job42:16 And after this Job lived a hundred and forty years
Job42:17 So Job died

Psalms1:6 For Jehovah knoweth the way of the righteous; But the way of the wicked shall perish.

Psalms18:2 Jehovah is my rock
Psalms18:3 I will call upon Jehovah
Psalms18:4 The cords of death compassed me
Psalms18:5 The cords of Sheol were round about me; The snares of death came upon me.
Psalms18:6 In my distress I called upon Jehovah
Psalms18:7 Then the earth shook and trembled; The foundations also of the mountains quaked And were shaken
Psalms18:8 There went up a smoke out of his nostrils
Psalms18:9 He bowed the heavens also
Psalms18:10 And he rode upon a cherub
Psalms18:11 He made darkness his hiding-place
Psalms18:12 At the brightness before him his thick clouds passed
Psalms18:13 Jehovah also thundered in the heavens
Psalms18:14 And he sent out his arrows
Psalms18:15 Then the channels of waters appeared
Psalms18:16 He sent from on high
Psalms18:17 He delivered me from my strong enemy
Psalms18:18 They came upon me in the day of my calamity; But Jehovah was my stay.
Psalms18:19 He brought me forth also into a large place; He delivered me
Psalms18:20 Jehovah hath rewarded me according to my righteousness; According to the cleanness of my hands hath he recompensed me.
Psalms18:21 For I have kept the ways of Jehovah
Psalms18:22 For all his ordinances were before me
Psalms18:23 I was also perfect with him
Psalms18:24 Therefore hath Jehovah recompensed me according to my righteousness
Psalms18:25 With the merciful thou wilt show thyself merciful; With the perfect man thou wilt show thyself perfect;
Psalms18:26 With the pure thou wilt show thyself pure; And with the perverse thou wilt show thyself froward.
Psalms18:27 For thou wilt save the afflicted people; But the haughty eyes thou wilt bring down.
Psalms18:28 For thou wilt light my lamp: Jehovah my God will lighten my darkness.
Psalms18:29 For by thee I run upon a troop; And by my God do I leap over a wall.
Psalms18:30 As for God
Psalms18:31 For who is God
Psalms18:32 The God that girdeth me with strength
Psalms18:33 He maketh my feet like hinds feet: And setteth me upon my high places.
Psalms18:34 He teacheth my hands to war; So that mine arms do bend a bow of brass.
Psalms18:35 Thou hast also given me the shield of thy salvation; And thy right hand hath holden me up
Psalms18:36 Thou hast enlarged my steps under me
Psalms18:37 I will pursue mine enemies
Psalms18:38 I will smite them through
Psalms18:39 For thou hast girded me with strength unto the battle: Thou hast subdued under me those that rose up against me.
Psalms18:40 Thou hast also made mine enemies turn their backs unto me
Psalms18:41 They cried
Psalms18:42 Then did I beat them small as the dust before the wind; I did cast them out as the mire of the streets.
Psalms18:43 Thou hast delivered me from the strivings of the people; Thou hast made me the head of the nations: A people whom I have not known shall serve me.
Psalms18:44 As soon as they hear of me they shall obey me; The foreigners shall submit themselves unto me.
Psalms18:45 The foreigners shall fade away

Psalms18:47 Even the God that executeth vengeance for me
Psalms18:48 He rescueth me from mine enemies; Yea
Psalms18:49 Therefore I will give thanks unto thee

Psalms19:2 Day unto day uttereth speech
Psalms19:3 There is no speech nor language; Their voice is not heard.
Psalms19:4 Their line is gone out through all the earth
Psalms19:5 Which is as a bridegroom coming out of his chamber
Psalms19:6 His going forth is from the end of the heavens
Psalms19:7 The law of Jehovah is perfect
Psalms19:8 The precepts of Jehovah are right
Psalms19:9 The fear of Jehovah is clean
Psalms19:10 More to be desired are they than gold
Psalms19:11 Moreover by them is thy servant warned: In keeping them there is great reward.
Psalms19:12 Who can discern his errors? Clear thou me from hidden faults.
Psalms19:13 Keep back thy servant also from presumptuous sins; Let them not have dominion over me: Then shall I be upright
Psalms19:14 Let the words of my mouth and the meditation of my heart Be acceptable in thy sight

Psalms21:11 For they intended evil against thee; They conceived a device which they are not able to perform.

Psalms22:1 My God
Psalms22:2 O my God
Psalms22:3 But thou art holy
Psalms22:4 Our fathers trusted in thee: They trusted
Psalms22:5 They cried unto thee
Psalms22:6 But I am a worm
Psalms22:7 All they that see me laugh me to scorn: They shoot out the lip
Psalms22:8 Commit thyself unto Jehovah; Let him deliver him: Let him rescue him
Psalms22:9 But thou art he that took me out of the womb; Thou didst make me trust when I was upon my mothers breasts.
Psalms22:10 I was cast upon thee from the womb; Thou art my God since my mother bare me.
Psalms22:11 Be not far from me; for trouble is near; For there is none to help.
Psalms22:12 Many bulls have compassed me; Strong bulls of Bashan have beset me round.
Psalms22:13 They gape upon me with their mouth
Psalms22:14 I am poured out like water
Psalms22:15 My strength is dried up like a potsherd; And my tongue cleaveth to my jaws; And thou hast brought me into the dust of death.
Psalms22:16 For dogs have compassed me: A company of evil-doers have inclosed me; They pierced my hands and my feet.
Psalms22:17 I may count all my bones; They look and stare upon me.
Psalms22:18 They part my garments among them
Psalms22:19 But be not thou far off
Psalms22:20 Deliver my soul from the sword

Psalms22:25 Of thee cometh my praise in the great assembly: I will pay my vows before them that fear him.
Psalms22:26 The meek shall eat and be satisfied; They shall praise Jehovah that seek after him: Let your heart live for ever.
Psalms22:27 All the ends of the earth shall remember and turn unto Jehovah; And all the kindreds of the nations shall worship before thee.
Psalms22:28 For the kingdom is Jehovahs; And he is the ruler over the nations.

Psalms44:3 For they gat not the land in possession by their own sword

Psalms44:5 Through thee will we push down our adversaries: Through thy name will we tread them under that rise up against us.
Psalms44:6 For I will not trust in my bow
Psalms44:7 But thou hast saved us from our adversaries
Psalms44:8 In God have we made our boast all the day long
Psalms44:9 But now thou hast cast us off
Psalms44:10 Thou makest us to turn back from the adversary; And they that hate us take spoil for themselves.
Psalms44:11 Thou hast made us like sheep appointed for food
Psalms44:12 Thou sellest thy people for nought
Psalms44:13 Thou makest us a reproach to our neighbors
Psalms44:14 Thou makest us a byword among the nations
Psalms44:15 All the day long is my dishonor before me
Psalms44:16 For the voice of him that reproacheth and blasphemeth
Psalms44:17 All this is come upon us; Yet have we not forgotten thee
Psalms44:18 Our heart is not turned back
Psalms44:19 That thou hast sore broken us in the place of jackals
Psalms44:20 If we have forgotten the name of our God
Psalms44:21 Will not God search this out? For he knoweth the secrets of the heart.
Psalms44:22 Yea
Psalms44:23 Awake
Psalms44:24 Wherefore hidest thou thy face
Psalms44:25 For our soul is bowed down to the dust: Our body cleaveth unto the earth.
Psalms44:26 Rise up for our help
Psalms45:1 My heart overfloweth with a goodly matter; I speak the things which I have made touching the king: My tongue is the pen of a ready writer.
Psalms45:2 Thou art fairer than the children of men; Grace is poured into thy lips: Therefore God hath blessed thee for ever.
Psalms45:3 Gird thy sword upon thy thigh
Psalms45:4 And in thy majesty ride on prosperously
Psalms45:5 Thine arrows are sharp; The peoples fall under thee; They are in the heart of the kings enemies.
Psalms45:6 Thy throne
Psalms45:7 Thou hast loved righteousness
Psalms45:8 All thy garments smell of myrrh
Psalms45:9 Kings daughters are among thy honorable women: At thy right hand doth stand the queen in gold of Ophir.
Psalms45:10 Hearken
Psalms45:11 So will the king desire thy beauty; For he is thy lord; and reverence thou him.
Psalms45:12 And the daughter of Tyre shall be there with a gift; The rich among the people shall entreat thy favor.
Psalms45:13 The kings daughter within the palace is all glorious: Her clothing is inwrought with gold.
Psalms45:14 She shall be led unto the king in broidered work: The virgins her companions that follow her Shall be brought unto thee.
Psalms45:15 With gladness and rejoicing shall they be led: They shall enter into the kings palace.
Psalms45:16 Instead of thy fathers shall be thy children
Psalms45:17 I will make thy name to be remembered in all generations: Therefore shall the peoples give thee thanks for ever and ever. Psalm 46 For the Chief Musician. A Psalm of the sons of Korah; set to Alamoth. A Song.

Psalms46:2 Therefore will we not fear

Psalms46:4 There is a river

Psalms46:6 The nations raged

Psalms46:11 Jehovah of hosts is with us; The God of Jacob is our refuge. Selah Psalm 47 For the Chief Musician. A Psalm of the sons of Korah.

Psalms47:2 For Jehovah Most High is terrible; He is a great King over all the earth.

Psalms47:4 He chooseth our inheritance for us
Psalms47:5 God is gone up with a shout

Psalms47:7 For God is the King of all the earth: Sing ye praises with understanding.
Psalms47:8 God reigneth over the nations: God sitteth upon his holy throne.
Psalms47:9 The princes of the peoples are gathered together To be the people of the God of Abraham: For the shields of the earth belong unto God; He is greatly exalted. Psalm 48 A Song; a Psalm of the sons of Korah.
Psalms48:1 Great is Jehovah
Psalms48:2 Beautiful in elevation
Psalms48:3 God hath made himself known in her palaces for a refuge.
Psalms48:4 For
Psalms48:5 They saw it
Psalms48:6 Trembling took hold of them there
Psalms48:7 With the east wind Thou breakest the ships of Tarshish.
Psalms48:8 As we have heard
Psalms48:9 We have thought on thy lovingkindness
Psalms48:10 As is thy name
Psalms48:11 Let mount Zion be glad
Psalms48:12 Walk about Zion
Psalms48:13 Mark ye well her bulwarks; Consider her palaces: That ye may tell it to the generation following.
Psalms48:14 For this God is our God for ever and ever: He will be our guide even unto death. Psalm 49 For the Chief Musician. A Psalm of the sons of Korah.
Psalms49:1 Hear this
Psalms49:2 Both low and high
Psalms49:3 My mouth shall speak wisdom; And the meditation of my heart shall be of understanding.
Psalms49:4 I will incline mine ear to a parable: I will open my dark saying upon the harp.
Psalms49:5 Wherefore should I fear in the days of evil
Psalms49:6 They that trust in their wealth
Psalms49:7 None of them can by any means redeem his brother
Psalms49:8 (For the redemption of their life is costly
Psalms49:9 That he should still live alway
Psalms49:10 For he shall see it. Wise men die; The fool and the brutish alike perish
Psalms49:11 Their inward thought is
Psalms49:12 But man being in honor abideth not: He is like the beasts that perish.
Psalms49:13 This their way is their folly: Yet after them men approve their sayings. Selah
Psalms49:14 They are appointed as a flock for Sheol; Death shall be their shepherd; And the upright shall have dominion over them in the morning; And their beauty shall be for Sheol to consume
Psalms49:15 But God will redeem my soul from the power of Sheol; For he will receive me. Selah
Psalms49:16 Be not thou afraid when one is made rich
Psalms49:17 For when he dieth he shall carry nothing away; His glory shall not descend after him.
Psalms49:18 Though while he lived he blessed his soul (And men praise thee
Psalms49:19 He shall go to the generation of his fathers; They shall never see the light.
Psalms49:20 Man that is in honor
Psalms50:1 The Mighty One
Psalms50:2 Out of Zion
Psalms50:3 Our God cometh
Psalms50:4 He calleth to the heavens above
Psalms50:5 Gather my saints together unto me
Psalms50:6 And the heavens shall declare his righteousness; For God is judge himself. Selah
Psalms50:7 Hear
Psalms50:8 I will not reprove thee for thy sacrifices; And thy burnt-offerings are continually before me.
Psalms50:9 I will take no bullock out of thy house
Psalms50:10 For every beast of the forest is mine
Psalms50:11 I know all the birds of the mountains; And the wild beasts of the field are mine.
Psalms50:12 If I were hungry
Psalms50:13 Will I eat the flesh of bulls
Psalms50:14 Offer unto God the sacrifice of thanksgiving; And pay thy vows unto the Most High:
Psalms50:15 And call upon me in the day of trouble; I will deliver thee
Psalms50:16 But unto the wicked God saith
Psalms50:17 Seeing thou hatest instruction
Psalms50:18 When thou sawest a thief
Psalms50:19 Thou givest thy mouth to evil
Psalms50:20 Thou sittest and speakest against thy brother; Thou slanderest thine own mothers son.
Psalms50:21 These things hast thou done
Psalms50:22 Now consider this
Psalms50:23 Whoso offereth the sacrifice of thanksgiving glorifieth me; And to him that ordereth his way aright Will I show the salvation of God. Psalm 51 For the Chief Musician. A Psalm of David; when Nathan the prophet came unto him
Psalms51:1 Have mercy upon me

Psalms51:3 For I know my transgressions; And my sin is ever before me.
Psalms51:4 Against thee
Psalms51:5 Behold
Psalms51:6 Behold

Psalms51:8 Make me to hear joy and gladness

Psalms51:11 Cast me not away from thy presence; And take not thy holy Spirit from me.
Psalms51:12 Restore unto me the joy of thy salvation; And uphold me with a willing spirit.
Psalms51:13 Then will I teach transgressors thy ways; And sinners shall be converted unto thee.
Psalms51:14 Deliver me from bloodguiltiness
Psalms51:15 O Lord
Psalms51:16 For thou delightest not in sacrifice; else would I give it: Thou hast no pleasure in burnt-offering.
Psalms51:17 The sacrifices of God are a broken spirit: A broken and contrite heart
Psalms51:18 Do good in thy good pleasure unto Zion: Build thou the walls of Jerusalem.
Psalms51:19 Then will thou delight in the sacrifices of righteousness
Psalms52:1 Why boastest thou thyself in mischief
Psalms52:2 Thy tongue deviseth very wickedness
Psalms52:3 Thou lovest evil more than good

Psalms52:6 The righteous also shall see it
Psalms52:7 Lo
Psalms52:8 But as for me
Psalms52:9 I will give thee thanks for ever

Psalms54:7 For he hath delivered me out of all trouble; And mine eye hath seen my desire upon mine enemies. Psalm 55 For the Chief Musician; on stringed instruments. Maschil of David.

Psalms55:6 And I said
Psalms55:7 Lo

Psalms55:11 Wickedness is in the midst thereof: Oppression and guile depart not from its streets.
Psalms55:12 For it was not an enemy that reproached me; Then I could have borne it: Neither was it he that hated me that did magnify himself against me; Then I would have hid myself from him:

Psalms56:1 Be merciful unto me
Psalms56:2 Mine enemies would swallow me up all the day long; For they are many that fight proudly against me.
Psalms56:3 What time I am afraid
Psalms56:4 In God (I will praise his word)

Psalms56:6 They gather themselves together
Psalms56:7 Shall they escape by iniquity? In anger cast down the peoples

Psalms60:5 That thy beloved may be delivered

Psalms60:12 Through God we shall do valiantly; For he it is that will tread down our adversaries. Psalm 61 For the Chief Musician; on a stringed instrument. A Psalm of David.

Psalms64:7 But God will shoot at them; With an arrow suddenly shall they be wounded.
Psalms64:8 So they shall be made to stumble

Psalms65:3 Iniquities prevail against me: As for our transgressions
Psalms65:4 Blessed is the man whom thou choosest

Psalms65:6 Who by his strength setteth fast the mountains
Psalms65:7 Who stilleth the roaring of the seas
Psalms65:8 They also that dwell in the uttermost parts are afraid at thy tokens: Thou makest the outgoings of the morning and evening to rejoice.

Psalms65:10 Thou waterest its furrows abundantly; Thou settlest the ridges thereof: Thou makest it soft with showers; Thou blessest the springing thereof.

Psalms68:35 O God
Psalms69:1 Save me
Psalms69:2 I sink in deep mire
Psalms69:3 I am weary with my crying; my throat is dried: Mine eyes fail while I wait for my God.
Psalms69:4 They that hate me without a cause are more than the hairs of my head: They that would cut me off
Psalms69:5 O God
Psalms69:6 Let not them that wait for thee be put to shame through me
Psalms69:7 Because for thy sake I have borne reproach; Shame hath covered my face.
Psalms69:8 I am become a stranger unto my brethren
Psalms69:9 For the zeal of thy house hath eaten me up; And the reproaches of them that reproach thee are fallen upon me.
Psalms69:10 When I wept
Psalms69:11 When I made sackcloth my clothing
Psalms69:12 They that sit in the gate talk of me; And I am the song of the drunkards.
Psalms69:13 But as for me
Psalms69:14 Deliver me out of the mire
Psalms69:15 Let not the waterflood overwhelm me
Psalms69:16 Answer me
Psalms69:17 And hide not thy face from thy servant; For I am in distress; answer me speedily.
Psalms69:18 Draw nigh unto my soul
Psalms69:19 Thou knowest my reproach
Psalms69:20 Reproach hath broken my heart; and I am full of heaviness: And I looked for some to take pity
Psalms69:21 They gave me also gall for my food; And in my thirst they gave me vinegar to drink.
Psalms69:22 Let their table before them become a snare; And when they are in peace
Psalms69:23 Let their eyes be darkened
Psalms69:24 Pour out thine indignation upon them
Psalms69:25 Let their habitation be desolate; Let none dwell in their tents.
Psalms69:26 For they persecute him whom thou hast smitten; And they tell of the sorrow of those whom thou hast wounded.
Psalms69:27 Add iniquity unto their iniquity; And let them not come into thy righteousness.
Psalms69:28 Let them be blotted out of the book of life
Psalms69:29 But I am poor and sorrowful: Let thy salvation
Psalms69:30 I will praise the name of God with a song
Psalms69:31 And it will please Jehovah better than an ox
Psalms69:32 The meek have seen it
Psalms69:33 For Jehovah heareth the needy
Psalms69:34 Let heaven and earth praise him
Psalms69:35 For God will save Zion
Psalms69:36 The seed also of his servants shall inherit it; And they that love his name shall dwell therein. Psalm 70 For the Chief Musician. A Psalm of David; to bring to remembrance.
Psalms70:1 Make haste

Psalms70:5 But I am poor and needy; Make haste unto me
Psalms71:1 In thee
Psalms71:2 Deliver me in thy righteousness
Psalms71:3 Be thou to me a rock of habitation
Psalms71:4 Rescue me
Psalms71:5 For thou art my hope
Psalms71:6 By thee have I been holden up from the womb; Thou art he that took me out of my mothers bowels: My praise shall be continually of thee.
Psalms71:7 I am as a wonder unto many; But thou art my strong refuge.
Psalms71:8 My mouth shall be filled with thy praise
Psalms71:9 Cast me not off in the time of old age; Forsake me not when my strength faileth.
Psalms71:10 For mine enemies speak concerning me; And they that watch for my soul take counsel together
Psalms71:11 Saying
Psalms71:12 O God
Psalms71:13 Let them be put to shame and consumed that are adversaries to my soul; Let them be covered with reproach and dishonor that seek my hurt.
Psalms71:14 But I will hope continually
Psalms71:15 My mouth shall tell of thy righteousness
Psalms71:16 I will come with the mighty acts of the Lord Jehovah: I will make mention of thy righteousness
Psalms71:17 O God
Psalms71:18 Yea
Psalms71:19 Thy righteousness also
Psalms71:20 Thou
Psalms71:21 Increase thou my greatness
Psalms71:22 I will also praise thee with the psaltery
Psalms71:23 My lips shall shout for joy when I sing praises unto thee; And my soul
Psalms71:24 My tongue also shall talk of thy righteousness all the day long; For they are put to shame
Psalms72:1 Give the king thy judgments
Psalms72:2 He will judge thy people with righteousness
Psalms72:3 The mountains shall bring peace to the people
Psalms72:4 He will judge the poor of the people
Psalms72:5 They shall fear thee while the sun endureth
Psalms72:6 He will come down like rain upon the mown grass
Psalms72:7 In his days shall the righteous flourish
Psalms72:8 He shall have dominion also from sea to sea
Psalms72:9 They that dwell in the wilderness shall bow before him; And his enemies shall lick the dust.
Psalms72:10 The kings of Tarshish and of the isles shall render tribute: The kings of Sheba and Seba shall offer gifts.
Psalms72:11 Yea
Psalms72:12 For he will deliver the needy when he crieth
Psalms72:13 He will have pity on the poor and needy
Psalms72:14 He will redeem their soul from oppression and violence; And precious will their blood be in his sight:
Psalms72:15 And they shall live; and to him shall be given of the gold of Sheba: And men shall pray for him continually; They shall bless him all the day long.
Psalms72:16 There shall be abundance of grain in the earth upon the top of the mountains; The fruit thereof shall shake like Lebanon: And they of the city shall flourish like grass of the earth.
Psalms72:17 His name shall endure for ever; His name shall be continued as long as the sun: And men shall be blessed in him; All nations shall call him happy.
Psalms72:18 Blessed be Jehovah God
Psalms72:19 And blessed be his glorious name for ever; And let the whole earth be filled with his glory. Amen
Psalms72:20 The prayers of David the son of Jesse are ended. Psalm 73 A Psalm of Asaph.
Psalms73:1 Surely God is good to Israel
Psalms73:2 But as for me
Psalms73:3 For I was envious at the arrogant
Psalms73:4 For there are no pangs in their death; But their strength is firm.
Psalms73:5 They are not in trouble as other men; Neither are they plagued like other men.
Psalms73:6 Therefore pride is as a chain about their neck; Violence covereth them as a garment.
Psalms73:7 Their eyes stand out with fatness: They have more than heart could wish.
Psalms73:8 They scoff
Psalms73:9 They have set their mouth in the heavens
Psalms73:10 Therefore his people return hither: And waters of a full cup are drained by them.
Psalms73:11 And they say
Psalms73:12 Behold
Psalms73:13 Surely in vain have I cleansed my heart
Psalms73:14 For all the day long have I been plagued
Psalms73:15 If I had said
Psalms73:16 When I thought how I might know this
Psalms73:17 Until I went into the sanctuary of God
Psalms73:18 Surely thou settest them in slippery places: Thou castest them down to destruction.
Psalms73:19 How are they become a desolation in a moment! They are utterly consumed with terrors.
Psalms73:20 As a dream when one awaketh
Psalms73:21 For my soul was grieved
Psalms73:22 So brutish was I
Psalms73:23 Nevertheless I am continually with thee: Thou hast holden my right hand.
Psalms73:24 Thou wilt guide me with thy counsel
Psalms73:25 Whom have I in heaven but thee? And there is none upon earth that I desire besides thee.
Psalms73:26 My flesh and my heart faileth; But God is the strength of my heart and my portion for ever.
Psalms73:27 For
Psalms73:28 But it is good for me to draw near unto God: I have made the Lord Jehovah my refuge
Psalms74:1 O God
Psalms74:2 Remember thy congregation
Psalms74:3 Lift up thy feet unto the perpetual ruins
Psalms74:4 Thine adversaries have roared in the midst of thine assembly; They have set up their ensigns for signs.
Psalms74:5 They seemed as men that lifted up Axes upon a thicket of trees.
Psalms74:6 And now all the carved work thereof They break down with hatchet and hammers.
Psalms74:7 They have set thy sanctuary on fire; They have profaned the dwelling-place of thy name by casting it to the ground.
Psalms74:8 They said in their heart
Psalms74:9 We see not our signs: There is no more any prophet; Neither is there among us any that knoweth how long.
Psalms74:10 How long
Psalms74:11 Why drawest thou back thy hand
Psalms74:12 Yet God is my King of old
Psalms74:13 Thou didst divide the sea by thy strength: Thou brakest the heads of the sea-monsters in the waters.
Psalms74:14 Thou brakest the heads of leviathan in pieces; Thou gavest him to be food to the people inhabiting the wilderness.
Psalms74:15 Thou didst cleave fountain and flood: Thou driedst up mighty rivers.
Psalms74:16 The day is thine
Psalms74:17 Thou hast set all the borders of the earth: Thou hast made summer and winter.
Psalms74:18 Remember this
Psalms74:19 Oh deliver not the soul of thy turtle-dove unto the wild beast: Forget not the life of thy poor for ever.
Psalms74:20 Have respect unto the covenant; For the dark places of the earth are full of the habitations of violence.
Psalms74:21 Oh let not the oppressed return ashamed: Let the poor and needy praise thy name.
Psalms74:22 Arise
Psalms74:23 Forget not the voice of thine adversaries: The tumult of those that rise up against thee ascendeth continually. Psalm 75 For the Chief Musician; set to Al-tash-heth. A Psalm of Asaph; a song.

Psalms76:7 Thou

Psalms76:11 Vow
Psalms76:12 He will cut off the spirit of princes: He is terrible to the kings of the earth. Psalm 77 For the Chief Musician; after the manner of Jeduthan. A Psalm of Asaph.
Psalms77:1 I will cry unto God with my voice
Psalms77:2 In the day of my trouble I sought the Lord: My hand was stretched out in the night
Psalms77:3 I remember God
Psalms77:4 Thou holdest mine eyes watching: I am so troubled that I cannot speak.
Psalms77:5 I have considered the days of old
Psalms77:6 I call to remembrance my song in the night: I commune with mine own heart; And my spirit maketh diligent search.
Psalms77:7 Will the Lord cast off for ever? And will he be favorable no more?
Psalms77:8 Is his lovingkindness clean gone for ever? Doth his promise fail for evermore?
Psalms77:9 Hath God forgotten to be gracious? Hath he in anger shut up his tender mercies? Selah
Psalms77:10 And I said
Psalms77:11 I will make mention of the deeds of Jehovah; For I will remember thy wonders of old.
Psalms77:12 I will meditate also upon all thy work
Psalms77:13 Thy way
Psalms77:14 Thou art the God that doest wonders: Thou hast made known thy strength among the peoples.
Psalms77:15 Thou hast with thine arm redeemed thy people
Psalms77:16 The waters saw thee
Psalms77:17 The clouds poured out water; The skies sent out a sound: Thine arrows also went abroad.
Psalms77:18 The voice of thy thunder was in the whirlwind; The lightnings lightened the world: The earth trembled and shook.
Psalms77:19 Thy way was in the sea
Psalms77:20 Thou leddest thy people like a flock
Psalms78:1 Give ear
Psalms78:2 I will open my mouth in a parable; I will utter dark sayings of old
Psalms78:3 Which we have heard and known
Psalms78:4 We will not hide them from their children
Psalms78:5 For he established a testimony in Jacob
Psalms78:6 That the generation to come might know them
Psalms78:7 That they might set their hope in God
Psalms78:8 And might not be as their fathers
Psalms78:9 The children of Ephraim
Psalms78:10 They kept not the covenant of God
Psalms78:11 And they forgat his doings
Psalms78:12 Marvellous things did he in the sight of their fathers
Psalms78:13 He clave the sea
Psalms78:14 In the day-time also he led them with a cloud
Psalms78:15 He clave rocks in the wilderness
Psalms78:16 He brought streams also out of the rock
Psalms78:17 Yet went they on still to sin against him
Psalms78:18 And they tempted God in their heart By asking food according to their desire.
Psalms78:19 Yea
Psalms78:20 Behold
Psalms78:21 Therefore Jehovah heard
Psalms78:22 Because they believed not in God
Psalms78:23 Yet he commanded the skies above
Psalms78:24 And he rained down manna upon them to eat
Psalms78:25 Man did eat the bread of the mighty: He sent them food to the full.
Psalms78:26 He caused the east wind to blow in the heavens; And by his power he guided the south wind.
Psalms78:27 He rained flesh also upon them as the dust
Psalms78:28 And he let it fall in the midst of their camp
Psalms78:29 So they did eat
Psalms78:30 They were not estranged from that which they desired
Psalms78:31 When the anger of God went up against them
Psalms78:32 For all this they sinned still
Psalms78:33 Therefore their days did he consume in vanity
Psalms78:34 When he slew them
Psalms78:35 And they remembered that God was their rock
Psalms78:36 But they flattered him with their mouth
Psalms78:37 For their heart was not right with him
Psalms78:38 But he
Psalms78:39 And he remembered that they were but flesh
Psalms78:40 How oft did they rebel against him in the wilderness
Psalms78:41 And they turned again and tempted God
Psalms78:42 They remember not his hand
Psalms78:43 How he set his signs in Egypt
Psalms78:44 And turned their rivers into blood
Psalms78:45 He sent among them swarms of flies
Psalms78:46 He gave also their increase unto the caterpillar
Psalms78:47 He destroyed their vines with hail
Psalms78:48 He gave over their cattle also to the hail
Psalms78:49 He cast upon them the fierceness of his anger
Psalms78:50 He made a path for his anger; He spared not their soul from death
Psalms78:51 And smote all the first-born in Egypt
Psalms78:52 But he led forth his own people like sheep
Psalms78:53 And he led them safely
Psalms78:54 And he brought them to the border of his sanctuary
Psalms78:55 He drove out the nations also before them
Psalms78:56 Yet they tempted and rebelled against the Most High God
Psalms78:57 But turned back
Psalms78:58 For they provoked him to anger with their high places
Psalms78:59 When God heard this
Psalms78:60 So that he forsook the tabernacle of Shiloh
Psalms78:61 And delivered his strength into captivity
Psalms78:62 He gave his people over also unto the sword
Psalms78:63 Fire devoured their young men; And their virgins had no marriage-song.
Psalms78:64 Their priests fell by the sword; And their widows made no lamentation.
Psalms78:65 Then the Lord awaked as one out of sleep
Psalms78:66 And he smote his adversaries backward: He put them to a perpetual reproach.
Psalms78:67 Moreover he refused the tent of Joseph
Psalms78:68 But chose the tribe of Judah
Psalms78:69 And he built his sanctuary like the heights
Psalms78:70 He chose David also his servant
Psalms78:71 From following the ewes that have their young he brought him
Psalms78:72 So he was their shepherd according to the integrity of his heart

Psalms89:26 He shall cry unto me
Psalms89:27 I also will make him my first-born
Psalms89:28 My lovingkindness will I keep for him for evermore; And my covenant shall stand fast with him.
Psalms89:29 His seed also will I make to endure for ever
Psalms89:30 If his children forsake my law
Psalms89:31 If they break my statutes
Psalms89:32 Then will I visit their transgression with the rod
Psalms89:33 But my lovingkindness will I not utterly take from him
Psalms89:34 My covenant will I not break

Psalms89:36 His seed shall endure for ever
Psalms89:37 It shall be established for ever as the moon

Psalms102:1 Hear my prayer
Psalms102:2 Hide not thy face from me in the day of my distress: Incline thine ear unto me; In the day when I call answer me speedily.

Psalms102:4 My heart is smitten like grass
Psalms102:5 By reason of the voice of my groaning My bones cleave to my flesh.
Psalms102:6 I am like a pelican of the wilderness; I am become as an owl of the waste places.
Psalms102:7 I watch
Psalms102:8 Mine enemies reproach me all the day; They that are mad against me do curse by me.
Psalms102:9 For I have eaten ashes like bread
Psalms102:10 Because of thine indignation and thy wrath: For thou hast taken me up
Psalms102:11 My days are like a shadow that declineth; And I am withered like grass.
Psalms102:12 But thou

Psalms102:14 For thy servants take pleasure in her stones

Psalms102:16 For Jehovah hath built up Zion; He hath appeared in his glory.
Psalms102:17 He hath regarded the prayer of the destitute
Psalms102:18 This shall be written for the generation to come; And a people which shall be created shall praise Jehovah.

Psalms102:22 When the peoples are gathered together

Psalms102:26 They shall perish
Psalms102:27 But thou art the same
Psalms102:28 The children of thy servants shall continue

Psalms104:4 Who maketh winds his messengers; Flames of fire his ministers;

Psalms104:6 Thou coveredst it with the deep as with a vesture; The waters stood above the mountains.

Psalms104:12 By them the birds of the heavens have their habitation; They sing among the branches.

Psalms104:15 And wine that maketh glad the heart of man
Psalms104:16 The trees of Jehovah are filled with moisture
Psalms104:17 Where the birds make their nests: As for the stork
Psalms104:18 The high mountains are for the wild goats; The rocks are a refuge for the conies.

Psalms104:21 The young lions roar after their prey
Psalms104:22 The sun ariseth

Psalms108:1 My heart is fixed

Psalms108:13 Through God we shall do valiantly: For he it is that will tread down our adversaries. Psalm 109 For the Chief Musicion. A Psalm of David.
Psalms109:1 Hold not thy peace
Psalms109:2 For the mouth of the wicked and the mouth of deceit have they opened against me: They have spoken unto me with a lying tongue.
Psalms109:3 They have compassed me about also with words of hatred
Psalms109:4 For my love they are my adversaries: But I give myself unto prayer.
Psalms109:5 And they have rewarded me evil for good
Psalms109:6 Set thou a wicked man over him; And let an adversary stand at his right hand.
Psalms109:7 When he is judged
Psalms109:8 Let his days be few; And let another take his office.
Psalms109:9 Let his children be fatherless
Psalms109:10 Let his children be vagabonds
Psalms109:11 Let the extortioner catch all that he hath; And let strangers make spoil of his labor.
Psalms109:12 Let there be none to extend kindness unto him; Neither let there be any to have pity on his fatherless children.
Psalms109:13 Let his posterity be cut off; In the generation following let their name be blotted out.
Psalms109:14 Let the iniquity of his fathers be remembered with Jehovah; And let not the sin of his mother be blotted out.
Psalms109:15 Let them be before Jehovah continually
Psalms109:16 Because he remembered not to show kindness
Psalms109:17 Yea
Psalms109:18 He clothed himself also with cursing as with his garment
Psalms109:19 Let it be unto him as the raiment wherewith he covereth himself
Psalms109:20 This is the reward of mine adversaries from Jehovah
Psalms109:21 But deal thou with me
Psalms109:22 For I am poor and needy
Psalms109:23 I am gone like the shadow when it declineth: I am tossed up and down as the locust.
Psalms109:24 My knees are weak through fasting; And my flesh faileth of fatness.
Psalms109:25 I am become also a reproach unto them: When they see me
Psalms109:26 Help me
Psalms109:27 That they may know that this is thy hand; That thou
Psalms109:28 Let them curse

Psalms109:31 For he will stand at the right hand of the needy

Psalms118:1 Oh give thanks unto Jehovah; for he is good; For his lovingkindness endureth for ever.

Psalms118:3 Let the house of Aaron now say
Psalms118:4 Let them now that fear Jehovah say
Psalms118:5 Out of my distress I called upon Jehovah: Jehovah answered me and set me in a large place.
Psalms118:6 Jehovah is on my side; I will not fear: What can man do unto me?
Psalms118:7 Jehovah is on my side among them that help me: Therefore shall I see my desire upon them that hate me.
Psalms118:8 It is better to take refuge in Jehovah Than to put confidence in man.
Psalms118:9 It is better to take refuge in Jehovah Than to put confidence in princes.
Psalms118:10 All nations compassed me about: In the name of Jehovah I will cut them off.
Psalms118:11 They compassed me about; yea
Psalms118:12 They compassed me about like bees; They are quenched as the fire of thorns: In the name of Jehovah I will cut them off.
Psalms118:13 Thou didst thrust sore at me that I might fall; But Jehovah helped me.
Psalms118:14 Jehovah is my strength and song; And he is become my salvation.
Psalms118:15 The voice of rejoicing and salvation is in the tents of the righteous: The right hand of Jehovah doeth valiantly.
Psalms118:16 The right hand of Jehovah is exalted: The right hand of Jehovah doeth valiantly.
Psalms118:17 I shall not die
Psalms118:18 Jehovah hath chastened me sore; But he hath not given me over unto death.
Psalms118:19 Open to me the gates of righteousness: I will enter into them
Psalms118:20 This is the gate of Jehovah; The righteous shall enter into it.
Psalms118:21 I will give thanks unto thee; for thou hast answered me

Psalms118:23 This is Jehovahs doing; It is marvellous in our eyes.
Psalms118:24 This is the day which Jehovah hath made; We will rejoice and be glad in it.

Psalms119:1 ALEPH. Blessed are they that are perfect in the way
Psalms119:2 Blessed are they that keep his testimonies
Psalms119:3 Yea
Psalms119:4 Thou hast commanded us thy precepts

Psalms119:6 Then shall I not be put to shame
Psalms119:7 I will give thanks unto thee with uprightness of heart
Psalms119:8 I will observe thy statutes: Oh forsake me not utterly.
Psalms119:9 BETH. Wherewith shall a young man cleanse his way? By taking heed thereto according to thy word.
Psalms119:10 With my whole heart have I sought thee: Oh let me not wander from thy commandments.
Psalms119:11 Thy word have I laid up in my heart
Psalms119:12 Blessed art thou
Psalms119:13 With my lips have I declared All the ordinances of thy mouth.
Psalms119:14 I have rejoiced in the way of thy testimonies
Psalms119:15 I will meditate on thy precepts
Psalms119:16 I will delight myself in thy statutes: I will not forget thy word.
Psalms119:17 GIMEL. Deal bountifully with thy servant
Psalms119:18 Open thou mine eyes
Psalms119:19 I am a sojourner in the earth: Hide not thy commandments from me.
Psalms119:20 My soul breaketh for the longing That it hath unto thine ordinances at all times.
Psalms119:21 Thou hast rebuked the proud that are cursed
Psalms119:22 Take away from me reproach and contempt; For I have kept thy testimonies.
Psalms119:23 Princes also sat and talked against me; But thy servant did meditate on thy statutes.
Psalms119:24 Thy testimonies also are my delight And my counsellors.
Psalms119:25 DALETH. My soul cleaveth unto the dust: Quicken thou me according to thy word.
Psalms119:26 I declared my ways
Psalms119:27 Make me to understand the way of thy precepts: So shall I meditate on thy wondrous works.
Psalms119:28 My soul melteth for heaviness: Strengthen thou me according unto thy word.
Psalms119:29 Remove from me the way of falsehood; And grant me thy law graciously.

Psalms119:32 I will run the way of thy commandments

Psalms119:38 Confirm unto thy servant thy word
Psalms119:39 Turn away my reproach whereof I am afraid; For thine ordinances are good.
Psalms119:40 Behold
