Psalms136:1 Oh give thanks unto Jehovah; for he is good; For his lovingkindness endureth for ever.
Psalms136:2 Oh give thanks unto the God of gods; For his lovingkindness endureth for ever.
Psalms136:3 Oh give thanks unto the Lord of lords; For his lovingkindness endureth for ever:
Psalms136:4 To him who alone doeth great wonders; For his lovingkindness endureth for ever:
Psalms136:5 To him that by understanding made the heavens; For his lovingkindness endureth for ever:
Psalms136:6 To him that spread forth the earth above the waters; For his lovingkindness endureth for ever:
Psalms136:7 To him that made great lights; For his lovingkindness endureth for ever:
Psalms136:8 The sun to rule by day; For his lovingkindness endureth for ever;
Psalms136:9 The moon and stars to rule by night; For his lovingkindness endureth for ever:
Psalms136:10 To him that smote Egypt in their first-born; For his lovingkindness endureth for ever;
Psalms136:11 And brought out Israel from among them; For his lovingkindness endureth for ever;
Psalms136:12 With a strong hand
Psalms136:13 To him that divided the Red Sea in sunder; For his lovingkindness endureth for ever;
Psalms136:14 And made Israel to pass through the midst of it; For his lovingkindness endureth for ever;
Psalms136:15 But overthrew Pharaoh and his host in the Red Sea; For his lovingkindness endureth for ever:
Psalms136:16 To him that led his people through the wilderness; For his lovingkindness endureth for ever:
Psalms136:17 To him that smote great kings; For his lovingkindness endureth for ever;
Psalms136:18 And slew famous kings; For his lovingkindness endureth for ever:
Psalms136:19 Sihon king of the Amorites; For his lovingkindness endureth forever;
Psalms136:20 And Og king of Bashan; For his lovingkindness endureth for ever;
Psalms136:21 And gave their land for a heritage; For his lovingkindness endureth for ever;
Psalms136:22 Even a heritage unto Israel his servant; For his lovingkindness endureth for ever:
Psalms136:23 Who remembered us in our low estate; For his lovingkindness endureth for ever;
Psalms136:24 And hath delivered us from our adversaries; For his lovingkindness endureth for ever:
Psalms136:25 Who giveth food to all flesh; For his lovingkindness endureth for ever.
Psalms136:26 Oh give thanks unto the God of heaven; For his lovingkindness endureth for ever.

Psalms138:2 I will worship toward thy holy temple
Psalms138:3 In the day that I called thou answeredst me
Psalms138:4 All the kings of the earth shall give thee thanks
Psalms138:5 Yea
Psalms138:6 For though Jehovah is high
Psalms138:7 Though I walk in the midst of trouble
Psalms138:8 Jehovah will perfect that which concerneth me: Thy lovingkindness
Psalms139:1 O Jehovah
Psalms139:2 Thou knowest my downsitting and mine uprising; Thou understandest my thought afar off.
Psalms139:3 Thou searchest out my path and my lying down
Psalms139:4 For there is not a word in my tongue
Psalms139:5 Thou hast beset me behind and before
Psalms139:6 Such knowledge is too wonderful for me; It is high
Psalms139:7 Whither shall I go from thy Spirit? Or whither shall I flee from thy presence?
Psalms139:8 If I ascend up into heaven
Psalms139:9 If I take the wings of the morning
Psalms139:10 Even there shall thy hand lead me
Psalms139:11 If I say
Psalms139:12 Even the darkness hideth not from thee
Psalms139:13 For thou didst form my inward parts: Thou didst cover me in my mothers womb.
Psalms139:14 I will give thanks unto thee; for I am fearfully and wonderfully made: Wonderful are thy works; And that my soul knoweth right well.
Psalms139:15 My frame was not hidden from thee
Psalms139:16 Thine eyes did see mine unformed substance; And in thy book they were all written
Psalms139:17 How precious also are thy thoughts unto me
Psalms139:18 If I should count them
Psalms139:19 Surely thou wilt slay the wicked
Psalms139:20 For they speak against thee wickedly
Psalms139:21 Do not I hate them
Psalms139:22 I hate them with perfect hatred: They are become mine enemies.
Psalms139:23 Search me
Psalms139:24 And see if there be any wicked way in me
Psalms140:1 Deliver me
Psalms140:2 Who devise mischiefs in their heart; Continually do they gather themselves together for war.
Psalms140:3 They have sharpened their tongue like a serpent; Adders poison is under their lips. Selah
Psalms140:4 Keep me
Psalms140:5 The proud have hid a snare for me
Psalms140:6 I said unto Jehovah
Psalms140:7 O Jehovah the Lord
Psalms140:8 Grant not
Psalms140:9 As for the head of those that compass me about
Psalms140:10 Let burning coals fall upon them: Let them be cast into the fire
Psalms140:11 An evil speaker shall not be established in the earth: Evil shall hunt the violent man to overthrow him.
Psalms140:12 I know that Jehovah will maintain the cause of the afflicted
Psalms140:13 Surely the righteous shall give thanks unto thy name: The upright shall dwell in thy presence. Psalm 141 A Psalm of David.
Psalms141:1 Jehovah
Psalms141:2 Let my prayer be set forth as incense before thee; The lifting up of my hands as the evening sacrifice.
Psalms141:3 Set a watch
Psalms141:4 Incline not my heart to any evil thing
Psalms141:5 Let the righteous smite me
Psalms141:6 Their judges are thrown down by the sides of the rock; And they shall hear my words; for they are sweet.
Psalms141:7 As when one ploweth and cleaveth the earth
Psalms141:8 For mine eyes are unto thee
Psalms141:9 Keep me from the snare which they have laid for me
Psalms141:10 Let the wicked fall into their own nets
Psalms142:1 I cry with my voice unto Jehovah; With my voice unto Jehovah do I make supplication.
Psalms142:2 I pour out my complaint before him; I show before him my trouble.
Psalms142:3 When my spirit was overwhelmed within me
Psalms142:4 Look on my right hand
Psalms142:5 I cried unto thee
Psalms142:6 Attend unto my cry; For I am brought very low: Deliver me from my persecutors; For they are stronger than I.
Psalms142:7 Bring my soul out of prison
Psalms143:1 Hear my prayer
Psalms143:2 And enter not into judgment with thy servant; For in thy sight no man living is righteous.
Psalms143:3 For the enemy hath persecuted my soul; He hath smitten my life down to the ground: He hath made me to dwell in dark places
Psalms143:4 Therefore is my spirit overwhelmed within me; My heart within me is desolate.
Psalms143:5 I remember the days of old; I meditate on all thy doings; I muse on the work of thy hands.
Psalms143:6 I spread forth my hands unto thee: My soul thirsteth after thee
Psalms143:7 Make haste to answer me
Psalms143:8 Cause me to hear thy lovingkindness in the morning; For in thee do I trust: Cause me to know the way wherein I should walk; For I lift up my soul unto thee.
Psalms143:9 Deliver me
Psalms143:10 Teach me to do thy will; For thou art my God: Thy Spirit is good; Lead me in the land of uprightness.
Psalms143:11 Quicken me
Psalms143:12 And in thy lovingkindness cut off mine enemies
Psalms144:1 Blessed be Jehovah my rock
Psalms144:2 My lovingkindness
Psalms144:3 Jehovah
Psalms144:4 Man is like to vanity: His days are as a shadow that passeth away.
Psalms144:5 Bow thy heavens
Psalms144:6 Cast forth lightning
Psalms144:7 Stretch forth thy hand from above; Rescue me
Psalms144:8 Whose mouth speaketh deceit
Psalms144:9 I will sing a new song unto thee
Psalms144:10 Thou art he that giveth salvation unto kings; Who rescueth David his servant from the hurtful sword.
Psalms144:11 Rescue me
Psalms144:12 When our sons shall be as plants grown up in their youth
Psalms144:13 When our garners are full
Psalms144:14 When our oxen are well laden; When there is no breaking in
Psalms144:15 Happy is the people that is in such a case; Yea
Psalms145:1 I will extol thee
Psalms145:2 Every day will I bless thee; And I will praise thy name for ever and ever.
Psalms145:3 Great is Jehovah
Psalms145:4 One generation shall laud thy works to another
Psalms145:5 Of the glorious majesty of thine honor
Psalms145:6 And men shall speak of the might of thy terrible acts; And I will declare thy greatness.
Psalms145:7 They shall utter the memory of thy great goodness
Psalms145:8 Jehovah is gracious
Psalms145:9 Jehovah is good to all; And his tender mercies are over all his works.
Psalms145:10 All thy works shall give thanks unto thee
Psalms145:11 They shall speak of the glory of thy kingdom
Psalms145:12 To make known to the sons of men his mighty acts
Psalms145:13 Thy kingdom is an everlasting kingdom
Psalms145:14 Jehovah upholdeth all that fall
Psalms145:15 The eyes of all wait for thee; And thou givest them their food in due season.
Psalms145:16 Thou openest thy hand
Psalms145:17 Jehovah is righteous in all his ways
Psalms145:18 Jehovah is nigh unto all them that call upon him
Psalms145:19 He will fulfil the desire of them that fear him; He also will hear their cry and will save them.
Psalms145:20 Jehovah preserveth all them that love him; But all the wicked will he destroy.
Psalms145:21 My mouth shall speak the praise of Jehovah; And let all flesh bless his holy name for ever and ever.
Psalms146:1 Praise ye Jehovah. Praise Jehovah
Psalms146:2 While I live will I praise Jehovah: I will sing praises unto my God while I have any being.
Psalms146:3 Put not your trust in princes
Psalms146:4 His breath goeth forth
Psalms146:5 Happy is he that hath the God of Jacob for his help
Psalms146:6 Who made heaven and earth
Psalms146:7 Who executeth justice for the oppressed; Who giveth food to the hungry. Jehovah looseth the prisoners;
Psalms146:8 Jehovah openeth the eyes of the blind; Jehovah raiseth up them that are bowed down; Jehovah loveth the righteous;
Psalms146:9 Jehovah preserveth the sojourners; He upholdeth the fatherless and widow; But the way of the wicked he turneth upside down.
Psalms146:10 Jehovah will reign for ever
Psalms147:1 Praise ye Jehovah; For it is good to sing praises unto our God; For it is pleasant
Psalms147:2 Jehovah doth build up Jerusalem; He gathereth together the outcasts of Israel.
Psalms147:3 He healeth the broken in heart
Psalms147:4 He counteth the number of the stars; He calleth them all by their names.
Psalms147:5 Great is our Lord
Psalms147:6 Jehovah upholdeth the meek: He bringeth the wicked down to the ground.
Psalms147:7 Sing unto Jehovah with thanksgiving; Sing praises upon the harp unto our God
Psalms147:8 Who covereth the heavens with clouds
Psalms147:9 He giveth to the beast his food
Psalms147:10 He delighteth not in the strength of the horse: He taketh no pleasure in the legs of a man.
Psalms147:11 Jehovah taketh pleasure in them that fear him
Psalms147:12 Praise Jehovah
Psalms147:13 For he hath strengthened the bars of thy gates; He hath blessed thy children within thee.
Psalms147:14 He maketh peace in thy borders; He filleth thee with the finest of the wheat.
Psalms147:15 He sendeth out his commandment upon earth; His word runneth very swiftly.
Psalms147:16 He giveth snow like wool; He scattereth the hoar-frost like ashes.
Psalms147:17 He casteth forth his ice like morsels: Who can stand before his cold?
Psalms147:18 He sendeth out his word
Psalms147:19 He showeth his word unto Jacob
Psalms147:20 He hath not dealt so with any nation; And as for his ordinances

Psalms148:4 Praise him
Psalms148:5 Let them praise the name of Jehovah; For he commanded
Psalms148:6 He hath also established them for ever and ever: He hath made a decree which shall not pass away.
Psalms148:7 Praise Jehovah from the earth
Psalms148:8 Fire and hail
Psalms148:9 Mountains and all hills; Fruitful trees and all cedars;
Psalms148:10 Beasts and all cattle; Creeping things and flying birds;
Psalms148:11 Kings of the earth and all peoples; Princes and all judges of the earth;
Psalms148:12 Both young men and virgins; Old men and children:
Psalms148:13 Let them praise the name of Jehovah; For his name alone is exalted; His glory is above the earth and the heavens.
Psalms148:14 And he hath lifted up the horn of his people
Psalms149:1 Praise ye Jehovah. Sing unto Jehovah a new song
Psalms149:2 Let Israel rejoice in him that made him: Let the children of Zion be joyful in their King.
Psalms149:3 Let them praise his name in the dance: Let them sing praises unto him with timbrel and harp.
Psalms149:4 For Jehovah taketh pleasure in his people: He will beautify the meek with salvation.
Psalms149:5 Let the saints exult in glory: Let them sing for joy upon their beds.
Psalms149:6 Let the high praises of God be in their mouth
Psalms149:7 To execute vengeance upon the nations
Psalms149:8 To bind their kings with chains
Psalms149:9 To execute upon them the judgment written: This honor have all his saints. Praise ye Jehovah.
Psalms150:1 Praise ye Jehovah. Praise God in his sanctuary: Praise him in the firmament of his power.
Psalms150:2 Praise him for his mighty acts: Praise him according to his excellent greatness.
Psalms150:3 Praise him with trumpet sound: Praise him with psaltery and harp.
Psalms150:4 Praise him with timbrel and dance: Praise him with stringed instruments and pipe.
Psalms150:5 Praise him with loud cymbals: Praise him with high sounding cymbals.
Psalms150:6 Let everything that hath breath praise Jehovah. Praise ye Jehovah.
Proverbs1:1 The proverbs of Solomon the son of David
Proverbs1:2 To know wisdom and instruction; To discern the words of understanding;
Proverbs1:3 To receive instruction in wise dealing
Proverbs1:4 To give prudence to the simple
Proverbs1:5 That the wise man may hear
Proverbs1:6 To understand a proverb
Proverbs1:7 The fear of Jehovah is the beginning of knowledge; But the foolish despise wisdom and instruction.
Proverbs1:8 My son
Proverbs1:9 For they shall be a chaplet of grace unto thy head
Proverbs1:10 My son
Proverbs1:11 If they say
Proverbs1:12 Let us swallow them up alive as Sheol
Proverbs1:13 We shall find all precious substance; We shall fill our houses with spoil;
Proverbs1:14 Thou shalt cast thy lot among us; We will all have one purse:
Proverbs1:15 My son
Proverbs1:16 For their feet run to evil
Proverbs1:17 For in vain is the net spread In the sight of any bird:
Proverbs1:18 And these lay wait for their own blood; They lurk privily for their own lives.
Proverbs1:19 So are the ways of every one that is greedy of gain; It taketh away the life of the owners thereof.
Proverbs1:20 Wisdom crieth aloud in the street; She uttereth her voice in the broad places;
Proverbs1:21 She crieth in the chief place of concourse; At the entrance of the gates
Proverbs1:22 How long
Proverbs1:23 Turn you at my reproof: Behold
Proverbs1:24 Because I have called
Proverbs1:25 But ye have set at nought all my counsel
Proverbs1:26 I also will laugh in the day of your calamity; I will mock when your fear cometh;
Proverbs1:27 When your fear cometh as a storm
Proverbs1:28 Then will they call upon me
Proverbs1:29 For that they hated knowledge
Proverbs1:30 They would none of my counsel; They despised all my reproof.
Proverbs1:31 Therefore shall they eat of the fruit of their own way
Proverbs1:32 For the backsliding of the simple shall slay them
Proverbs1:33 But whoso hearkeneth unto me shall dwell securely
Proverbs2:1 My son
Proverbs2:2 So as to incline thine ear unto wisdom
Proverbs2:3 Yea
Proverbs2:4 If thou seek her as silver
Proverbs2:5 Then shalt thou understand the fear of Jehovah
Proverbs2:6 For Jehovah giveth wisdom; Out of his mouth cometh knowledge and understanding:
Proverbs2:7 He layeth up sound wisdom for the upright; He is a shield to them that walk in integrity;
Proverbs2:8 That he may guard the paths of justice
Proverbs2:9 Then shalt thou understand righteousness and justice
Proverbs2:10 For wisdom shall enter into thy heart
Proverbs2:11 Discretion shall watch over thee; Understanding shall keep thee:
Proverbs2:12 To deliver thee from the way of evil
Proverbs2:13 Who forsake the paths of uprightness
Proverbs2:14 Who rejoice to do evil
Proverbs2:15 Who are crooked in their ways
Proverbs2:16 To deliver thee from the strange woman
Proverbs2:17 That forsaketh the friend of her youth
Proverbs2:18 For her house inclineth unto death
Proverbs2:19 None that go unto her return again
Proverbs2:20 That thou mayest walk in the way of good men
Proverbs2:21 For the upright shall dwell in the land
Proverbs2:22 But the wicked shall be cut off from the land
Proverbs3:1 My son
Proverbs3:2 For length of days
Proverbs3:3 Let not kindness and truth forsake thee: Bind them about thy neck; Write them upon the tablet of thy heart:
Proverbs3:4 So shalt thou find favor and good understanding In the sight of God and man.
Proverbs3:5 Trust in Jehovah with all thy heart
Proverbs3:6 In all thy ways acknowledge him
Proverbs3:7 Be not wise in thine own eyes; Fear Jehovah
Proverbs3:8 It will be health to thy navel
Proverbs3:9 Honor Jehovah with thy substance
Proverbs3:10 So shall thy barns be filled with plenty
Proverbs3:11 My son
Proverbs3:12 For whom Jehovah loveth he reproveth; Even as a father the son in whom he delighteth.
Proverbs3:13 Happy is the man that findeth wisdom
Proverbs3:14 For the gaining of it is better than the gaining of silver
Proverbs3:15 She is more precious than rubies: And none of the things thou canst desire are to be compared unto her.
Proverbs3:16 Length of days is in her right hand; In her left hand are riches and honor.
Proverbs3:17 Her ways are ways of pleasantness
Proverbs3:18 She is a tree of life to them that lay hold upon her: And happy is every one that retaineth her.
Proverbs3:19 Jehovah by wisdom founded the earth; By understanding he established the heavens.
Proverbs3:20 By his knowledge the depths were broken up
Proverbs3:21 My son
Proverbs3:22 So shall they be life unto thy soul
Proverbs3:23 Then shalt thou walk in thy way securely
Proverbs3:24 When thou liest down
Proverbs3:25 Be not afraid of sudden fear
Proverbs3:26 For Jehovah will be thy confidence
Proverbs3:27 Withhold not good from them to whom it is due
Proverbs3:28 Say not unto thy neighbor
Proverbs3:29 Devise not evil against thy neighbor
Proverbs3:30 Strive not with a man without cause
Proverbs3:31 Envy thou not the man of violence
Proverbs3:32 For the perverse is an abomination to Jehovah; But his friendship is with the upright.
Proverbs3:33 The curse of Jehovah is in the house of the wicked; But he blesseth the habitation of the righteous.
Proverbs3:34 Surely he scoffeth at the scoffers; But he giveth grace unto the lowly.
Proverbs3:35 The wise shall inherit glory; But shame shall be the promotion of fools.
Proverbs4:1 Hear
Proverbs4:2 For I give you good doctrine; Forsake ye not my law.
Proverbs4:3 For I was a son unto my father
Proverbs4:4 And he taught me
Proverbs4:5 Get wisdom
Proverbs4:6 Forsake her not
Proverbs4:7 Wisdom is the principal thing; therefore get wisdom; Yea
Proverbs4:8 Exalt her
Proverbs4:9 She will give to thy head a chaplet of grace; A crown of beauty will she deliver to thee.
Proverbs4:10 Hear
Proverbs4:11 I have taught thee in the way of wisdom; I have led thee in paths of uprightness.
Proverbs4:12 When thou goest
Proverbs4:13 Take fast hold of instruction; let her not go: Keep her; for she is thy life.
Proverbs4:14 Enter not into the path of the wicked
Proverbs4:15 Avoid it
Proverbs4:16 For they sleep not
Proverbs4:17 For they eat the bread of wickedness
Proverbs4:18 But the path of the righteous is as the dawning light
Proverbs4:19 The way of the wicked is as darkness: They know not at what they stumble.
Proverbs4:20 My son
Proverbs4:21 Let them not depart from thine eyes; Keep them in the midst of thy heart.
Proverbs4:22 For they are life unto those that find them
Proverbs4:23 Keep thy heart with all diligence; For out of it are the issues of life.
Proverbs4:24 Put away from thee a wayward mouth
Proverbs4:25 Let thine eyes look right on
Proverbs4:26 Make level the path of thy feet
Proverbs4:27 Turn not to the right hand nor to the left: Remove thy foot from evil.
Proverbs5:1 My son
Proverbs5:2 That thou mayest preserve discretion
Proverbs5:3 For the lips of a strange woman drop honey
Proverbs5:4 But in the end she is bitter as wormwood
Proverbs5:5 Her feet go down to death; Her steps take hold on Sheol;
Proverbs5:6 So that she findeth not the level path of life: Her ways are unstable
Proverbs5:7 Now therefore
Proverbs5:8 Remove thy way far from her
Proverbs5:9 Lest thou give thine honor unto others
Proverbs5:10 Lest strangers be filled with thy strength
Proverbs5:11 And thou mourn at thy latter end
Proverbs5:12 And say
Proverbs5:13 Neither have I obeyed the voice of my teachers
Proverbs5:14 I was well-nigh in all evil In the midst of the assembly and congregation.
Proverbs5:15 Drink waters out of thine own cistern
Proverbs5:16 Should thy springs be dispersed abroad
Proverbs5:17 Let them be for thyself alone
Proverbs5:18 Let thy fountain be blessed; And rejoice in the wife of thy youth.
Proverbs5:19 As a loving hind and a pleasant doe
Proverbs5:20 For why shouldest thou
Proverbs5:21 For the ways of man are before the eyes of Jehovah; And he maketh level all his paths.
Proverbs5:22 His own iniquities shall take the wicked
Proverbs5:23 He shall die for lack of instruction; And in the greatness of his folly he shall go astray.
Proverbs6:1 My son
Proverbs6:2 Thou art snared with the words of thy mouth
Proverbs6:3 Do this now
Proverbs6:4 Give not sleep to thine eyes
Proverbs6:5 Deliver thyself as a roe from the hand of the hunter
Proverbs6:6 Go to the ant
Proverbs6:7 Which having no chief
Proverbs6:8 Provideth her bread in the summer
Proverbs6:9 How long wilt thou sleep
Proverbs6:10 Yet a little sleep
Proverbs6:11 So shall thy poverty come as a robber
Proverbs6:12 A worthless person
Proverbs6:13 That winketh with his eyes
Proverbs6:14 In whose heart is perverseness
Proverbs6:15 Therefore shall his calamity come suddenly; On a sudden shall he be broken
Proverbs6:16 There are six things which Jehovah hateth; Yea
Proverbs6:17 Haughty eyes
Proverbs6:18 A heart that deviseth wicked purposes
Proverbs6:19 A false witness that uttereth lies
Proverbs6:20 My son
Proverbs6:21 Bind them continually upon thy heart; Tie them about thy neck.
Proverbs6:22 When thou walkest
Proverbs6:23 For the commandment is a lamp; and the law is light; And reproofs of instruction are the way of life:
Proverbs6:24 To keep thee from the evil woman
Proverbs6:25 Lust not after her beauty in thy heart; Neither let her take thee with her eyelids.
Proverbs6:26 For on account of a harlot a man is brought to a piece of bread; And the adulteress hunteth for the precious life.
Proverbs6:27 Can a man take fire in his bosom
Proverbs6:28 Or can one walk upon hot coals
Proverbs6:29 So he that goeth in to his neighbors wife; Whosoever toucheth her shall not be unpunished.
Proverbs6:30 Men do not despise a thief
Proverbs6:31 But if he be found
Proverbs6:32 He that committeth adultery with a woman is void of understanding: He doeth it who would destroy his own soul.
Proverbs6:33 Wounds and dishonor shall he get; And his reproach shall not be wiped away.
Proverbs6:34 For jealousy is the rage of a man; And he will not spare in the day of vengeance.
Proverbs6:35 He will not regard any ransom; Neither will he rest content
Proverbs7:1 My son
Proverbs7:2 Keep my commandments and live; And my law as the apple of thine eye.
Proverbs7:3 Bind them upon thy fingers; Write them upon the tablet of thy heart.
Proverbs7:4 Say unto wisdom
Proverbs7:5 That they may keep thee from the strange woman
Proverbs7:6 For at the window of my house I looked forth through my lattice;
Proverbs7:7 And I beheld among the simple ones
Proverbs7:8 Passing through the street near her corner; And he went the way to her house
Proverbs7:9 In the twilight
Proverbs7:10 And
Proverbs7:11 She is clamorous and wilful; Her feet abide not in her house:
Proverbs7:12 Now she is in the streets
Proverbs7:13 So she caught him
Proverbs7:14 Sacrifices of peace-offerings are with me; This day have I paid my vows.
Proverbs7:15 Therefore came I forth to meet thee
Proverbs7:16 I have spread my couch with carpets of tapestry
Proverbs7:17 I have perfumed my bed With myrrh
Proverbs7:18 Come
Proverbs7:19 For the man is not at home; He is gone a long journey:
Proverbs7:20 He hath taken a bag of money with him; He will come home at the full moon.
Proverbs7:21 With her much fair speech she causeth him to yield; With the flattering of her lips she forceth him along.
Proverbs7:22 He goeth after her straightway
Proverbs7:23 Till an arrow strike through his liver; As a bird hasteth to the snare
Proverbs7:24 Now therefore
Proverbs7:25 Let not thy heart decline to her ways; Go not astray in her paths.
Proverbs7:26 For she hath cast down many wounded: Yea
Proverbs7:27 Her house is the way to Sheol
Proverbs8:1 Doth not wisdom cry
Proverbs8:2 On the top of high places by the way
Proverbs8:3 Beside the gates
Proverbs8:4 Unto you
Proverbs8:5 O ye simple
Proverbs8:6 Hear
Proverbs8:7 For my mouth shall utter truth; And wickedness is an abomination to my lips.
Proverbs8:8 All the words of my mouth are in righteousness; There is nothing crooked or perverse in them.
Proverbs8:9 They are all plain to him that understandeth
Proverbs8:10 Receive my instruction
Proverbs8:11 For wisdom is better than rubies; And all the things that may be desired are not to be compared unto it.
Proverbs8:12 I wisdom have made prudence my dwelling
Proverbs8:13 The fear of Jehovah is to hate evil: Pride
Proverbs8:14 Counsel is mine
Proverbs8:15 By me kings reign
Proverbs8:16 By me princes rule
Proverbs8:17 I love them that love me; And those that seek me diligently shall find me.
Proverbs8:18 Riches and honor are with me; Yea
Proverbs8:19 My fruit is better than gold
Proverbs8:20 I walk in the way of righteousness
Proverbs8:21 That I may cause those that love me to inherit substance
Proverbs8:22 Jehovah possessed me in the beginning of his way
Proverbs8:23 I was set up from everlasting
Proverbs8:24 When there were no depths
Proverbs8:25 Before the mountains were settled
Proverbs8:26 While as yet he had not made the earth
Proverbs8:27 When he established the heavens
Proverbs8:28 When he made firm the skies above
Proverbs8:29 When he gave to the sea its bound
Proverbs8:30 Then I was by him
Proverbs8:31 Rejoicing in his habitable earth; And my delight was with the sons of men.
Proverbs8:32 Now therefore
Proverbs8:33 Hear instruction
Proverbs8:34 Blessed is the man that heareth me
Proverbs8:35 For whoso findeth me findeth life
Proverbs8:36 But he that sinneth against me wrongeth his own soul: All they that hate me love death.
Proverbs9:1 Wisdom hath builded her house; She hath hewn out her seven pillars:
Proverbs9:2 She hath killed her beasts; She hath mingled her wine; She hath also furnished her table:
Proverbs9:3 She hath sent forth her maidens; She crieth upon the highest places of the city:
Proverbs9:4 Whoso is simple
Proverbs9:5 Come
Proverbs9:6 Leave off
Proverbs9:7 He that correcteth a scoffer getteth to himself reviling; And he that reproveth a wicked man getteth himself a blot.
Proverbs9:8 Reprove not a scoffer
Proverbs9:9 Give instruction to a wise man
Proverbs9:10 The fear of Jehovah is the beginning of wisdom; And the knowledge of the Holy One is understanding.
Proverbs9:11 For by me thy days shall be multiplied
Proverbs9:12 If thou art wise
Proverbs9:13 The foolish woman is clamorous; She is simple
Proverbs9:14 And she sitteth at the door of her house
Proverbs9:15 To call to them that pass by
Proverbs9:16 Whoso is simple
Proverbs9:17 Stolen waters are sweet
Proverbs9:18 But he knoweth not that the dead are there; That her guests are in the depths of Sheol.
Proverbs10:1 The proverbs of Solomon. A wise son maketh a glad father; But a foolish son is the heaviness of his mother.
Proverbs10:2 Treasures of wickedness profit nothing; But righteousness delivereth from death.
Proverbs10:3 Jehovah will not suffer the soul of the righteous to famish; But he thrusteth away the desire of the wicked.
Proverbs10:4 He becometh poor that worketh with a slack hand; But the hand of the diligent maketh rich.
Proverbs10:5 He that gathereth in summer is a wise son; But he that sleepeth in harvest is a son that causeth shame.
Proverbs10:6 Blessings are upon the head of the righteous; But violence covereth the mouth of the wicked.
Proverbs10:7 The memory of the righteous is blessed; But the name of the wicked shall rot.
Proverbs10:8 The wise in heart will receive commandments; But a prating fool shall fall.
Proverbs10:9 He that walketh uprightly walketh surely; But he that perverteth his ways shall be known.
Proverbs10:10 He that winketh with the eye causeth sorrow; But a prating fool shall fall.
Proverbs10:11 The mouth of the righteous is a fountain of life; But violence covereth the mouth of the wicked.
Proverbs10:12 Hatred stirreth up strifes; But love covereth all transgressions.
Proverbs10:13 In the lips of him that hath discernment wisdom is found; But a rod is for the back of him that is void of understanding.
Proverbs10:14 Wise men lay up knowledge; But the mouth of the foolish is a present destruction.
Proverbs10:15 The rich mans wealth is his strong city: The destruction of the poor is their poverty.
Proverbs10:16 The labor of the righteous tendeth to life; The increase of the wicked
Proverbs10:17 He is in the way of life that heedeth correction; But he that forsaketh reproof erreth.
Proverbs10:18 He that hideth hatred is of lying lips; And he that uttereth a slander is a fool.
Proverbs10:19 In the multitude of words there wanteth not transgression; But he that refraineth his lips doeth wisely.
Proverbs10:20 The tongue of the righteous is as choice silver: The heart of the wicked is little worth.
Proverbs10:21 The lips of the righteous feed many; But the foolish die for lack of understanding.
Proverbs10:22 The blessing of Jehovah
Proverbs10:23 It is as sport to a fool to do wickedness; And so is wisdom to a man of understanding.
Proverbs10:24 The fear of the wicked
Proverbs10:25 When the whirlwind passeth
Proverbs10:26 As vinegar to the teeth
Proverbs10:27 The fear of Jehovah prolongeth days; But the years of the wicked shall be shortened.
Proverbs10:28 The hope of the righteous shall be gladness; But the expectation of the wicked shall perish.
Proverbs10:29 The way of Jehovah is a stronghold to the upright; But it is a destruction to the workers of iniquity.
Proverbs10:30 The righteous shall never be removed; But the wicked shall not dwell in the land.
Proverbs10:31 The mouth of the righteous bringeth forth wisdom; But the perverse tongue shall be cut off.
Proverbs10:32 The lips of the righteous know what is acceptable; But the mouth of the wicked speaketh perverseness.

Proverbs11:12 He that despiseth his neighbor is void of wisdom; But a man of understanding holdeth his peace.
Proverbs11:13 He that goeth about as a tale-bearer revealeth secrets; But he that is of a faithful spirit concealeth a matter.
Proverbs11:14 Where no wise guidance is
Proverbs11:15 He that is surety for a stranger shall smart for it; But he that hateth suretyship is secure.
Proverbs11:16 A gracious woman obtaineth honor; And violent men obtain riches.
Proverbs11:17 The merciful man doeth good to his own soul; But he that is cruel troubleth his own flesh.
Proverbs11:18 The wicked earneth deceitful wages; But he that soweth righteousness hath a sure reward.
Proverbs11:19 He that is stedfast in righteousness shall attain unto life; And he that pursueth evil doeth it to his own death.
Proverbs11:20 They that are perverse in heart are an abomination to Jehovah; But such as are perfect in their way are his delight.
Proverbs11:21 Though hand join in hand
Proverbs11:22 As a ring of gold in a swines snout
Proverbs11:23 The desire of the righteous is only good; But the expectation of the wicked is wrath.

Proverbs12:1 Whoso loveth correction loveth knowledge; But he that hateth reproof is brutish.
Proverbs12:2 A good man shall obtain favor of Jehovah; But a man of wicked devices will he condemn.
Proverbs12:3 A man shall not be established by wickedness; But the root of the righteous shall not be moved.
Proverbs12:4 A worthy woman is the crown of her husband; But she that maketh ashamed is as rottenness in his bones.
Proverbs12:5 The thoughts of the righteous are just; But the counsels of the wicked are deceit.
Proverbs12:6 The words of the wicked are of lying in wait for blood; But the mouth of the upright shall deliver them.
Proverbs12:7 The wicked are overthrown
Proverbs12:8 A man shall be commended according to his wisdom; But he that is of a perverse heart shall be despised.
Proverbs12:9 Better is he that is lightly esteemed
Proverbs12:10 A righteous man regardeth the life of his beast; But the tender mercies of the wicked are cruel.
Proverbs12:11 He that tilleth his land shall have plenty of bread; But he that followeth after vain persons is void of understanding.
Proverbs12:12 The wicked desireth the net of evil men; But the root of the righteous yieldeth fruit.
Proverbs12:13 In the transgression of the lips is a snare to the evil man; But the righteous shall come out of trouble.
Proverbs12:14 A man shall be satisfied with good by the fruit of his mouth; And the doings of a mans hands shall be rendered unto him.
Proverbs12:15 The way of a fool is right in his own eyes; But he that is wise hearkeneth unto counsel.
Proverbs12:16 A fools vexation is presently known; But a prudent man concealeth shame.
Proverbs12:17 He that uttereth truth showeth forth righteousness; But a false witness
Proverbs12:18 There is that speaketh rashly like the piercings of a sword; But the tongue of the wise is health.
Proverbs12:19 The lip of truth shall be established for ever; But a lying tongue is but for a moment.
Proverbs12:20 Deceit is in the heart of them that devise evil; But to the counsellors of peace is joy.
Proverbs12:21 There shall no mischief happen to the righteous; But the wicked shall be filled with evil.
Proverbs12:22 Lying lips are an abomination to Jehovah; But they that deal truly are his delight.
Proverbs12:23 A prudent man concealeth knowledge; But the heart of fools proclaimeth foolishness.
Proverbs12:24 The hand of the diligent shall bear rule; But the slothful shall be put under taskwork.
Proverbs12:25 Heaviness in the heart of a man maketh it stoop; But a good word maketh it glad.
Proverbs12:26 The righteous is a guide to his neighbor; But the way of the wicked causeth them to err.
Proverbs12:27 The slothful man roasteth not that which he took in hunting; But the precious substance of men is to the diligent.
Proverbs12:28 In the way of righteousness is life; And in the pathway thereof there is no death.

Proverbs20:26 A wise king winnoweth the wicked

Proverbs20:28 Kindness and truth preserve the king; And his throne is upholden by kindness.
Proverbs20:29 The glory of young men is their strength; And the beauty of old men is the hoary head.
Proverbs20:30 Stripes that wound cleanse away evil; And strokes reach the innermost parts.
Proverbs21:1 The kings heart is in the hand of Jehovah as the watercourses: He turneth it whithersoever he will.
Proverbs21:2 Every way of a man is right in his own eyes; But Jehovah weigheth the hearts.
Proverbs21:3 To do righteousness and justice Is more acceptable to Jehovah than sacrifice.
Proverbs21:4 A high look
Proverbs21:5 The thoughts of the diligent tend only to plenteousness; But every one that is hasty hasteth only to want.
Proverbs21:6 The getting of treasures by a lying tongue Is a vapor driven to and fro by them that seek death.
Proverbs21:7 The violence of the wicked shall sweep them away
Proverbs21:8 The way of him that is laden with guilt is exceeding crooked; But as for the pure
Proverbs21:9 It is better to dwell in the corner of the housetop
Proverbs21:10 The soul of the wicked desireth evil: His neighbor findeth no favor in his eyes.
Proverbs21:11 When the scoffer is punished
Proverbs21:12 The righteous man considereth the house of the wicked
Proverbs21:13 Whoso stoppeth his ears at the cry of the poor
Proverbs21:14 A gift in secret pacifieth anger; And a present in the bosom
Proverbs21:15 It is joy to the righteous to do justice; But it is a destruction to the workers of iniquity.
Proverbs21:16 The man that wandereth out of the way of understanding Shall rest in the assembly of the dead.
Proverbs21:17 He that loveth pleasure shall be a poor man: He that loveth wine and oil shall not be rich.
Proverbs21:18 The wicked is a ransom for the righteous; And the treacherous cometh in the stead of the upright.
Proverbs21:19 It is better to dwell in a desert land
Proverbs21:20 There is precious treasure and oil in the dwelling of the wise; But a foolish man swalloweth it up.
Proverbs21:21 He that followeth after righteousness and kindness Findeth life
Proverbs21:22 A wise man scaleth the city of the mighty
Proverbs21:23 Whoso keepeth his mouth and his tongue Keepeth his soul from troubles.
Proverbs21:24 The proud and haughty man
Proverbs21:25 The desire of the sluggard killeth him; For his hands refuse to labor.
Proverbs21:26 There is that coveteth greedily all the day long; But the righteous giveth and withholdeth not.
Proverbs21:27 The sacrifice of the wicked is an abomination: How much more
Proverbs21:28 A false witness shall perish; But the man that heareth shall speak so as to endure.
Proverbs21:29 A wicked man hardeneth his face; But as for the upright
Proverbs21:30 There is no wisdom nor understanding Nor counsel against Jehovah.
Proverbs21:31 The horse is prepared against the day of battle; But victory is of Jehovah.

Proverbs28:1 The wicked flee when no man pursueth; But the righteous are bold as a lion.
Proverbs28:2 For the transgression of a land many are the princes thereof; But by men of understanding and knowledge the state thereof shall be prolonged.
Proverbs28:3 A needy man that oppresseth the poor Is like a sweeping rain which leaveth no food.
Proverbs28:4 They that forsake the law praise the wicked; But such as keep the law contend with them.
Proverbs28:5 Evil men understand not justice; But they that seek Jehovah understand all things.
Proverbs28:6 Better is the poor that walketh in his integrity
Proverbs28:7 Whoso keepeth the law is a wise son; But he that is a companion of gluttons shameth his father.
Proverbs28:8 He that augmenteth his substance by interest and increase
Proverbs28:9 He that turneth away his ear from hearing the law
Proverbs28:10 Whoso causeth the upright to go astray in an evil way
Proverbs28:11 The rich man is wise in his own conceit; But the poor that hath understanding searcheth him out.
Proverbs28:12 When the righteous triumph
Proverbs28:13 He that covereth his transgressions shall not prosper: But whoso confesseth and forsaketh them shall obtain mercy.
Proverbs28:14 Happy is the man that feareth alway; But he that hardeneth his heart shall fall into mischief.
Proverbs28:15 As a roaring lion
Proverbs28:16 The prince that lacketh understanding is also a great oppressor; But he that hateth covetousness shall prolong his days.
Proverbs28:17 A man that is laden with the blood of any person Shall flee unto the pit; let no man stay him.
Proverbs28:18 Whoso walketh uprightly shall be delivered; But he that is perverse in his ways shall fall at once.
Proverbs28:19 He that tilleth his land shall have plenty of bread; But he that followeth after vain persons shall have poverty enough.
Proverbs28:20 A faithful man shall abound with blessings; But he that maketh haste to be rich shall not be unpunished.
Proverbs28:21 To have respect of persons is not good; Neither that a man should transgress for a piece of bread.
Proverbs28:22 he that hath an evil eye hasteth after riches
Proverbs28:23 He that rebuketh a man shall afterward find more favor Than he that flattereth with the tongue.
Proverbs28:24 Whoso robbeth his father or his mother
Proverbs28:25 He that is of a greedy spirit stirreth up strife; But he that putteth his trust in Jehovah shall be made fat.
Proverbs28:26 He that trusteth in his own heart is a fool; But whoso walketh wisely
Proverbs28:27 He that giveth unto the poor shall not lack; But he that hideth his eyes shall have many a curse.
Proverbs28:28 When the wicked rise
Proverbs29:1 He that being often reproved hardeneth his neck Shall suddenly be destroyed
Proverbs29:2 When the righteous are increased
Proverbs29:3 Whoso loveth wisdom rejoiceth his father; But he that keepeth company with harlots wasteth his substance.
Proverbs29:4 The king by justice establisheth the land; But he that exacteth gifts overthroweth it.
Proverbs29:5 A man that flattereth his neighbor Spreadeth a net for his steps.
Proverbs29:6 In the transgression of an evil man there is a snare; But the righteous doth sing and rejoice.
Proverbs29:7 The righteous taketh knowledge of the cause of the poor; The wicked hath not understanding to know it.
Proverbs29:8 Scoffers set a city in a flame; But wise men turn away wrath.
Proverbs29:9 If a wise man hath a controversy with a foolish man
Proverbs29:10 The bloodthirsty hate him that is perfect; And as for the upright
Proverbs29:11 A fool uttereth all his anger; But a wise man keepeth it back and stilleth it.
Proverbs29:12 If a ruler hearkeneth to falsehood
Proverbs29:13 The poor man and the oppressor meet together; Jehovah lighteneth the eyes of them both.
Proverbs29:14 The king that faithfully judgeth the poor
Proverbs29:15 The rod and reproof give wisdom; But a child left to himself causeth shame to his mother.
Proverbs29:16 When the wicked are increased
Proverbs29:17 Correct thy son
Proverbs29:18 Where there is no vision
Proverbs29:19 A servant will not be corrected by words; For though he understand
Proverbs29:20 Seest thou a man that is hasty in his words? There is more hope of a fool than of him.
Proverbs29:21 He that delicately bringeth up his servant from a child Shall have him become a son at the last.
Proverbs29:22 An angry man stirreth up strife
Proverbs29:23 A mans pride shall bring him low; But he that is of a lowly spirit shall obtain honor.
Proverbs29:24 Whoso is partner with a thief hateth his own soul; He heareth the adjuration and uttereth nothing.
Proverbs29:25 The fear of man bringeth a snare; But whoso putteth his trust in Jehovah shall be safe.
Proverbs29:26 Many seek the rulers favor; But a mans judgment cometh from Jehovah.
Proverbs29:27 An unjust man is an abomination to the righteous; And he that is upright in the way is an abomination to the wicked.

Proverbs31:9 Open thy mouth
Proverbs31:10 A worthy woman who can find? For her price is far above rubies.
Proverbs31:11 The heart of her husband trusteth in her
Proverbs31:12 She doeth him good and not evil All the days of her life.
Proverbs31:13 She seeketh wool and flax
Proverbs31:14 She is like the merchant-ships; She bringeth her bread from afar.
Proverbs31:15 She riseth also while it is yet night
Proverbs31:16 She considereth a field
Proverbs31:17 She girdeth her loins with strength
Proverbs31:18 She perceiveth that her merchandise is profitable: Her lamp goeth not out by night.
Proverbs31:19 She layeth her hands to the distaff
Proverbs31:20 She stretcheth out her hand to the poor; Yea
Proverbs31:21 She is not afraid of the snow for her household; For all her household are clothed with scarlet.
Proverbs31:22 She maketh for herself carpets of tapestry; Her clothing is fine linen and purple.
Proverbs31:23 Her husband is known in the gates
Proverbs31:24 She maketh linen garments and selleth them
Proverbs31:25 Strength and dignity are her clothing; And she laugheth at the time to come.
Proverbs31:26 She openeth her mouth with wisdom; And the law of kindness is on her tongue.
Proverbs31:27 She looketh well to the ways of her household
Proverbs31:28 Her children rise up
Proverbs31:29 Many daughters have done worthily
Proverbs31:30 Grace is deceitful
Proverbs31:31 Give her of the fruit of her hands; And let her works praise her in the gates.
Ecclesiastes1:1 The words of the Preacher
Ecclesiastes1:2 Vanity of vanities
Ecclesiastes1:3 What profit hath man of all his labor wherein he laboreth under the sun?
Ecclesiastes1:4 One generation goeth
Ecclesiastes1:5 The sun also ariseth
Ecclesiastes1:6 The wind goeth toward the south
Ecclesiastes1:7 All the rivers run into the sea
Ecclesiastes1:8 All things are full of weariness; man cannot utter it: the eye is not satisfied with seeing
Ecclesiastes1:9 That which hath been is that which shall be; and that which hath been done is that which shall be done: and there is no new thing under the sun.
Ecclesiastes1:10 Is there a thing whereof it may be said
Ecclesiastes1:11 There is no remembrance of the former generations; neither shall there be any remembrance of the latter generations that are to come
Ecclesiastes1:12 I the Preacher was king over Israel in Jerusalem.
Ecclesiastes1:13 And I applied my heart to seek and to search out by wisdom concerning all that is done under heaven: it is a sore travail that God hath given to the sons of men to be exercised therewith.
Ecclesiastes1:14 I have seen all the works that are done under the sun; and
Ecclesiastes1:15 That which is crooked cannot be made straight; and that which is wanting cannot be numbered.
Ecclesiastes1:16 I communed with mine own hear
Ecclesiastes1:17 And I applied my heart to know wisdom
Ecclesiastes1:18 For in much wisdom is much grief; and he that increaseth knowledge increaseth sorrow.
Ecclesiastes2:1 I said in my heart
Ecclesiastes2:2 I said of laughter
Ecclesiastes2:3 I searched in my heart how to cheer my flesh with wine
Ecclesiastes2:4 I made me great works; I builded me houses; I planted me vineyards;
Ecclesiastes2:5 I made me gardens and parks
Ecclesiastes2:6 I made me pools of water
Ecclesiastes2:7 I bought men-servants and maid-servants
Ecclesiastes2:8 I gathered me also silver and gold
Ecclesiastes2:9 So I was great
Ecclesiastes2:10 And whatsoever mine eyes desired I kept not from them; I withheld not my heart from any joy; for my heart rejoiced because of all my labor; and this was my portion from all my labor.
Ecclesiastes2:11 Then I looked on all the works that my hands had wrought
Ecclesiastes2:12 And I turned myself to behold wisdom
Ecclesiastes2:13 Then I saw that wisdom excelleth folly
Ecclesiastes2:14 The wise mans eyes are in his head
Ecclesiastes2:15 Then said I in my heart
Ecclesiastes2:16 For of the wise man
Ecclesiastes2:17 So I hated life
Ecclesiastes2:18 And I hated all my labor wherein I labored under the sun
Ecclesiastes2:19 And who knoweth whether he will be a wise man or a fool? yet will he have rule over all my labor wherein I have labored
Ecclesiastes2:20 Therefore I turned about to cause my heart to despair concerning all the labor wherein I had labored under the sun.
Ecclesiastes2:21 For there is a man whose labor is with wisdom
Ecclesiastes2:22 For what hath a man of all his labor
Ecclesiastes2:23 For all his days are but sorrows
Ecclesiastes2:24 There is nothing better for a man than that he should eat and drink
Ecclesiastes2:25 For who can eat
Ecclesiastes2:26 For to the man that pleaseth him God giveth wisdom
Ecclesiastes3:1 For everything there is a season
Ecclesiastes3:2 a time to be born
Ecclesiastes3:3 a time to kill
Ecclesiastes3:4 a time to weep
Ecclesiastes3:5 a time to cast away stones
Ecclesiastes3:6 a time to seek
Ecclesiastes3:7 a time to rend
Ecclesiastes3:8 a time to love
Ecclesiastes3:9 What profit hath he that worketh in that wherein he laboreth?
Ecclesiastes3:10 I have seen the travail which God hath given to the sons of men to be exercised therewith.
Ecclesiastes3:11 He hath made everything beautiful in its time: also he hath set eternity in their heart
Ecclesiastes3:12 I know that there is nothing better for them
Ecclesiastes3:13 And also that every man should eat and drink
Ecclesiastes3:14 I know that
Ecclesiastes3:15 That which is hath been long ago; and that which is to be hath long ago been: and God seeketh again that which is passed away.
Ecclesiastes3:16 And moreover I saw under the sun
Ecclesiastes3:17 I said in my heart
Ecclesiastes3:18 I said in my heart
Ecclesiastes3:19 For that which befalleth the sons of men befalleth beasts; even one thing befalleth them: as the one dieth
Ecclesiastes3:20 All go unto one place; all are of the dust
Ecclesiastes3:21 Who knoweth the spirit of man
Ecclesiastes3:22 Wherefore I saw that there is nothing better
Ecclesiastes4:1 Then I returned and saw all the oppressions that are done under the sun: and
Ecclesiastes4:2 Wherefore I praised the dead that have been long dead more than the living that are yet alive;
Ecclesiastes4:3 yea
Ecclesiastes4:4 Then I saw all labor and every skilful work
Ecclesiastes4:5 The fool foldeth his hands together
Ecclesiastes4:6 Better is a handful
Ecclesiastes4:7 Then I returned and saw vanity under the sun.
Ecclesiastes4:8 There is one that is alone
Ecclesiastes4:9 Two are better than one
Ecclesiastes4:10 For if they fall
Ecclesiastes4:11 Again
Ecclesiastes4:12 And if a man prevail against him that is alone
Ecclesiastes4:13 Better is a poor and wise youth than an old and foolish king
Ecclesiastes4:14 For out of prison he came forth to be king; yea
Ecclesiastes4:15 I saw all the living that walk under the sun
Ecclesiastes4:16 There was no end of all the people
Ecclesiastes5:1 Keep thy foot when thou goest to the house of God; for to draw nigh to hear is better than to give the sacrifice of fools: for they know not that they do evil.
Ecclesiastes5:2 Be not rash with thy mouth
Ecclesiastes5:3 For a dream cometh with a multitude of business
Ecclesiastes5:4 When thou vowest a vow unto God
Ecclesiastes5:5 Better is it that thou shouldest not vow
Ecclesiastes5:6 Suffer not thy mouth to cause thy flesh to sin; neither say thou before the angel
Ecclesiastes5:7 For in the multitude of dreams there are vanities
Ecclesiastes5:8 If thou seest the oppression of the poor
Ecclesiastes5:9 Moreover the profit of the earth is for all: the king himself is served by the field.
Ecclesiastes5:10 He that loveth silver shall not be satisfied with silver; nor he that loveth abundance
Ecclesiastes5:11 When goods increase
Ecclesiastes5:12 The sleep of a laboring man is sweet
Ecclesiastes5:13 There is a grievous evil which I have seen under the sun
Ecclesiastes5:14 and those riches perish by evil adventure; and if he hath begotten a son
Ecclesiastes5:15 As he came forth from his mothers womb
Ecclesiastes5:16 And this also is a grievous evil
Ecclesiastes5:17 All his days also he eateth in darkness
Ecclesiastes5:18 Behold
Ecclesiastes5:19 Every man also to whom God hath given riches and wealth
Ecclesiastes5:20 For he shall not much remember the days of his life; because God answereth him in the joy of his heart.
Ecclesiastes6:1 There is an evil which I have seen under the sun
Ecclesiastes6:2 a man to whom God giveth riches
Ecclesiastes6:3 If a man beget a hundred children
Ecclesiastes6:4 for it cometh in vanity
Ecclesiastes6:5 moreover it hath not seen the sun nor known it; this hath rest rather than the other:
Ecclesiastes6:6 yea
Ecclesiastes6:7 All the labor of man is for his mouth
Ecclesiastes6:8 For what advantage hath the wise more than the fool? or what hath the poor man
Ecclesiastes6:9 Better is the sight of the eyes than the wandering of the desire: this also is vanity and a striving after wind.
Ecclesiastes6:10 Whatsoever hath been
Ecclesiastes6:11 Seeing there are many things that increase vanity
Ecclesiastes6:12 For who knoweth what is good for man in his life
Ecclesiastes7:1 A good name is better than precious oil; and the day of death
Ecclesiastes7:2 It is better to go to the house of mourning than to go to the house of feasting: for that is the end of all men; and the living will lay it to his heart.
Ecclesiastes7:3 Sorrow is better than laughter; for by the sadness of the countenance the heart is made glad.
Ecclesiastes7:4 The heart of the wise is in the house of mourning; but the heart of fools is in the house of mirth.
Ecclesiastes7:5 It is better to hear the rebuke of the wise
Ecclesiastes7:6 For as the crackling of thorns under a pot
Ecclesiastes7:7 Surely extortion maketh the wise man foolish; and a bribe destroyeth the understanding.
Ecclesiastes7:8 Better is the end of a thing than the beginning thereof; and the patient in spirit is better than the proud in spirit.
Ecclesiastes7:9 Be not hasty in thy spirit to be angry; for anger resteth in the bosom of fools.
Ecclesiastes7:10 Say not thou
Ecclesiastes7:11 Wisdom is as good as an inheritance; yea
Ecclesiastes7:12 For wisdom is a defence
Ecclesiastes7:13 Consider the work of God: for who can make that straight
Ecclesiastes7:14 In the day of prosperity be joyful
Ecclesiastes7:15 All this have I seen in my days of vanity: there is a righteous man that perisheth in his righteousness
Ecclesiastes7:16 Be not righteous overmuch; neither make thyself overwise: why shouldest thou destroy thyself?
Ecclesiastes7:17 Be not overmuch wicked
Ecclesiastes7:18 It is good that thou shouldest take hold of this; yea
Ecclesiastes7:19 Wisdom is a strength to the wise man more than ten rulers that are in a city.
Ecclesiastes7:20 Surely there is not a righteous man upon earth
Ecclesiastes7:21 Also take not heed unto all words that are spoken
Ecclesiastes7:22 for oftentimes also thine own heart knoweth that thou thyself likewise hast cursed others.
Ecclesiastes7:23 All this have I proved in wisdom: I said
Ecclesiastes7:24 That which is
Ecclesiastes7:25 I turned about
Ecclesiastes7:26 And I find more bitter than death the woman whose heart is snares and nets
Ecclesiastes7:27 Behold
Ecclesiastes7:28 which my soul still seeketh
Ecclesiastes7:29 Behold
Ecclesiastes8:1 Who is as the wise man? and who knoweth the interpretation of a thing? A mans wisdom maketh his face to shine
Ecclesiastes8:2 I counsel thee
Ecclesiastes8:3 Be not hasty to go out of his presence; persist not in an evil thing: for he doeth whatsoever pleaseth him.
Ecclesiastes8:4 For the kings word hath power; and who may say unto him
Ecclesiastes8:5 Whoso keepeth the commandment shall know no evil thing; and a wise mans heart discerneth time and judgment:
Ecclesiastes8:6 for to every purpose there is a time and judgment; because the misery of man is great upon him:
Ecclesiastes8:7 for he knoweth not that which shall be; for who can tell him how it shall be?
Ecclesiastes8:8 There is no man that hath power over the spirit to retain the spirit; neither hath he power over the day of death; and there is no discharge in war: neither shall wickedness deliver him that is given to it.
Ecclesiastes8:9 All this have I seen
Ecclesiastes8:10 So I saw the wicked buried
Ecclesiastes8:11 Because sentence against an evil work is not executed speedily
Ecclesiastes8:12 Though a sinner do evil a hundred times
Ecclesiastes8:13 but it shall not be well with the wicked
Ecclesiastes8:14 There is a vanity which is done upon the earth
Ecclesiastes8:15 Then I commended mirth
Ecclesiastes8:16 When I applied my heart to know wisdom
Ecclesiastes8:17 then I beheld all the work of God
Ecclesiastes9:1 For all this I laid to my heart
Ecclesiastes9:2 All things come alike to all: there is one event to the righteous and to the wicked; to the good and to the clean and to the unclean; to him that sacrificeth and to him that sacrificeth not; as is the good
Ecclesiastes9:3 This is an evil in all that is done under the sun
Ecclesiastes9:4 For to him that is joined with all the living there is hope; for a living dog is better than a dead lion.
Ecclesiastes9:5 For the living know that they shall die: but the dead know not anything
Ecclesiastes9:6 As well their love
Ecclesiastes9:7 Go thy way
Ecclesiastes9:8 Let thy garments be always white; and let not thy head lack oil.
Ecclesiastes9:9 Live joyfully with the wife whom thou lovest all the days of thy life of vanity
Ecclesiastes9:10 Whatsoever thy hand findeth to do
Ecclesiastes9:11 I returned
Ecclesiastes9:12 For man also knoweth not his time: as the fishes that are taken in an evil net
Ecclesiastes9:13 I have also seen wisdom under the sun on this wise
Ecclesiastes9:14 There was a little city
Ecclesiastes9:15 Now there was found in it a poor wise man
Ecclesiastes9:16 Then said I
Ecclesiastes9:17 The words of the wise heard in quiet are better than the cry of him that ruleth among fools.
Ecclesiastes9:18 Wisdom is better than weapons of war; but one sinner destroyeth much good.
Ecclesiastes10:1 Dead flies cause the oil of the perfumer to send forth an evil odor; so doth a little folly outweigh wisdom and honor.
Ecclesiastes10:2 A wise mans heart is at his right hand; but a fools heart at his left.
Ecclesiastes10:3 Yea also
Ecclesiastes10:4 If the spirit of the ruler rise up against thee
Ecclesiastes10:5 There is an evil which I have seen under the sun
Ecclesiastes10:6 folly is set in great dignity
Ecclesiastes10:7 I have seen servants upon horses
Ecclesiastes10:8 He that diggeth a pit shall fall into it; and whoso breaketh through a wall
Ecclesiastes10:9 Whoso heweth out stones shall be hurt therewith; and he that cleaveth wood is endangered thereby.
Ecclesiastes10:10 If the iron be blunt
Ecclesiastes10:11 If the serpent bite before it is charmed
Ecclesiastes10:12 The words of a wise mans mouth are gracious; but the lips of a fool will swallow up himself.
Ecclesiastes10:13 The beginning of the words of his mouth is foolishness; and the end of his talk is mischievous madness.
Ecclesiastes10:14 A fool also multiplieth words: yet man knoweth not what shall be; and that which shall be after him
Ecclesiastes10:15 The labor of fools wearieth every one of them; for he knoweth not how to go to the city.
Ecclesiastes10:16 Woe to thee
Ecclesiastes10:17 Happy art thou
Ecclesiastes10:18 By slothfulness the roof sinketh in; and through idleness of the hands the house leaketh.
Ecclesiastes10:19 A feast is made for laughter
Ecclesiastes10:20 Revile not the king

Ecclesiastes11:2 Give a portion to seven
Ecclesiastes11:3 If the clouds be full of rain

Ecclesiastes11:5 As thou knowest not what is the way of the wind
Ecclesiastes11:6 In the morning sow thy seed
Ecclesiastes11:7 Truly the light is sweet
Ecclesiastes11:8 Yea
Ecclesiastes11:9 Rejoice
Ecclesiastes11:10 Therefore remove sorrow from thy heart

Ecclesiastes12:2 before the sun

Ecclesiastes12:7 and the dust returneth to the earth as it was
Ecclesiastes12:8 Vanity of vanities

Isaiah1:1 The vision of Isaiah the son of Amoz
Isaiah1:2 Hear
Isaiah1:3 The ox knoweth his owner
Isaiah1:4 Ah sinful nation
Isaiah1:5 Why will ye be still stricken
Isaiah1:6 From the sole of the foot even unto the head there is no soundness in it; but wounds
Isaiah1:7 Your country is desolate; your cities are burned with fire; your land
Isaiah1:8 And the daughter of Zion is left as a booth in a vineyard
Isaiah1:9 Except Jehovah of hosts had left unto us a very small remnant
Isaiah1:10 Hear the word of Jehovah
Isaiah1:11 What unto me is the multitude of your sacrifices? saith Jehovah: I have had enough of the burnt-offerings of rams
Isaiah1:12 When ye come to appear before me
Isaiah1:13 Bring no more vain oblations; incense is an abomination unto me; new moon and sabbath
Isaiah1:14 Your new moons and your appointed feasts my soul hateth; they are a trouble unto me; I am weary of bearing them.
Isaiah1:15 And when ye spread forth your hands
Isaiah1:16 Wash you
Isaiah1:17 learn to do well; seek justice
Isaiah1:18 Come now
Isaiah1:19 If ye be willing and obedient
Isaiah1:20 but if ye refuse and rebel
Isaiah1:21 How is the faithful city become a harlot! she that was full of justice! righteousness lodged in her
Isaiah1:22 Thy silver is become dross
Isaiah1:23 Thy princes are rebellious
Isaiah1:24 Therefore saith the Lord
Isaiah1:25 and I will turn my hand upon thee
Isaiah1:26 and I will restore thy judges as at the first
Isaiah1:27 Zion shall be redeemed with justice
Isaiah1:28 But the destruction of transgressors and sinners shall be together
Isaiah1:29 For they shall be ashamed of the oaks which ye have desired
Isaiah1:30 For ye shall be as an oak whose leaf fadeth
Isaiah1:31 And the strong shall be as tow

Jeremiah44:27 Behold
Jeremiah44:28 And they that escape the sword shall return out of the land of Egypt into the land of Judah
Jeremiah44:29 And this shall be the sign unto you
Jeremiah44:30 Thus saith Jehovah
