Jeremiah45:2 Thus saith Jehovah
Jeremiah45:3 Thou didst say
Jeremiah45:4 Thus shalt thou say unto him
Jeremiah45:5 And seekest thou great things for thyself? seek them not; for
Jeremiah46:1 The word of Jehovah which came to Jeremiah the prophet concerning the nations.
Jeremiah46:2 Of Egypt: concerning the army of Pharaoh-neco king of Egypt
Jeremiah46:3 Prepare ye the buckler and shield
Jeremiah46:4 Harness the horses
Jeremiah46:5 Wherefore have I seen it? they are dismayed and are turned backward; and their mighty ones are beaten down
Jeremiah46:6 Let not the swift flee away
Jeremiah46:7 Who is this that riseth up like the Nile
Jeremiah46:8 Egypt riseth up like the Nile
Jeremiah46:9 Go up
Jeremiah46:10 For that day is a day of the Lord
Jeremiah46:11 Go up into Gilead
Jeremiah46:12 The nations have heard of thy shame
Jeremiah46:13 The word that Jehovah spake to Jeremiah the prophet
Jeremiah46:14 Declare ye in Egypt
Jeremiah46:15 Why are thy strong ones swept away? they stood not
Jeremiah46:16 He made many to stumble
Jeremiah46:17 They cried there
Jeremiah46:18 As I live
Jeremiah46:19 O thou daughter that dwellest in Egypt
Jeremiah46:20 Egypt is a very fair heifer; but destruction out of the north is come
Jeremiah46:21 Also her hired men in the midst of her are like calves of the stall; for they also are turned back
Jeremiah46:22 The sound thereof shall go like the serpent; for they shall march with an army
Jeremiah46:23 They shall cut down her forest
Jeremiah46:24 The daughter of Egypt shall be put to shame; she shall be delivered into the hand of the people of the north.
Jeremiah46:25 Jehovah of hosts
Jeremiah46:26 and I will deliver them into the hand of those that seek their lives
Jeremiah46:27 But fear not thou
Jeremiah46:28 Fear not thou
Jeremiah47:1 The word of Jehovah that came to Jeremiah the prophet concerning the Philistines
Jeremiah47:2 Thus saith Jehovah: Behold
Jeremiah47:3 At the noise of the stamping of the hoofs of his strong ones
Jeremiah47:4 because of the day that cometh to destroy all the Philistines
Jeremiah47:5 Baldness is come upon Gaza; Ashkelon is brought to nought
Jeremiah47:6 O thou sword of Jehovah
Jeremiah47:7 How canst thou be quiet
Jeremiah48:1 Of Moab. Thus saith Jehovah of hosts
Jeremiah48:2 The praise of Moab is no more; in Heshbon they have devised evil against her: Come
Jeremiah48:3 The sound of a cry from Horonaim
Jeremiah48:4 Moab is destroyed; her little ones have caused a cry to be heard.
Jeremiah48:5 For by the ascent of Luhith with continual weeping shall they go up; for at the descent of Horonaim they have heard the distress of the cry of destruction.
Jeremiah48:6 Flee
Jeremiah48:7 For
Jeremiah48:8 And the destroyer shall come upon every city
Jeremiah48:9 Give wings unto Moab
Jeremiah48:10 Cursed be he that doeth the work of Jehovah negligently; and cursed be he that keepeth back his sword from blood.
Jeremiah48:11 Moab hath been at ease from his youth
Jeremiah48:12 Therefore
Jeremiah48:13 And Moab shall be ashamed of Chemosh
Jeremiah48:14 How say ye
Jeremiah48:15 Moab is laid waste
Jeremiah48:16 The calamity of Moab is near to come
Jeremiah48:17 All ye that are round about him
Jeremiah48:18 O thou daughter that dwellest in Dibon
Jeremiah48:19 O inhabitant of Aroer
Jeremiah48:20 Moab is put to shame; for it is broken down: wail and cry; tell ye it by the Arnon
Jeremiah48:21 And judgment is come upon the plain country
Jeremiah48:22 and upon Dibon
Jeremiah48:23 and upon Kiriathaim
Jeremiah48:24 and upon Kerioth
Jeremiah48:25 The horn of Moab is cut off
Jeremiah48:26 Make ye him drunken; for he magnified himself against Jehovah: and Moab shall wallow in his vomit
Jeremiah48:27 For was not Israel a derision unto thee? was he found among thieves? for as often as thou speakest of him
Jeremiah48:28 O ye inhabitants of Moab
Jeremiah48:29 We have heard of the pride of Moab
Jeremiah48:30 I know his wrath
Jeremiah48:31 Therefore will I wail for Moab; yea
Jeremiah48:32 With more than the weeping of Jazer will I weep for thee
Jeremiah48:33 And gladness and joy is taken away from the fruitful field and from the land of Moab; and I have caused wine to cease from the winepresses: none shall tread with shouting; the shouting shall be no shouting.
Jeremiah48:34 From the cry of Heshbon even unto Elealeh
Jeremiah48:35 Moreover I will cause to cease in Moab
Jeremiah48:36 Therefore my heart soundeth for Moab like pipes
Jeremiah48:37 For every head is bald
Jeremiah48:38 On all the housetops of Moab and in the streets thereof there is lamentation every where; for I have broken Moab like a vessel wherein none delighteth
Jeremiah48:39 How is it broken down! how do they wail! how hath Moab turned the back with shame! so shall Moab become a derision and a terror to all that are round about him.
Jeremiah48:40 For thus saith Jehovah: Behold
Jeremiah48:41 Kerioth is taken
Jeremiah48:42 And Moab shall be destroyed from being a people
Jeremiah48:43 Fear
Jeremiah48:44 He that fleeth from the fear shall fall into the pit; and he that getteth up out of the pit shall be taken in the snare: for I will bring upon him
Jeremiah48:45 They that fled stand without strength under the shadow of Heshbon; for a fire is gone forth out of Heshbon
Jeremiah48:46 Woe unto thee
Jeremiah48:47 Yet will I bring back the captivity of Moab in the latter days
Jeremiah49:1 Of the children of Ammon. Thus saith Jehovah: Hath Israel no sons? hath he no heir? why then doth Malcam possess Gad
Jeremiah49:2 Therefore
Jeremiah49:3 Wail
Jeremiah49:4 Wherefore gloriest thou in the valleys
Jeremiah49:5 Behold
Jeremiah49:6 But afterward I will bring back the captivity of the children of Ammon
Jeremiah49:7 Of Edom. Thus saith Jehovah of hosts: Is wisdom no more in Teman? is counsel perished from the prudent? is their wisdom vanished?
Jeremiah49:8 Flee ye
Jeremiah49:9 If grape-gatherers came to thee
Jeremiah49:10 But I have made Esau bare
Jeremiah49:11 Leave thy fatherless children
Jeremiah49:12 For thus saith Jehovah: Behold
Jeremiah49:13 For I have sworn by myself
Jeremiah49:14 I have heard tidings from Jehovah
Jeremiah49:15 For
Jeremiah49:16 As for thy terribleness
Jeremiah49:17 And Edom shall become an astonishment: every one that passeth by it shall be astonished
Jeremiah49:18 As in the overthrow of Sodom and Gomorrah and the neighbor cities thereof
Jeremiah49:19 Behold
Jeremiah49:20 Therefore hear ye the counsel of Jehovah
Jeremiah49:21 The earth trembleth at the noise of their fall; there is a cry
Jeremiah49:22 Behold
Jeremiah49:23 Of Damascus. Hamath is confounded
Jeremiah49:24 Damascus is waxed feeble
Jeremiah49:25 How is the city of praise not forsaken
Jeremiah49:26 Therefore her young men shall fall in her streets
Jeremiah49:27 And I will kindle a fire in the wall of Damascus
Jeremiah49:28 Of Kedar
Jeremiah49:29 Their tents and their flocks shall they take; they shall carry away for themselves their curtains
Jeremiah49:30 Flee ye
Jeremiah49:31 Arise
Jeremiah49:32 And their camels shall be a booty
Jeremiah49:33 And Hazor shall be a dwelling-place of jackals
Jeremiah49:34 The word of Jehovah that came to Jeremiah the prophet concerning Elam
Jeremiah49:35 Thus saith Jehovah of hosts: Behold
Jeremiah49:36 And upon Elam will I bring the four winds from the four quarters of heaven
Jeremiah49:37 And I will cause Elam to be dismayed before their enemies
Jeremiah49:38 and I will set my throne in Elam
Jeremiah49:39 But it shall come to pass in the latter days
Jeremiah50:1 The word that Jehovah spake concerning Babylon
Jeremiah50:2 Declare ye among the nations and publish
Jeremiah50:3 For out of the north there cometh up a nation against her
Jeremiah50:4 In those days
Jeremiah50:5 They shall inquire concerning Zion with their faces thitherward
Jeremiah50:6 My people have been lost sheep: their shepherds have caused them to go astray; they have turned them away on the mountains; they have gone from mountain to hill; they have forgotten their resting-place.
Jeremiah50:7 All that found them have devoured them; and their adversaries said
Jeremiah50:8 Flee out of the midst of Babylon
Jeremiah50:9 For
Jeremiah50:10 And Chaldea shall be a prey: all that prey upon her shall be satisfied
Jeremiah50:11 Because ye are glad
Jeremiah50:12 your mother shall be utterly put to shame; she that bare you shall be confounded: behold
Jeremiah50:13 Because of the wrath of Jehovah she shall not be inhabited
Jeremiah50:14 Set yourselves in array against Babylon round about
Jeremiah50:15 Shout against her round about: she hath submitted herself; her bulwarks are fallen
Jeremiah50:16 Cut off the sower from Babylon
Jeremiah50:17 Israel is a hunted sheep; the lions have driven him away: first
Jeremiah50:18 Therefore thus saith Jehovah of hosts
Jeremiah50:19 And I will bring Israel again to his pasture
Jeremiah50:20 In those days
Jeremiah50:21 Go up against the land of Merathaim
Jeremiah50:22 A sound of battle is in the land
Jeremiah50:23 How is the hammer of the whole earth cut asunder and broken! how is Babylon become a desolation among the nations!
Jeremiah50:24 I have laid a snare for thee
Jeremiah50:25 Jehovah hath opened his armory
Jeremiah50:26 Come against her from the utmost border; open her store-houses; cast her up as heaps
Jeremiah50:27 Slay all her bullocks; let them go down to the slaughter: woe unto them! for their day is come
Jeremiah50:28 The voice of them that flee and escape out of the land of Babylon
Jeremiah50:29 Call together the archers against Babylon
Jeremiah50:30 Therefore shall her young men fall in her streets
Jeremiah50:31 Behold
Jeremiah50:32 And the proud one shall stumble and fall
Jeremiah50:33 Thus saith Jehovah of hosts: The children of Israel and the children of Judah are oppressed together; and all that took them captive hold them fast; they refuse to let them go.
Jeremiah50:34 Their Redeemer is strong; Jehovah of hosts is his name: he will thoroughly plead their cause
Jeremiah50:35 A sword is upon the Chaldeans
Jeremiah50:36 A sword is upon the boasters
Jeremiah50:37 A sword is upon their horses
Jeremiah50:38 A drought is upon her waters
Jeremiah50:39 Therefore the wild beasts of the desert with the wolves shall dwell there
Jeremiah50:40 As when God overthrew Sodom and Gomorrah and the neighbor cities thereof
Jeremiah50:41 Behold
Jeremiah50:42 They lay hold on bow and spear; they are cruel
Jeremiah50:43 The king of Babylon hath heard the tidings of them
Jeremiah50:44 Behold
Jeremiah50:45 Therefore hear ye the counsel of Jehovah
Jeremiah50:46 At the noise of the taking of Babylon the earth trembleth
Jeremiah51:1 Thus saith Jehovah: Behold
Jeremiah51:2 And I will send unto Babylon strangers
Jeremiah51:3 Against him that bendeth let the archer bend his bow
Jeremiah51:4 And they shall fall down slain in the land of the Chaldeans
Jeremiah51:5 For Israel is not forsaken
Jeremiah51:6 Flee out of the midst of Babylon
Jeremiah51:7 Babylon hath been a golden cup in Jehovahs hand
Jeremiah51:8 Babylon is suddenly fallen and destroyed: wail for her; take balm for her pain
Jeremiah51:9 We would have healed Babylon
Jeremiah51:10 Jehovah hath brought forth our righteousness: come
Jeremiah51:11 Make sharp the arrows; hold firm the shields: Jehovah hath stirred up the spirit of the kings of the Medes; because his purpose is against Babylon
Jeremiah51:12 Set up a standard against the walls of Babylon
Jeremiah51:13 O thou that dwellest upon many waters
Jeremiah51:14 Jehovah of hosts hath sworn by himself
Jeremiah51:15 He hath made the earth by his power
Jeremiah51:16 when he uttereth his voice
Jeremiah51:17 Every man is become brutish and is without knowledge; every goldsmith is put to shame by his image; for his molten image is falsehood
Jeremiah51:18 They are vanity
Jeremiah51:19 The portion of Jacob is not like these; for he is the former of all things; and Israel is the tribe of his inheritance: Jehovah of hosts is his name.
Jeremiah51:20 Thou art my battle-axe and weapons of war: and with thee will I break in pieces the nations; and with thee will I destroy kingdoms;
Jeremiah51:21 and with thee will I break in pieces the horse and his rider;
Jeremiah51:22 and with thee will I break in pieces the chariot and him that rideth therein; and with thee will I break in pieces man and woman; and with thee will I break in pieces the old man and the youth; and with thee will I break in pieces the young man and the virgin;
Jeremiah51:23 and with thee will I break in pieces the shepherd and his flock; and with thee will I break in pieces the husbandman and his yoke of oxen; and with thee will I break in pieces governors and deputies.
Jeremiah51:24 And I will render unto Babylon and to all the inhabitants of Chaldea all their evil that they have done in Zion in your sight
Jeremiah51:25 Behold
Jeremiah51:26 And they shall not take of thee a stone for a corner
Jeremiah51:27 Set ye up a standard in the land
Jeremiah51:28 Prepare against her the nations
Jeremiah51:29 And the land trembleth and is in pain; for the purposes of Jehovah against Babylon do stand
Jeremiah51:30 The mighty men of Babylon have forborne to fight
Jeremiah51:31 One post shall run to meet another
Jeremiah51:32 and the passages are seized
Jeremiah51:33 For thus saith Jehovah of hosts
Jeremiah51:34 Nebuchadrezzar the king of Babylon hath devoured me
Jeremiah51:35 The violence done to me and to my flesh be upon Babylon
Jeremiah51:36 Therefore thus saith Jehovah: Behold
Jeremiah51:37 And Babylon shall become heaps
Jeremiah51:38 They shall roar together like young lions; they shall growl as lions whelps.
Jeremiah51:39 When they are heated
Jeremiah51:40 I will bring them down like lambs to the slaughter
Jeremiah51:41 How is Sheshach taken! and the praise of the whole earth seized! how is Babylon become a desolation among the nations!
Jeremiah51:42 The sea is come up upon Babylon; she is covered with the multitude of the waves thereof.
Jeremiah51:43 Her cities are become a desolation
Jeremiah51:44 And I will execute judgment upon Bel in Babylon
Jeremiah51:45 My people
Jeremiah51:46 And let not your heart faint
Jeremiah51:47 Therefore
Jeremiah51:48 Then the heavens and the earth
Jeremiah51:49 As Babylon hath caused the slain of Israel to fall
Jeremiah51:50 Ye that have escaped the sword
Jeremiah51:51 We are confounded
Jeremiah51:52 Wherefore
Jeremiah51:53 Though Babylon should mount up to heaven
Jeremiah51:54 The sound of a cry from Babylon
Jeremiah51:55 For Jehovah layeth Babylon waste
Jeremiah51:56 for the destroyer is come upon her
Jeremiah51:57 And I will make drunk her princes and her wise men
Jeremiah51:58 Thus saith Jehovah of hosts: The broad walls of Babylon shall be utterly overthrown
Jeremiah51:59 The word which Jeremiah the prophet commanded Seraiah the son of Neriah
Jeremiah51:60 And Jeremiah wrote in a book all the evil that should come upon Babylon
Jeremiah51:61 And Jeremiah said to Seraiah
Jeremiah51:62 and say
Jeremiah51:63 And it shall be
Jeremiah51:64 and thou shalt say
Jeremiah52:1 Zedekiah was one and twenty years old when he began to reign; and he reigned eleven years in Jerusalem: and his mothers name was Hamutal the daughter of Jeremiah of Libnah.
Jeremiah52:2 And he did that which was evil in the sight of Jehovah
Jeremiah52:3 For through the anger of Jehovah did it come to pass in Jerusalem and Judah
Jeremiah52:4 And it came to pass in the ninth year of his reign
Jeremiah52:5 So the city was besieged unto the eleventh year of king Zedekiah.
Jeremiah52:6 In the fourth month
Jeremiah52:7 Then a breach was made in the city
Jeremiah52:8 But the army of the Chaldeans pursued after the king
Jeremiah52:9 Then they took the king
Jeremiah52:10 And the king of Babylon slew the sons of Zedekiah before his eyes: he slew also all the princes of Judah in Riblah.
Jeremiah52:11 And he put out the eyes of Zedekiah; and the king of Babylon bound him in fetters
Jeremiah52:12 Now in the fifth month
Jeremiah52:13 and he burned the house of Jehovah
Jeremiah52:14 And all the army of the Chaldeans
Jeremiah52:15 Then Nebuzaradan the captain of the guard carried away captive of the poorest of the people
Jeremiah52:16 But Nebuzaradan the captain of the guard left of the poorest of the land to be vinedressers and husbandmen.
Jeremiah52:17 And the pillars of brass that were in the house of Jehovah
Jeremiah52:18 The pots also
Jeremiah52:19 And the cups
Jeremiah52:20 The two pillars
Jeremiah52:21 And as for the pillars
Jeremiah52:22 And a capital of brass was upon it; and the height of the one capital was five cubits
Jeremiah52:23 And there were ninety and six pomegranates on the sides; all the pomegranates were a hundred upon the network round about.
Jeremiah52:24 And the captain of the guard took Seraiah the chief priest
Jeremiah52:25 and out of the city he took an officer that was set over the men of war; and seven men of them that saw the kings face
Jeremiah52:26 And Nebuzaradan the captain of the guard took them
Jeremiah52:27 And the king of Babylon smote them
Jeremiah52:28 This is the people whom Nebuchadrezzar carried away captive: in the seventh year three thousand Jews and three and twenty;
Jeremiah52:29 in the eighteenth year of Nebuchadrezzar he carried away captive from Jerusalem eight hundred thirty and two persons;
Jeremiah52:30 in the three and twentieth year of Nebuchadrezzar Nebuzaradan the captain of the guard carried away captive of the Jews seven hundred forty and five persons: all the persons were four thousand and six hundred.
Jeremiah52:31 And it came to pass in the seven and thirtieth year of the captivity of Jehoiachin king of Judah
Jeremiah52:32 and he spake kindly to him
Jeremiah52:33 and changed his prison garments. And Jehoiachin did eat bread before him continually all the days of his life:
Jeremiah52:34 and for his allowance
Lamentations1:1 How doth the city sit solitary
Lamentations1:2 She weepeth sore in the night
Lamentations1:3 Judah is gone into captivity because of affliction
Lamentations1:4 The ways of Zion do mourn
Lamentations1:5 Her adversaries are become the head
Lamentations1:6 And from the daughter of Zion all her majesty is departed: Her princes are become like harts that find no pasture
Lamentations1:7 Jerusalem remembereth in the days of her affliction and of her miseries all her pleasant things that were from the days of old: When her people fell into the hand of the adversary
Lamentations1:8 Jerusalem hath grievously sinned; therefore she is become as an unclean thing; All that honored her despise her
Lamentations1:9 Her filthiness was in her skirts; she remembered not her latter end; Therefore is she come down wonderfully; she hath no comforter: Behold
Lamentations1:10 The adversary hath spread out his hand upon all her pleasant things: For she hath seen that the nations are entered into her sanctuary
Lamentations1:11 All her people sigh
Lamentations1:12 Is it nothing to you
Lamentations1:13 From on high hath he sent fire into my bones
Lamentations1:14 The yoke of my transgressions is bound by his hand; They are knit together
Lamentations1:15 The Lord hath set at nought all my mighty men in the midst of me; He hath called a solemn assembly against me to crush my young men: The Lord hath trodden as in a winepress the virgin daughter of Judah.
Lamentations1:16 For these things I weep; mine eye
Lamentations1:17 Zion spreadeth forth her hands; there is none to comfort her; Jehovah hath commanded concerning Jacob
Lamentations1:18 Jehovah is righteous; for I have rebelled against his commandment: Hear
Lamentations1:19 I called for my lovers
Lamentations1:20 Behold
Lamentations1:21 They have heard that I sigh; there is none to comfort me; All mine enemies have heard of my trouble; they are glad that thou hast done it: Thou wilt bring the day that thou hast proclaimed
Lamentations1:22 Let all their wickedness come before thee; And do unto them
Lamentations2:1 How hath the Lord covered the daughter of Zion with a cloud in his anger! He hath cast down from heaven unto the earth the beauty of Israel
Lamentations2:2 The Lord hath swallowed up all the habitations of Jacob
Lamentations2:3 He hath cut off in fierce anger all the horn of Israel; He hath drawn back his right hand from before the enemy: And he hath burned up Jacob like a flaming fire
Lamentations2:4 He hath bent his bow like an enemy
Lamentations2:5 The Lord is become as an enemy
Lamentations2:6 And he hath violently taken away his tabernacle
Lamentations2:7 The Lord hath cast off his altar
Lamentations2:8 Jehovah hath purposed to destroy the wall of the daughter of Zion; He hath stretched out the line
Lamentations2:9 Her gates are sunk into the ground; he hath destroyed and broken her bars: Her king and her princes are among the nations where the law is not; Yea
Lamentations2:10 The elders of the daughter of Zion sit upon the ground
Lamentations2:11 Mine eyes do fail with tears
Lamentations2:12 They say to their mothers
Lamentations2:13 What shall I testify unto thee? what shall I liken to thee
Lamentations2:14 Thy prophets have seen for thee false and foolish visions; And they have not uncovered thine iniquity
Lamentations2:15 All that pass by clap their hands at thee; They hiss and wag their head at the daughter of Jerusalem
Lamentations2:16 All thine enemies have opened their mouth wide against thee; They hiss and gnash the teeth; they say
Lamentations2:17 Jehovah hath done that which he purposed; he hath fulfilled his word that he commanded in the days of old; He hath thrown down
Lamentations2:18 Their heart cried unto the Lord: O wall of the daughter of Zion
Lamentations2:19 Arise
Lamentations2:20 See
Lamentations2:21 The youth and the old man lie on the ground in the streets; My virgins and my young men are fallen by the sword: Thou hast slain them in the day of thine anger; thou hast slaughtered
Lamentations2:22 Thou hast called
Lamentations3:1 I am the man that hath seen affliction by the rod of his wrath.
Lamentations3:2 He hath led me and caused me to walk in darkness
Lamentations3:3 Surely against me he turneth his hand again and again all the day.
Lamentations3:4 My flesh and my skin hath he made old; he hath broken my bones.
Lamentations3:5 He hath builded against me
Lamentations3:6 He hath made me to dwell in dark places
Lamentations3:7 He hath walled me about
Lamentations3:8 Yea
Lamentations3:9 He hath walled up my ways with hewn stone; he hath made my paths crooked.
Lamentations3:10 He is unto me as a bear lying in wait
Lamentations3:11 He hath turned aside my ways
Lamentations3:12 He hath bent his bow
Lamentations3:13 He hath caused the shafts of his quiver to enter into my reins.
Lamentations3:14 I am become a derision to all my people
Lamentations3:15 He hath filled me with bitterness
Lamentations3:16 He hath also broken my teeth with gravel stones; he hath covered me with ashes.
Lamentations3:17 And thou hast removed my soul far off from peace; I forgat prosperity.
Lamentations3:18 And I said
Lamentations3:19 Remember mine affliction and my misery
Lamentations3:20 My soul hath them still in remembrance
Lamentations3:21 This I recall to my mind; therefore have I hope.
Lamentations3:22 It is of Jehovahs lovingkindnesses that we are not consumed
Lamentations3:23 They are new every morning; great is thy faithfulness.
Lamentations3:24 Jehovah is my portion
Lamentations3:25 Jehovah is good unto them that wait for him
Lamentations3:26 It is good that a man should hope and quietly wait for the salvation of Jehovah.
Lamentations3:27 It is good for a man that he bear the yoke in his youth.
Lamentations3:28 Let him sit alone and keep silence
Lamentations3:29 Let him put his mouth in the dust
Lamentations3:30 Let him give his cheek to him that smiteth him; let him be filled full with reproach.
Lamentations3:31 For the Lord will not cast off for ever.
Lamentations3:32 For though he cause grief
Lamentations3:33 For he doth not afflict willingly
Lamentations3:34 To crush under foot all the prisoners of the earth
Lamentations3:35 To turn aside the right of a man before the face of the Most High
Lamentations3:36 To subvert a man in his cause
Lamentations3:37 Who is he that saith
Lamentations3:38 Out of the mouth of the Most High cometh there not evil and good?
Lamentations3:39 Wherefore doth a living man complain
Lamentations3:40 Let us search and try our ways
Lamentations3:41 Let us lift up our heart with our hands unto God in the heavens.
Lamentations3:42 We have transgressed and have rebelled; thou hast not pardoned.
Lamentations3:43 Thou hast covered with anger and pursued us; thou hast slain
Lamentations3:44 Thou hast covered thyself with a cloud
Lamentations3:45 Thou hast made us an off-scouring and refuse in the midst of the peoples.
Lamentations3:46 All our enemies have opened their mouth wide against us.
Lamentations3:47 Fear and the pit are come upon us
Lamentations3:48 Mine eye runneth down with streams of water
Lamentations3:49 Mine eye poureth down
Lamentations3:50 Till Jehovah look down
Lamentations3:51 Mine eye affecteth my soul
Lamentations3:52 They have chased me sore like a bird
Lamentations3:53 They have cut off my life in the dungeon
Lamentations3:54 Waters flowed over my head; I said
Lamentations3:55 I called upon thy name
Lamentations3:56 Thou heardest my voice; hide not thine ear at my breathing
Lamentations3:57 Thou drewest near in the day that I called upon thee; thou saidst
Lamentations3:58 O Lord
Lamentations3:59 O Jehovah
Lamentations3:60 Thou hast seen all their vengeance and all their devices against me.
Lamentations3:61 Thou hast heard their reproach
Lamentations3:62 The lips of those that rose up against me
Lamentations3:63 Behold thou their sitting down
Lamentations3:64 Thou wilt render unto them a recompense
Lamentations3:65 Thou wilt give them hardness of heart
Lamentations3:66 Thou wilt pursue them in anger
Lamentations4:1 How is the gold become dim! how is the most pure gold changed! The stones of the sanctuary are poured out at the head of every street.
Lamentations4:2 The precious sons of Zion
Lamentations4:3 Even the jackals draw out the breast
Lamentations4:4 The tongue of the sucking child cleaveth to the roof of his mouth for thirst: The young children ask bread
Lamentations4:5 They that did feed delicately are desolate in the streets: They that were brought up in scarlet embrace dunghills.
Lamentations4:6 For the iniquity of the daughter of my people is greater than the sin of Sodom
Lamentations4:7 Her nobles were purer than snow
Lamentations4:8 Their visage is blacker than a coal; they are not known in the streets: Their skin cleaveth to their bones; it is withered
Lamentations4:9 They that are slain with the sword are better than they that are slain with hunger; For these pine away
Lamentations4:10 The hands of the pitiful women have boiled their own children; They were their food in the destruction of the daughter of my people.
Lamentations4:11 Jehovah hath accomplished his wrath
Lamentations4:12 The kings of the earth believed not
Lamentations4:13 It is because of the sins of her prophets

Lamentations5:1 Remember
Lamentations5:2 Our inheritance is turned unto strangers
Lamentations5:3 We are orphans and fatherless; Our mothers are as widows.
Lamentations5:4 We have drunken our water for money; Our wood is sold unto us.
Lamentations5:5 Our pursuers are upon our necks: We are weary
Lamentations5:6 We have given the hand to the Egyptians
Lamentations5:7 Our fathers sinned
Lamentations5:8 Servants rule over us: There is none to deliver us out of their hand.
Lamentations5:9 We get our bread at the peril of our lives
Lamentations5:10 Our skin is black like an oven
Lamentations5:11 They ravished the women in Zion
Lamentations5:12 Princes were hanged up by their hand: The faces of elders were not honored.
Lamentations5:13 The young men bare the mill; And the children stumbled under the wood.
Lamentations5:14 The elders have ceased from the gate
Lamentations5:15 The joy of our heart is ceased; Our dance is turned into mourning.
Lamentations5:16 The crown is fallen from our head: Woe unto us! for we have sinned.
Lamentations5:17 For this our heart is faint; For these things our eyes are dim;
Lamentations5:18 For the mountain of Zion
Lamentations5:19 Thou
Lamentations5:20 Wherefore dost thou forget us for ever
Lamentations5:21 Turn thou us unto thee
Lamentations5:22 But thou hast utterly rejected us; Thou art very wroth against us.
Ezekiel1:1 Now it came to pass in the thirtieth year
Ezekiel1:2 In the fifth day of the month
Ezekiel1:3 the word of Jehovah came expressly unto Ezekiel the priest
Ezekiel1:4 And I looked
Ezekiel1:5 And out of the midst thereof came the likeness of four living creatures. And this was their appearance: they had the likeness of a man.
Ezekiel1:6 And every one had four faces
Ezekiel1:7 And their feet were straight feet; and the sole of their feet was like the sole of a calfs foot; and they sparkled like burnished brass.
Ezekiel1:8 And they had the hands of a man under their wings on their four sides; and they four had their faces and their wings thus:
Ezekiel1:9 their wings were joined one to another; they turned not when they went; they went every one straight forward.
Ezekiel1:10 As for the likeness of their faces
Ezekiel1:11 And their faces and their wings were separate above; two wings of every one were joined one to another
Ezekiel1:12 And they went every one straight forward: whither the spirit was to go
Ezekiel1:13 As for the likeness of the living creatures
Ezekiel1:14 And the living creatures ran and returned as the appearance of a flash of lightning.
Ezekiel1:15 Now as I beheld the living creatures
Ezekiel1:16 The appearance of the wheels and their work was like unto a beryl: and they four had one likeness; and their appearance and their work was as it were a wheel within a wheel.
Ezekiel1:17 When they went
Ezekiel1:18 As for their rims
Ezekiel1:19 And when the living creatures went
Ezekiel1:20 Whithersoever the spirit was to go
Ezekiel1:21 When those went

Ezekiel1:23 And under the firmament were their wings straight
Ezekiel1:24 And when they went
Ezekiel1:25 And there was a voice above the firmament that was over their heads: when they stood
Ezekiel1:26 And above the firmament that was over their heads was the likeness of a throne
Ezekiel1:27 And I saw as it were glowing metal
Ezekiel1:28 As the appearance of the bow that is in the cloud in the day of rain

Daniel1:1 In the third year of the reign of Jehoiakim king of Judah came Nebuchadnezzar king of Babylon unto Jerusalem
Daniel1:2 And the Lord gave Jehoiakim king of Judah into his hand
Daniel1:3 And the king spake unto Ashpenaz the master of his eunuchs
Daniel1:4 youths in whom was no blemish
Daniel1:5 And the king appointed for them a daily portion of the kings dainties
Daniel1:6 Now among these were
Daniel1:7 And the prince of the eunuchs gave names unto them: unto Daniel he gave the name of Belteshazzar; and to Hananiah
Daniel1:8 But Daniel purposed in his heart that he would not defile himself with the kings dainties
Daniel1:9 Now God made Daniel to find kindness and compassion in the sight of the prince of the eunuchs.
Daniel1:10 And the prince of the eunuchs said unto Daniel
Daniel1:11 Then said Daniel to the steward whom the prince of the eunuchs had appointed over Daniel

Daniel1:13 Then let our countenances be looked upon before thee

Daniel1:16 So the steward took away their dainties
Daniel1:17 Now as for these four youths
Daniel1:18 And at the end of the days which the king had appointed for bringing them in
Daniel1:19 And the king communed with them; and among them all was found none like Daniel
Daniel1:20 And in every matter of wisdom and understanding
Daniel1:21 And Daniel continued even unto the first year of king Cyrus.

Daniel11:16 But he that cometh against him shall do according to his own will
Daniel11:17 And he shall set his face to come with the strength of his whole kingdom

Daniel11:19 Then he shall turn his face toward the fortresses of his own land; but he shall stumble and fall

Daniel11:21 And in his place shall stand up a contemptible person
Daniel11:22 And the overwhelming forces shall be overwhelmed from before him

Daniel11:25 And he shall stir up his power and his courage against the king of the south with a great army; and the king of the south shall war in battle with an exceeding great and mighty army; but he shall not stand; for they shall devise devices against him.

Daniel11:27 And as for both these kings
Daniel11:28 Then shall he return into his land with great substance; and his heart shall be against the holy covenant; and he shall do his pleasure
Daniel11:29 At the time appointed he shall return
Daniel11:30 For ships of Kittim shall come against him; therefore he shall be grieved
Daniel11:31 And forces shall stand on his part
Daniel11:32 And such as do wickedly against the covenant shall he pervert by flatteries; but the people that know their God shall be strong
Daniel11:33 And they that are wise among the people shall instruct many; yet they shall fall by the sword and by flame
Daniel11:34 Now when they shall fall
Daniel11:35 And some of them that are wise shall fall
Daniel11:36 And the king shall do according to his will; and he shall exalt himself
Daniel11:37 Neither shall he regard the gods of his fathers
Daniel11:38 But in his place shall he honor the god of fortresses; and a god whom his fathers knew not shall he honor with gold
Daniel11:39 And he shall deal with the strongest fortresses by the help of a foreign god: whosoever acknowledgeth him he will increase with glory; and he shall cause them to rule over many
Daniel11:40 And at the time of the end shall the king of the south contend with him; and the king of the north shall come against him like a whirlwind
Daniel11:41 He shall enter also into the glorious land
Daniel11:42 He shall stretch forth his hand also upon the countries; and the land of Egypt shall not escape.
Daniel11:43 But he shall have power over the treasures of gold and of silver
Daniel11:44 But tidings out of the east and out of the north shall trouble him; and he shall go forth with great fury to destroy and utterly to sweep away many.
Daniel11:45 And he shall plant the tents of his palace between the sea and the glorious holy mountain; yet he shall come to his end

Hosea1:5 And it shall come to pass at that day
Hosea1:6 And she conceived again

Hosea1:9 And Jehovah said
Hosea1:10 Yet the number of the children of Israel shall be as the sand of the sea
Hosea1:11 And the children of Judah and the children of Israel shall be gathered together
Hosea2:1 Say ye unto your brethren
Hosea2:2 Contend with your mother
Hosea2:3 lest I strip her naked
Hosea2:4 Yea
Hosea2:5 for their mother hath played the harlot; she that conceived them hath done shamefully; for she said
Hosea2:6 Therefore
Hosea2:7 And she shall follow after her lovers
Hosea2:8 For she did not know that I gave her the grain
Hosea2:9 Therefore will I take back my grain in the time thereof
Hosea2:10 And now will I uncover her lewdness in the sight of her lovers
Hosea2:11 I will also cause all her mirth to cease
Hosea2:12 And I will lay waste her vines and her fig-trees
Hosea2:13 And I will visit upon her the days of the Baalim
Hosea2:14 Therefore
Hosea2:15 And I will give her her vineyards from thence
Hosea2:16 And it shall be at that day
Hosea2:17 For I will take away the names of the Baalim out of her mouth
Hosea2:18 And in that day will I make a covenant for them with the beasts of the field
Hosea2:19 And I will betroth thee unto me for ever; yea
Hosea2:20 I will even betroth thee unto me in faithfulness; and thou shalt know Jehovah.
Hosea2:21 And it shall come to pass in that day
Hosea2:22 and the earth shall answer the grain
Hosea2:23 And I will sow her unto me in the earth; and I will have mercy upon her that had not obtained mercy; and I will say to them that were not my people
Hosea3:1 And Jehovah said unto me
Hosea3:2 So I bought her to me for fifteen pieces of silver
Hosea3:3 and I said unto her
Hosea3:4 For the children of Israel shall abide many days without king
Hosea3:5 afterward shall the children of Israel return
Hosea4:1 Hear the word of Jehovah
Hosea4:2 There is nought but swearing and breaking faith
Hosea4:3 Therefore shall the land mourn
Hosea4:4 Yet let no man strive
Hosea4:5 And thou shalt stumble in the day
Hosea4:6 My people are destroyed for lack of knowledge: because thou hast rejected knowledge
Hosea4:7 As they were multiplied
Hosea4:8 They feed on the sin of my people
Hosea4:9 And it shall be
Hosea4:10 And they shall eat
Hosea4:11 Whoredom and wine and new wine take away the understanding.
Hosea4:12 My people ask counsel at their stock
Hosea4:13 They sacrifice upon the tops of the mountains
Hosea4:14 I will not punish your daughters when they play the harlot
Hosea4:15 Though thou
Hosea4:16 For Israel hath behaved himself stubbornly
Hosea4:17 Ephraim is joined to idols; let him alone.
Hosea4:18 Their drink is become sour; they play the harlot continually; her rulers dearly love shame.
Hosea4:19 The wind hath wrapped her up in its wings; and they shall be put to shame because of their sacrifices.
Hosea5:1 Hear this
Hosea5:2 And the revolters are gone deep in making slaughter; but I am a rebuker of them all.
Hosea5:3 I know Ephraim
Hosea5:4 Their doings will not suffer them to turn unto their God; for the spirit of whoredom is within them
Hosea5:5 And the pride of Israel doth testify to his face: therefore Israel and Ephraim shall stumble in their iniquity; Judah also shall stumble with them.
Hosea5:6 They shall go with their flocks and with their herds to seek Jehovah; but they shall not find him: he hath withdrawn himself from them.
Hosea5:7 They have dealt treacherously against Jehovah; for they have borne strange children: now shall the new moon devour them with their fields.
Hosea5:8 Blow ye the cornet in Gibeah
Hosea5:9 Ephraim shall become a desolation in the day of rebuke: among the tribes of Israel have I made known that which shall surely be.
Hosea5:10 The princes of Judah are like them that remove the landmark: I will pour out my wrath upon them like water.
Hosea5:11 Ephraim is oppressed
Hosea5:12 Therefore am I unto Ephraim as a moth
Hosea5:13 When Ephraim saw his sickness
Hosea5:14 For I will be unto Ephraim as a lion
Hosea5:15 I will go and return to my place
Hosea6:1 Come
Hosea6:2 After two days will he revive us: on the third day he will raise us up
Hosea6:3 And let us know
Hosea6:4 O Ephraim
Hosea6:5 Therefore have I hewed them by the prophets; I have slain them by the words of my mouth: and thy judgments are as the light that goeth forth.
Hosea6:6 For I desire goodness
Hosea6:7 But they like Adam have transgressed the covenant: there have they dealt treacherously against me.
Hosea6:8 Gilead is a city of them that work iniquity; it is stained with blood.
Hosea6:9 And as troops of robbers wait for a man
Hosea6:10 In the house of Israel I have seen a horrible thing: there whoredom is found in Ephraim
Hosea6:11 Also
Hosea7:1 When I would heal Israel
Hosea7:2 And they consider not in their hearts that I remember all their wickedness: now have their own doings beset them about; they are before my face.
Hosea7:3 They make the king glad with their wickedness
Hosea7:4 They are all adulterers; they are as an oven heated by the baker; he ceaseth to stir the fire
Hosea7:5 On the day of our king the princes made themselves sick with the heat of wine; he stretched out his hand with scoffers.
Hosea7:6 For they have made ready their heart like an oven
Hosea7:7 They are all hot as an oven
Hosea7:8 Ephraim
Hosea7:9 Strangers have devoured his strength
Hosea7:10 And the pride of Israel doth testify to his face: yet they have not returned unto Jehovah their God
Hosea7:11 And Ephraim is like a silly dove
Hosea7:12 When they shall go
Hosea7:13 Woe unto them! for they have wandered from me; destruction unto them! for they have trespassed against me: though I would redeem them
Hosea7:14 And they have not cried unto me with their heart
Hosea7:15 Though I have taught and strengthened their arms
Hosea7:16 They return
Hosea8:1 Set the trumpet to thy mouth. As an eagle he cometh against the house of Jehovah
Hosea8:2 They shall cry unto me
Hosea8:3 Israel hath cast off that which is good: the enemy shall pursue him.
Hosea8:4 They have set up kings
Hosea8:5 He hath cast off thy calf
Hosea8:6 For from Israel is even this; the workman made it
Hosea8:7 For they sow the wind
Hosea8:8 Israel is swallowed up: now are they among the nations as a vessel wherein none delighteth.
Hosea8:9 For they are gone up to Assyria
Hosea8:10 Yea
Hosea8:11 Because Ephraim hath multiplied altars for sinning
Hosea8:12 I wrote for him the ten thousand things of my law; but they are counted as a strange thing.
Hosea8:13 As for the sacrifices of mine offerings
Hosea8:14 For Israel hath forgotten his Maker
Hosea9:1 Rejoice not
Hosea9:2 The threshing-floor and the winepress shall not feed them
Hosea9:3 They shall not dwell in Jehovahs land; but Ephraim shall return to Egypt
Hosea9:4 They shall not pour out wine-offerings to Jehovah
Hosea9:5 What will ye do in the day of solemn assembly
Hosea9:6 For
Hosea9:7 The days of visitation are come
Hosea9:8 Ephraim was a watchman with my God: as for the prophet
Hosea9:9 They have deeply corrupted themselves
Hosea9:10 I found Israel like grapes in the wilderness; I saw your fathers as the first-ripe in the fig-tree at its first season: but they came to Baal-peor
Hosea9:11 As for Ephraim
Hosea9:12 Though they bring up their children
Hosea9:13 Ephraim
Hosea9:14 Give them
Hosea9:15 All their wickedness is in Gilgal; for there I hated them: because of the wickedness of their doings I will drive them out of my house; I will love them no more; all their princes are revolters.
Hosea9:16 Ephraim is smitten
Hosea9:17 My God will cast them away
Hosea10:1 Israel is a luxuriant vine
Hosea10:2 Their heart is divided; now shall they be found guilty: he will smite their altars
Hosea10:3 Surely now shall they say
Hosea10:4 They speak vain words
Hosea10:5 The inhabitants of Samaria shall be in terror for the calves of Beth-aven; for the people thereof shall mourn over it
Hosea10:6 It also shall be carried unto Assyria for a present to king Jareb: Ephraim shall receive shame
Hosea10:7 As for Samaria
Hosea10:8 The high places also of Aven
Hosea10:9 O Israel
Hosea10:10 When it is my desire
Hosea10:11 And Ephraim is a heifer that is taught
Hosea10:12 Sow to yourselves in righteousness
Hosea10:13 Ye have plowed wickedness
Hosea10:14 Therefore shall a tumult arise among thy people
Hosea10:15 So shall Beth-el do unto you because of your great wickedness: at daybreak shall the king of Israel be utterly cut off.
Hosea11:1 When Israel was a child
Hosea11:2 The more the prophets called them
Hosea11:3 Yet I taught Ephraim to walk; I took them on my arms; but they knew not that I healed them.
Hosea11:4 I drew them with cords of a man
Hosea11:5 They shall not return into the land of Egypt; but the Assyrian shall be their king
Hosea11:6 And the sword shall fall upon their cities
Hosea11:7 And my people are bent on backsliding from me: though they call them to him that is on high
Hosea11:8 How shall I give thee up
Hosea11:9 I will not execute the fierceness of mine anger
Hosea11:10 They shall walk after Jehovah
Hosea11:11 They shall come trembling as a bird out of Egypt
Hosea11:12 Ephraim compasseth me about with falsehood
Hosea12:1 Ephraim feedeth on wind
Hosea12:2 Jehovah hath also a controversy with Judah
Hosea12:3 In the womb he took his brother by the heel; and in his manhood he had power with God:
Hosea12:4 yea
Hosea12:5 even Jehovah
Hosea12:6 Therefore turn thou to thy God: keep kindness and justice
Hosea12:7 He is a trafficker
Hosea12:8 And Ephraim said
Hosea12:9 But I am Jehovah thy God from the land of Egypt; I will yet again make thee to dwell in tents
Hosea12:10 I have also spoken unto the prophets
Hosea12:11 Is Gilead iniquity? they are altogether false; in Gilgal they sacrifice bullocks; yea
Hosea12:12 And Jacob fled into the field of Aram
Hosea12:13 And by a prophet Jehovah brought Israel up out of Egypt
Hosea12:14 Ephraim hath provoked to anger most bitterly: therefore shall his blood be left upon him
Hosea13:1 When Ephraim spake
Hosea13:2 And now they sin more and more
Hosea13:3 Therefore they shall be as the morning cloud
Hosea13:4 Yet I am Jehovah thy God from the land of Egypt; and thou shalt know no god but me
Hosea13:5 I did know thee in the wilderness
Hosea13:6 According to their pasture
Hosea13:7 Therefore am I unto them as a lion; as a leopard will I watch by the way;
Hosea13:8 I will meet them as a bear that is bereaved of her whelps
Hosea13:9 It is thy destruction
Hosea13:10 Where now is thy king
Hosea13:11 I have given thee a king in mine anger
Hosea13:12 The iniquity of Ephraim is bound up; his sin is laid up in store.
Hosea13:13 The sorrows of a travailing woman shall come upon him: he is an unwise son; for it is time he should not tarry in the place of the breaking forth of children.
Hosea13:14 I will ransom them from the power of Sheol; I will redeem them from death: O death
Hosea13:15 Though he be fruitful among his brethren
Hosea13:16 Samaria shall bear her guilt; for she hath rebelled against her God: they shall fall by the sword; their infants shall be dashed in pieces
Hosea14:1 O Israel
Hosea14:2 Take with you words
Hosea14:3 Assyria shall not save us; we will not ride upon horses; neither will we say any more to the work of our hands
Hosea14:4 I will heal their backsliding
Hosea14:5 I will be as the dew unto Israel; he shall blossom as the lily
Hosea14:6 His branches shall spread
Hosea14:7 They that dwell under his shadow shall return; they shall revive as the grain
Hosea14:8 Ephraim shall say
Hosea14:9 Who is wise
Joel1:1 The word of Jehovah that came to Joel the son of Pethuel.
Joel1:2 Hear this
Joel1:3 Tell ye your children of it
Joel1:4 That which the palmer-worm hath left hath the locust eaten; and that which the locust hath left hath the canker-worm eaten; and that which the canker-worm hath left hath the caterpillar eaten.
Joel1:5 Awake
Joel1:6 For a nation is come up upon my land
Joel1:7 He hath laid my vine waste
Joel1:8 Lament like a virgin girded with sackcloth for the husband of her youth.
Joel1:9 The meal-offering and the drink-offering are cut off from the house of Jehovah; the priests
Joel1:10 The field is laid waste
Joel1:11 Be confounded
Joel1:12 The vine is withered
Joel1:13 Gird yourselves with sackcloth
Joel1:14 Sanctify a fast
Joel1:15 Alas for the day! for the day of Jehovah is at hand
Joel1:16 Is not the food cut off before our eyes
Joel1:17 The seeds rot under their clods; the garners are laid desolate
Joel1:18 How do the beasts groan! the herds of cattle are perplexed
Joel1:19 O Jehovah
Joel1:20 Yea
Joel2:1 Blow ye the trumpet in Zion
Joel2:2 a day of darkness and gloominess
Joel2:3 A fire devoureth before them; and behind them a flame burneth: the land is as the garden of Eden before them
Joel2:4 The appearance of them is as the appearance of horses; and as horsemen
Joel2:5 Like the noise of chariots on the tops of the mountains do they leap
Joel2:6 At their presence the peoples are in anguish; all faces are waxed pale.
Joel2:7 They run like mighty men; they climb the wall like men of war; and they march every one on his ways
Joel2:8 Neither doth one thrust another; they march every one in his path; and they burst through the weapons
Joel2:9 They leap upon the city; they run upon the wall; they climb up into the houses; they enter in at the windows like a thief.
Joel2:10 The earth quaketh before them; the heavens tremble; the sun and the moon are darkened
Joel2:11 And Jehovah uttereth his voice before his army; for his camp is very great; for he is strong that executeth his word; for the day of Jehovah is great and very terrible; and who can abide it?
Joel2:12 Yet even now
Joel2:13 and rend your heart
Joel2:14 Who knoweth whether he will not turn and repent
Joel2:15 Blow the trumpet in Zion
Joel2:16 gather the people
Joel2:17 Let the priests
Joel2:18 Then was Jehovah jealous for his land
Joel2:19 And Jehovah answered and said unto his people
Joel2:20 but I will remove far off from you the northern army
Joel2:21 Fear not
Joel2:22 Be not afraid
Joel2:23 Be glad then
Joel2:24 And the floors shall be full of wheat
Joel2:25 And I will restore to you the years that the locust hath eaten
Joel2:26 And ye shall eat in plenty and be satisfied
Joel2:27 And ye shall know that I am in the midst of Israel
Joel2:28 And it shall come to pass afterward
Joel2:29 and also upon the servants and upon the handmaids in those days will I pour out my Spirit.
Joel2:30 And I will show wonders in the heavens and in the earth: blood
Joel2:31 The sun shall be turned into darkness
Joel2:32 And it shall come to pass
Joel3:1 For
Joel3:2 I will gather all nations
Joel3:3 and have cast lots for my people
Joel3:4 Yea
Joel3:5 Forasmuch as ye have taken my silver and my gold
Joel3:6 and have sold the children of Judah and the children of Jerusalem unto the sons of the Grecians
Joel3:7 behold
Joel3:8 and I will sell your sons and your daughters into the hand of the children of Judah
Joel3:9 Proclaim ye this among the nations; prepare war; stir up the mighty men; let all the men of war draw near
Joel3:10 Beat your plowshares into swords
Joel3:11 Haste ye
Joel3:12 Let the nations bestir themselves
Joel3:13 Put ye in the sickle; for the harvest is ripe: come
Joel3:14 Multitudes
Joel3:15 The sun and the moon are darkened
Joel3:16 And Jehovah will roar from Zion
Joel3:17 So shall ye know that I am Jehovah your God
Joel3:18 And it shall come to pass in that day
Joel3:19 Egypt shall be a desolation
Joel3:20 But Judah shall abide for ever
Joel3:21 And I will cleanse their blood
Amos1:1 The words of Amos
Amos1:2 And he said
Amos1:3 Thus saith Jehovah: For three transgressions of Damascus
Amos1:4 but I will send a fire into the house of Hazael
Amos1:5 And I will break the bar of Damascus
Amos1:6 Thus saith Jehovah: For three transgressions of Gaza
Amos1:7 but I will send a fire on the wall of Gaza
Amos1:8 And I will cut off the inhabitant from Ashdod
Amos1:9 Thus saith Jehovah: For three transgressions of Tyre
Amos1:10 but I will send a fire on the wall of Tyre
Amos1:11 Thus saith Jehovah: For three transgressions of Edom
Amos1:12 but I will send a fire upon Teman
Amos1:13 Thus saith Jehovah: For three transgressions of the children of Ammon
Amos1:14 But I will kindle a fire in the wall of Rabbah
Amos1:15 and their king shall go into captivity
Amos2:1 Thus saith Jehovah: For three transgressions of Moab
Amos2:2 but I will send a fire upon Moab
Amos2:3 and I will cut off the judge from the midst thereof
Amos2:4 Thus saith Jehovah: For three transgressions of Judah
Amos2:5 but I will send a fire upon Judah
Amos2:6 Thus saith Jehovah: For three transgressions of Israel
Amos2:7 they that pant after the dust of the earth on the head of the poor
Amos2:8 and they lay themselves down beside every altar upon clothes taken in pledge; and in the house of their God they drink the wine of such as have been fined.
Amos2:9 Yet destroyed I the Amorite before them
Amos2:10 Also I brought you up out of the land of Egypt
Amos2:11 And I raised up of your sons for prophets
Amos2:12 But ye gave the Nazirites wine to drink
Amos2:13 Behold
Amos2:14 And flight shall perish from the swift; and the strong shall not strengthen his force; neither shall the mighty deliver himself;
Amos2:15 neither shall he stand that handleth the bow; and he that is swift of foot shall not deliver himself; neither shall he that rideth the horse deliver himself;
Amos2:16 and he that is courageous among the mighty shall flee away naked in that day
Amos3:1 Hear this word that Jehovah hath spoken against you
Amos3:2 You only have I known of all the families of the earth: therefore I will visit upon you all your iniquities.
Amos3:3 Shall two walk together
Amos3:4 Will a lion roar in the forest
Amos3:5 Can a bird fall in a snare upon the earth
Amos3:6 Shall the trumpet be blown in a city
Amos3:7 Surely the Lord Jehovah will do nothing
Amos3:8 The lion hath roared; who will not fear? The Lord Jehovah hath spoken; who can but prophesy?
Amos3:9 Publish ye in the palaces at Ashdod
Amos3:10 For they know not to do right
Amos3:11 Therefore thus saith the Lord Jehovah: An adversary there shall be
Amos3:12 Thus saith Jehovah: As the shepherd rescueth out of the mouth of the lion two legs
Amos3:13 Hear ye
Amos3:14 For in the day that I shall visit the transgressions of Israel upon him
Amos3:15 And I will smite the winter-house with the summer-house; and the houses of ivory shall perish
Amos4:1 Hear this word
Amos4:2 The Lord Jehovah hath sworn by his holiness
Amos4:3 And ye shall go out at the breaches
Amos4:4 Come to Beth-el
Amos4:5 and offer a sacrifice of thanksgiving of that which is leavened
Amos4:6 And I also have given you cleanness of teeth in all your cities
Amos4:7 And I also have withholden the rain from you
Amos4:8 So two or three cities wandered unto one city to drink water
Amos4:9 I have smitten you with blasting and mildew: the multitude of your gardens and your vineyards and your fig-trees and your olive-trees hath the palmer-worm devoured: yet have ye not returned unto me
Amos4:10 I have sent among you the pestilence after the manner of Egypt: your young men have I slain with the sword
Amos4:11 I have overthrown cities among you
Amos4:12 Therefore thus will I do unto thee
Amos4:13 For
Amos5:1 Hear ye this word which I take up for a lamentation over you
Amos5:2 The virgin of Israel is fallen; she shall no more rise: she is cast down upon her land; there is none to raise her up.
Amos5:3 For thus saith the Lord Jehovah: The city that went forth a thousand shall have a hundred left
Amos5:4 For thus saith Jehovah unto the house of Israel
Amos5:5 but seek not Beth-el
Amos5:6 Seek Jehovah
Amos5:7 Ye who turn justice to wormwood
Amos5:8 seek him that maketh the Pleiades and Orion
Amos5:9 that bringeth sudden destruction upon the strong
Amos5:10 They hate him that reproveth in the gate
Amos5:11 Forasmuch therefore as ye trample upon the poor
Amos5:12 For I know how manifold are your transgressions
Amos5:13 Therefore he that is prudent shall keep silence in such a time; for it is an evil time.
Amos5:14 Seek good
Amos5:15 Hate the evil
Amos5:16 Therefore thus saith Jehovah
Amos5:17 And in all vineyards shall be wailing; for I will pass through the midst of thee
Amos5:18 Woe unto you that desire the day of Jehovah! Wherefore would ye have the day of Jehovah? It is darkness
Amos5:19 As if a man did flee from a lion
Amos5:20 Shall not the day of Jehovah be darkness
Amos5:21 I hate
Amos5:22 Yea
Amos5:23 Take thou away from me the noise of thy songs; for I will not hear the melody of thy viols.
Amos5:24 But let justice roll down as waters
Amos5:25 Did ye bring unto me sacrifices and offerings in the wilderness forty years
Amos5:26 Yea
Amos5:27 Therefore will I cause you to go into captivity beyond Damascus

Amos6:2 Pass ye unto Calneh
Amos6:3 -ye that put far away the evil day
Amos6:4 that lie upon beds of ivory
Amos6:5 that sing idle songs to the sound of the viol; that invent for themselves instruments of music
Amos6:6 that drink wine in bowls

Amos6:11 For

Amos6:13 ye that rejoice in a thing of nought
Amos6:14 For

Amos7:2 And it came to pass that

Amos9:12 that they may possess the remnant of Edom
Amos9:13 Behold
Amos9:14 And I will bring back the captivity of my people Israel
Amos9:15 And I will plant them upon their land
Obadiah1:1 The vision of Obadiah. Thus saith the Lord Jehovah concerning Edom: We have heard tidings from Jehovah
Obadiah1:2 Behold
Obadiah1:3 The pride of thy heart hath deceived thee
Obadiah1:4 Though thou mount on high as the eagle
Obadiah1:5 If thieves came to thee
Obadiah1:6 How are the things of Esau searched! how are his hidden treasures sought out!
Obadiah1:7 All the men of thy confederacy have brought thee on thy way
Obadiah1:8 Shall I not in that day
Obadiah1:9 And thy mighty men
Obadiah1:10 For the violence done to thy brother Jacob
Obadiah1:11 In the day that thou stoodest on the other side
Obadiah1:12 But look not thou on the day of thy brother in the day of his disaster
Obadiah1:13 Enter not into the gate of my people in the day of their calamity; yea
Obadiah1:14 And stand thou not in the crossway
Obadiah1:15 For the day of Jehovah is near upon all the nations: as thou hast done
Obadiah1:16 For as ye have drunk upon my holy mountain
Obadiah1:17 But in mount Zion there shall be those that escape
Obadiah1:18 And the house of Jacob shall be a fire
Obadiah1:19 And they of the South shall possess the mount of Esau
Obadiah1:20 And the captives of this host of the children of Israel
Obadiah1:21 And saviours shall come up on mount Zion to judge the mount of Esau; and the kingdom shall be Jehovahs.
Jonah1:1 Now the word of Jehovah came unto Jonah the son of Amittai
Jonah1:2 Arise
Jonah1:3 But Jonah rose up to flee unto Tarshish from the presence of Jehovah; and he went down to Joppa
Jonah1:4 But Jehovah sent out a great wind upon the sea
Jonah1:5 Then the mariners were afraid
Jonah1:6 So the shipmaster came to him
Jonah1:7 And they said every one to his fellow
Jonah1:8 Then said they unto him
Jonah1:9 And he said unto them
Jonah1:10 Then were the men exceedingly afraid
Jonah1:11 Then said they unto him
Jonah1:12 And he said unto them
Jonah1:13 Nevertheless the men rowed hard to get them back to the land; but they could not: for the sea grew more and more tempestuous against them.
Jonah1:14 Wherefore they cried unto Jehovah
Jonah1:15 So they took up Jonah
Jonah1:16 Then the men feared Jehovah exceedingly; and they offered a sacrifice unto Jehovah
Jonah1:17 And Jehovah prepared a great fish to swallow up Jonah; and Jonah was in the belly of the fish three days and three nights.
Jonah2:1 Then Jonah prayed unto Jehovah his God out of the fishs belly.
Jonah2:2 And he said
Jonah2:3 For thou didst cast me into the depth
Jonah2:4 And I said
Jonah2:5 The waters compassed me about
Jonah2:6 I went down to the bottoms of the mountains; The earth with its bars closed upon me for ever: Yet hast thou brought up my life from the pit
Jonah2:7 When my soul fainted within me
Jonah2:8 They that regard lying vanities Forsake their own mercy.
Jonah2:9 But I will sacrifice unto thee with the voice of thanksgiving; I will pay that which I have vowed. Salvation is of Jehovah.
Jonah2:10 And Jehovah spake unto the fish

Jonah3:2 Arise
Jonah3:3 So Jonah arose
Jonah3:4 And Jonah began to enter into the city a days journey
Jonah3:5 And the people of Nineveh believed God; and they proclaimed a fast
Jonah3:6 And the tidings reached the king of Nineveh
Jonah3:7 And he made proclamation and published through Nineveh by the decree of the king and his nobles
Jonah3:8 but let them be covered with sackcloth
Jonah3:9 Who knoweth whether God will not turn and repent
Jonah3:10 And God saw their works

Jonah4:2 And he prayed unto Jehovah

Jonah4:5 Then Jonah went out of the city

Jonah4:7 But God prepared a worm when the morning rose the next day
Jonah4:8 And it came to pass
Jonah4:9 And God said to Jonah
Jonah4:10 And Jehovah said
Jonah4:11 and should not I have regard for Nineveh
Micah1:1 The word of Jehovah that came to Micah the Morashtite in the days of Jotham
Micah1:2 Hear
Micah1:3 For
Micah1:4 And the mountains shall be melted under him
Micah1:5 For the transgression of Jacob is all this
Micah1:6 Therefore I will make Samaria as a heap of the field
Micah1:7 And all her graven images shall be beaten to pieces
Micah1:8 For this will I lament and wail; I will go stripped and naked; I will make a wailing like the jackals
Micah1:9 For her wounds are incurable; for it is come even unto Judah; it reacheth unto the gate of my people
Micah1:10 Tell it not in Gath
Micah1:11 Pass away
Micah1:12 For the inhabitant of Maroth waiteth anxiously for good

Micah7:2 The godly man is perished out of the earth

Nahum1:1 The burden of Nineveh. The book of the vision of Nahum the Elkoshite.

Nahum1:4 He rebuketh the sea
Nahum1:5 The mountains quake at him

Nahum1:8 But with an over-running flood he will make a full end of her place

Nahum1:14 And Jehovah hath given commandment concerning thee
Nahum1:15 Behold

Nahum2:2 For Jehovah restoreth the excellency of Jacob
Nahum2:3 The shield of his mighty men is made red
Nahum2:4 The chariots rage in the streets; they rush to and fro in the broad ways: the appearance of them is like torches; they run like the lightnings.

Nahum2:7 And it is decreed: she is uncovered

Nahum2:10 She is empty

Nahum2:12 The lion did tear in pieces enough for his whelps

Nahum3:8 Art thou better than No-amon
Nahum3:9 Ethiopia and Egypt were her strength
Nahum3:10 Yet was she carried away
Nahum3:11 Thou also shalt be drunken; thou shalt be hid; thou also shalt seek a stronghold because of the enemy.
Nahum3:12 All thy fortresses shall be like fig-trees with the first-ripe figs: if they be shaken
Nahum3:13 Behold
Nahum3:14 Draw thee water for the siege; strengthen thy fortresses; go into the clay
Nahum3:15 There shall the fire devour thee; the sword shall cut thee off; it shall devour thee like the canker-worm: make thyself many as the canker-worm; make thyself many as the locust.
Nahum3:16 Thou hast multiplied thy merchants above the stars of heaven: the canker-worm ravageth
Nahum3:17 Thy princes are as the locusts
Nahum3:18 Thy shepherds slumber
Nahum3:19 There is no assuaging of thy hurt: thy wound is grievous: all that hear the report of thee clap their hands over thee; for upon whom hath not thy wickedness passed continually?

Matthew1:1 The book of the generation of Jesus Christ
Matthew1:2 Abraham begat Isaac; and Isaac begat Jacob; and Jacob begat Judah and his brethren;
Matthew1:3 and Judah begat Perez and Zerah of Tamar; and Perez begat Hezron; and Hezron begat Ram;
Matthew1:4 and Ram begat Amminadab; and Amminadab begat Nahshon; and Nahshon begat Salmon;
Matthew1:5 and Salmon begat Boaz of Rahab; and Boaz begat Obed of Ruth; and Obed begat Jesse;
Matthew1:6 and Jesse begat David the king. And David begat Solomon of her that had been the wife of Uriah;
Matthew1:7 and Solomon begat Rehoboam; and Rehoboam begat Abijah; and Abijah begat Asa;
Matthew1:8 and Asa begat Jehoshaphat; and Jehoshaphat begat Joram; and Joram begat Uzziah;
Matthew1:9 and Uzziah begat Jotham; and Jotham begat Ahaz; and Ahaz begat Hezekiah;
Matthew1:10 and Hezekiah begat Manasseh; and Manasseh begat Amon; and Amon begat Josiah;
Matthew1:11 and Josiah begat Jechoniah and his brethren
Matthew1:12 And after the carrying away to Babylon
Matthew1:13 and Zerubbabel begat Abiud; and Abiud begat Eliakim; and Eliakim begat Azor;
Matthew1:14 and Azor begat Sadoc; and Sadoc begat Achim; and Achim begat Eliud;
Matthew1:15 and Eliud begat Eleazar; and Eleazar begat Matthan; and Matthan begat Jacob;
Matthew1:16 and Jacob begat Joseph the husband of Mary
Matthew1:17 So all the generations from Abraham unto David are fourteen generations; and from David unto the carrying away to Babylon fourteen generations; and from the carrying away to Babylon unto the Christ fourteen generations.
Matthew1:18 Now the birth of Jesus Christ was on this wise: When his mother Mary had been betrothed to Joseph
Matthew1:19 And Joseph her husband
Matthew1:20 But when he thought on these things
Matthew1:21 And she shall bring forth a son; and thou shalt call his name JESUS; for it is he that shall save his people from their sins.
Matthew1:22 Now all this is come to pass
Matthew1:23 Behold
Matthew1:24 And Joseph arose from his sleep
Matthew1:25 and knew her not till she had brought forth a son: and he called his name JESUS.

Matthew10:19 But when they deliver you up
Matthew10:20 For it is not ye that speak
Matthew10:21 And brother shall deliver up brother to death
Matthew10:22 And ye shall be hated of all men for my names sake: but he that endureth to the end

Matthew10:24 A disciple is not above his teacher
Matthew10:25 It is enough for the disciple that he be as his teacher
Matthew10:26 Fear them not therefore: for there is nothing covered
Matthew10:27 What I tell you in the darkness
Matthew10:28 And be not afraid of them that kill the body
Matthew10:29 Are not two sparrows sold for a penny? and not one of them shall fall on the ground without your Father:
Matthew10:30 but the very hairs of your head are all numbered.
Matthew10:31 Fear not therefore: ye are of more value than many sparrows.
Matthew10:32 Every one therefore who shall confess me before men
Matthew10:33 But whosoever shall deny me before men
Matthew10:34 Think not that I came to send peace on the earth: I came not to send peace
Matthew10:35 For I came to set a man at variance against his father
Matthew10:36 and a mans foes shall be they of his own household.
Matthew10:37 He that loveth father or mother more than me is not worthy of me; and he that loveth son or daughter more than me is not worthy of me.
Matthew10:38 And he that doth not take his cross and follow after me
Matthew10:39 He that findeth his life shall lose it; and he that loseth his life for my sake shall find it.
Matthew10:40 He that receiveth you receiveth me
Matthew10:41 He that receiveth a prophet in the name of a prophet shall receive a prophets reward: and he that receiveth a righteous man in the name of a righteous man shall receive a righteous mans reward.
Matthew10:42 And whosoever shall give to drink unto one of these little ones a cup of cold water only

Matthew11:30 For my yoke is easy
Matthew12:1 At that season Jesus went on the sabbath day through the grainfields; and his disciples were hungry and began to pluck ears and to eat.
Matthew12:2 But the Pharisees
Matthew12:3 But he said unto them
Matthew12:4 how he entered into the house of God
Matthew12:5 Or have ye not read in the law
Matthew12:6 But I say unto you
Matthew12:7 But if ye had known what this meaneth
Matthew12:8 For the Son of man is lord of the sabbath.
Matthew12:9 And he departed thence
Matthew12:10 and behold
Matthew12:11 And he said unto them
Matthew12:12 How much then is a man of more value than a sheep! Wherefore it is lawful to do good on the sabbath day.
Matthew12:13 Then saith he to the man
Matthew12:14 But the Pharisees went out
Matthew12:15 And Jesus perceiving it withdrew from thence: and many followed him; and he healed them all
Matthew12:16 and charged them that they should not make him known:
Matthew12:17 that it might be fulfilled which was spoken through Isaiah the prophet
Matthew12:18 Behold
Matthew12:19 He shall not strive
Matthew12:20 A bruised reed shall he not break
Matthew12:21 And in his name shall the Gentiles hope.
Matthew12:22 Then was brought unto him one possessed with a demon
Matthew12:23 And all the multitudes were amazed
Matthew12:24 But when the Pharisees heard it
Matthew12:25 And knowing their thoughts he said unto them
Matthew12:26 and if Satan casteth out Satan
Matthew12:27 And if I by Beelzebub cast out demons
Matthew12:28 But if I by the Spirit of God cast out demons
Matthew12:29 Or how can one enter into the house of the strong man
Matthew12:30 He that is not with me is against me
Matthew12:31 Therefore I say unto you
Matthew12:32 And whosoever shall speak a word against the Son of man
Matthew12:33 Either make the tree good
Matthew12:34 Ye offspring of vipers
Matthew12:35 The good man out of his good treasure bringeth forth good things: and the evil man out of his evil treasure bringeth forth evil things.
Matthew12:36 And I say unto you
Matthew12:37 For by thy words thou shalt be justified
Matthew12:38 Then certain of the scribes and Pharisees answered him
Matthew12:39 But he answered and said unto them
Matthew12:40 for as Jonah was three days and three nights in the belly of the whale; so shall the Son of man be three days and three nights in the heart of the earth.
Matthew12:41 The men of Nineveh shall stand up in the judgment with this generation
Matthew12:42 The queen of the south shall rise up in the judgment with this generation
Matthew12:43 But the unclean spirit
Matthew12:44 Then he saith
Matthew12:45 Then goeth he
Matthew12:46 While he was yet speaking to the multitudes
Matthew12:47 And one said unto him
Matthew12:48 But he answered and said unto him that told him
Matthew12:49 And he stretched forth his hand towards his disciples
Matthew12:50 For whosoever shall do the will of my Father who is in heaven
Matthew13:1 On that day went Jesus out of the house
Matthew13:2 And there were gathered unto him great multitudes
Matthew13:3 And he spake to them many things in parables
Matthew13:4 and as he sowed
Matthew13:5 and others fell upon the rocky places
Matthew13:6 and when the sun was risen
Matthew13:7 And others fell upon the thorns; and the thorns grew up and choked them:
Matthew13:8 and others fell upon the good ground
Matthew13:9 He that hath ears
Matthew13:10 And the disciples came
Matthew13:11 And he answered and said unto them
Matthew13:12 For whosoever hath
Matthew13:13 Therefore speak I to them in parables; because seeing they see not
Matthew13:14 And unto them is fulfilled the prophecy of Isaiah
Matthew13:15 For this peoples heart is waxed gross
Matthew13:16 But blessed are your eyes
Matthew13:17 For verily I say unto you
Matthew13:18 Hear then ye the parable of the sower.
Matthew13:19 When any one heareth the word of the kingdom
Matthew13:20 And he that was sown upon the rocky places
Matthew13:21 yet hath he not root in himself
Matthew13:22 And he that was sown among the thorns
Matthew13:23 And he that was sown upon the good ground
Matthew13:24 Another parable set he before them
Matthew13:25 but while men slept
Matthew13:26 But when the blade sprang up and brought forth fruit
Matthew13:27 And the servants of the householder came and said unto him
Matthew13:28 And he said unto them
Matthew13:29 But he saith
Matthew13:30 Let both grow together until the harvest: and in the time of the harvest I will say to the reapers
Matthew13:31 Another parable set he before them
Matthew13:32 which indeed is less than all seeds; but when it is grown
Matthew13:33 Another parable spake he unto them; The kingdom of heaven is like unto leaven
Matthew13:34 All these things spake Jesus in parables unto the multitudes; and without a parable spake he nothing unto them:
Matthew13:35 that it might be fulfilled which was spoken through the prophet
Matthew13:36 Then he left the multitudes
Matthew13:37 And he answered and said
Matthew13:38 and the field is the world; and the good seed
Matthew13:39 and the enemy that sowed them is the devil: and the harvest is the end of the world; and the reapers are angels.
Matthew13:40 As therefore the tares are gathered up and burned with fire; so shall it be in the end of the world.
Matthew13:41 The Son of man shall send forth his angels
Matthew13:42 and shall cast them into the furnace of fire: there shall be the weeping and the gnashing of teeth.
Matthew13:43 Then shall the righteous shine forth as the sun in the kingdom of their Father. He that hath ears
Matthew13:44 The kingdom of heaven is like unto a treasure hidden in the field; which a man found
Matthew13:45 Again
Matthew13:46 and having found one pearl of great price
Matthew13:47 Again
Matthew13:48 which
Matthew13:49 So shall it be in the end of the world: the angels shall come forth
Matthew13:50 and shall cast them into the furnace of fire: there shall be the weeping and the gnashing of teeth.
Matthew13:51 Have ye understood all these things? They say unto him
Matthew13:52 And he said unto them
Matthew13:53 And it came to pass
Matthew13:54 And coming into his own country he taught them in their synagogue
Matthew13:55 Is not this the carpenters son? is not his mother called Mary? and his brethren
Matthew13:56 And his sisters
Matthew13:57 And they were offended in him. But Jesus said unto them
Matthew13:58 And he did not many mighty works there because of their unbelief.

Matthew15:2 Why do thy disciples transgress the tradition of the elders? for they wash not their hands when they eat bread.
Matthew15:3 And he answered and said unto them

Matthew15:5 But ye say
Matthew15:6 he shall not honor his father. And ye have made void the word of God because of your tradition.
Matthew15:7 Ye hypocrites

Matthew15:9 But in vain do they worship me
Matthew15:10 And he called to him the multitude

Matthew15:12 Then came the disciples
Matthew15:13 But he answered and said
Matthew15:14 Let them alone: they are blind guides. And if the blind guide the blind
Matthew15:15 And Peter answered and said unto him
Matthew15:16 And he said
Matthew15:17 Perceive ye not

Matthew15:20 these are the things which defile the man; but to eat with unwashen hands defileth not the man.

Matthew18:9 And if thine eye causeth thee to stumble
Matthew18:10 See that ye despise not one of these little ones; for I say unto you

Matthew24:1 And Jesus went out from the temple
Matthew24:2 But he answered and said unto them
Matthew24:3 And as he sat on the mount of Olives
Matthew24:4 And Jesus answered and said unto them
Matthew24:5 For many shall come in my name
Matthew24:6 And ye shall hear of wars and rumors of wars; see that ye be not troubled: for these things must needs come to pass; but the end is not yet.
Matthew24:7 For nation shall rise against nation
Matthew24:8 But all these things are the beginning of travail.
Matthew24:9 Then shall they deliver you up unto tribulation
Matthew24:10 And then shall many stumble
Matthew24:11 And many false prophets shall arise
Matthew24:12 And because iniquity shall be multiplied
Matthew24:13 But he that endureth to the end
Matthew24:14 And this gospel of the kingdom shall be preached in the whole world for a testimony unto all the nations; and then shall the end come.
Matthew24:15 When therefore ye see the abomination of desolation
Matthew24:16 then let them that are in Judaea flee unto the mountains:
Matthew24:17 let him that is on the housetop not go down to take out things that are in his house:
Matthew24:18 and let him that is in the field not return back to take his cloak.
Matthew24:19 But woe unto them that are with child and to them that give suck in those days!
Matthew24:20 And pray ye that your flight be not in the winter
Matthew24:21 for then shall be great tribulation
Matthew24:22 And except those days had been shortened
Matthew24:23 Then if any man shall say unto you
Matthew24:24 For there shall arise false Christs
Matthew24:25 Behold
Matthew24:26 If therefore they shall say unto you
Matthew24:27 For as the lightning cometh forth from the east
Matthew24:28 Wheresoever the carcase is
Matthew24:29 But immediately after the tribulation of those days the sun shall be darkened
Matthew24:30 and then shall appear the sign of the Son of man in heaven: and then shall all the tribes of the earth mourn
Matthew24:31 And he shall send forth his angels with a great sound of a trumpet
Matthew24:32 Now from the fig tree learn her parable: when her branch is now become tender
Matthew24:33 even so ye also
Matthew24:34 Verily I say unto you
Matthew24:35 Heaven and earth shall pass away
Matthew24:36 But of that day and hour knoweth no one
Matthew24:37 And as were the days of Noah
Matthew24:38 For as in those days which were before the flood they were eating and drinking
Matthew24:39 and they knew not until the flood came
Matthew24:40 Then shall two man be in the field; one is taken
Matthew24:41 two women shall be grinding at the mill; one is taken
Matthew24:42 Watch therefore: for ye know not on what day your Lord cometh.
Matthew24:43 But know this
Matthew24:44 Therefore be ye also ready; for in an hour that ye think not the Son of man cometh.
Matthew24:45 Who then is the faithful and wise servant
Matthew24:46 Blessed is that servant
Matthew24:47 Verily I say unto you
Matthew24:48 But if that evil servant shall say in his heart
Matthew24:49 and shall begin to beat his fellow-servants
Matthew24:50 the lord of that servant shall come in a day when he expecteth not
Matthew24:51 and shall cut him asunder

Matthew25:2 And five of them were foolish
Matthew25:3 For the foolish
Matthew25:4 but the wise took oil in their vessels with their lamps.

Matthew25:6 But at midnight there is a cry
Matthew25:7 Then all those virgins arose
Matthew25:8 And the foolish said unto the wise
Matthew25:9 But the wise answered
Matthew25:10 And while they went away to buy
Matthew25:11 Afterward came also the other virgins
Matthew25:12 But he answered and said
Matthew25:13 Watch therefore
Matthew25:14 For it is as when a man

Matthew25:16 Straightway he that received the five talents went and traded with them
Matthew25:17 In like manner he also that received the two gained other two.
Matthew25:18 But he that received the one went away and digged in the earth
Matthew25:19 Now after a long time the lord of those servants cometh
Matthew25:20 And he that received the five talents came and brought other five talents
Matthew25:21 His lord said unto him
Matthew25:22 And he also that received the two talents came and said

Matthew25:28 Take ye away therefore the talent from him

Matthew25:32 and before him shall be gathered all the nations: and he shall separate them one from another

Matthew25:35 for I was hungry
Matthew25:36 naked

Matthew25:39 And when saw we thee sick
Matthew25:40 And the King shall answer and say unto them

Matthew25:42 for I was hungry
Matthew25:43 I was a stranger
Matthew25:44 Then shall they also answer
Matthew25:45 Then shall he answer them
Matthew25:46 And these shall go away into eternal punishment: but the righteous into eternal life.
Matthew26:1 And it came to pass

Mark1:1 The beginning of the gospel of Jesus Christ
Mark1:2 Even as it is written in Isaiah the prophet
Mark1:3 The voice of one crying in the wilderness
Mark1:4 John came
Mark1:5 And there went out unto him all the country of Judaea
Mark1:6 And John was clothed with camels hair
Mark1:7 And he preached
Mark1:8 I baptized you in water; But he shall baptize you in the Holy Spirit.
Mark1:9 And it came to pass in those days
Mark1:10 And straightway coming up out of the water
Mark1:11 And a voice came out of the heavens
Mark1:12 And straightway the Spirit driveth him forth into the wilderness.
Mark1:13 And he was in the wilderness forty days tempted of Satan; And he was with the wild beasts; And the angels ministered unto him.
Mark1:14 Now after John was delivered up

Mark1:17 And Jesus said unto them

Mark1:31 and he came and took her by the hand
Mark1:32 And at even
Mark1:33 And all the city was gathered together at the door.

John4:38 I sent you to reap that whereon ye have not labored: others have labored

John5:1 After these things there was a feast of the Jews; and Jesus went up to Jerusalem.
John5:2 Now there is in Jerusalem by the sheep gate a pool
John5:3 In these lay a multitude of them that were sick

John5:15 The man went away
John5:16 And for this cause the Jews persecuted Jesus
John5:17 But Jesus answered them

John5:19 Jesus therefore answered and said unto them
John5:20 For the Father loveth the Son
John5:21 For as the Father raiseth the dead and giveth them life
John5:22 For neither doth the Father judge any man
John5:23 that all may honor the Son
John5:24 Verily
John5:25 Verily
John5:26 For as the Father hath life in himself
John5:27 and he gave him authority to execute judgment
