John5:29 and shall come forth; they that have done good
John5:30 I can of myself do nothing: as I hear
John5:31 If I bear witness of myself
John5:32 It is another that beareth witness of me; and I know that the witness which he witnesseth of me is true.
John5:33 Ye have sent unto John
John5:34 But the witness which I receive is not from man: howbeit I say these things
John5:35 He was the lamp that burneth and shineth; and ye were willing to rejoice for a season in his light.
John5:36 But the witness which I have is greater than that of John; for the works which the Father hath given me to accomplish
John5:37 And the Father that sent me
John5:38 And ye have not his word abiding in you: for whom he sent
John5:39 Ye search the scriptures
John5:40 and ye will not come to me

John5:47 But if ye believe not his writings

Romans2:1 Wherefore thou art without excuse
Romans2:2 And we know that the judgment of God is according to truth against them that practise such things.
Romans2:3 And reckonest thou this
Romans2:4 Or despisest thou the riches of his goodness and forbearance and longsuffering
Romans2:5 but after thy hardness and impenitent heart treasurest up for thyself wrath in the day of wrath and revelation of the righteous judgment of God;
Romans2:6 who will render to every man according to his works:
Romans2:7 to them that by patience in well-doing seek for glory and honor and incorruption
Romans2:8 but unto them that are factious
Romans2:9 tribulation and anguish
Romans2:10 but glory and honor and peace to every man that worketh good
Romans2:11 for there is no respect of persons with God.
Romans2:12 For as many as have sinned without law shall also perish without the law: and as many as have sinned under the law shall be judged by the law;
Romans2:13 for not the hearers of the law are just before God
Romans2:14 (for when Gentiles that have not the law do by nature the things of the law
Romans2:15 in that they show the work of the law written in their hearts
Romans2:16 in the day when God shall judge the secrets of men
Romans2:17 But if thou bearest the name of a Jew
Romans2:18 and knowest his will
Romans2:19 and art confident that thou thyself art a guide of the blind

Romans2:22 thou that sayest a man should not commit adultery
Romans2:23 thou who gloriest in the law
Romans2:24 For the name of God is blasphemed among the Gentiles because of you
Romans2:25 For circumcision indeed profiteth
Romans2:26 If therefore the uncircumcision keep the ordinances of the law
Romans2:27 and shall not the uncircumcision which is by nature
Romans2:28 For he is not a Jew who is one outwardly; neither is that circumcision which is outward in the flesh:
Romans2:29 but he is a Jew who is one inwardly; and circumcision is that of the heart
Romans3:1 What advantage then hath the Jew? or what is the profit of circumcision?
Romans3:2 Much every way: first of all
Romans3:3 For what if some were without faith? shall their want of faith make of none effect the faithfulness of God?
Romans3:4 God forbid: yea
Romans3:5 But if our righteousness commendeth the righteousness of God
Romans3:6 God forbid: for then how shall God judge the world?
Romans3:7 But if the truth of God through my lie abounded unto his glory
Romans3:8 and why not (as we are slanderously reported
Romans3:9 What then? are we better than they? No
Romans3:10 as it is written
Romans3:11 There is none that understandeth
Romans3:12 They have all turned aside
Romans3:13 Their throat is an open sepulchre; With their tongues they have used deceit: The poison of asps is under their lips:
Romans3:14 Whose mouth is full of cursing and bitterness:
Romans3:15 Their feet are swift to shed blood;
Romans3:16 Destruction and misery are in their ways;
Romans3:17 And the way of peace have they not known:
Romans3:18 There is no fear of God before their eyes.
Romans3:19 Now we know that what things soever the law saith
Romans3:20 because by the works of the law shall no flesh be justified in his sight; for through the law cometh the knowledge of sin.
Romans3:21 But now apart from the law a righteousness of God hath been manifested
Romans3:22 even the righteousness of God through faith in Jesus Christ unto all them that believe; for there is no distinction;
Romans3:23 for all have sinned
Romans3:24 being justified freely by his grace through the redemption that is in Christ Jesus:
Romans3:25 whom God set forth to be a propitiation
Romans3:26 for the showing
Romans3:27 Where then is the glorying? It is excluded. By what manner of law? of works? Nay: but by a law of faith.
Romans3:28 We reckon therefore that a man is justified by faith apart from the works of the law.
Romans3:29 Or is God the God of Jews only? is he not the God of Gentiles also? Yea
Romans3:30 if so be that God is one
Romans3:31 Do we then make the law of none effect through faith? God forbid: nay

Romans6:1 What shall we say then? Shall we continue in sin
Romans6:2 God forbid. We who died to sin
Romans6:3 Or are ye ignorant that all we who were baptized into Christ Jesus were baptized into his death?
Romans6:4 We were buried therefore with him through baptism unto death: that like as Christ was raised from the dead through the glory of the Father
Romans6:5 For if we have become united with him in the likeness of his death
Romans6:6 knowing this
Romans6:7 for he that hath died is justified from sin.
Romans6:8 But if we died with Christ
Romans6:9 knowing that Christ being raised from the dead dieth no more; death no more hath dominion over him.
Romans6:10 For the death that he died
Romans6:11 Even so reckon ye also yourselves to be dead unto sin
Romans6:12 Let not sin therefore reign in your mortal body
Romans6:13 neither present your members unto sin as instruments of unrighteousness; but present yourselves unto God
Romans6:14 For sin shall not have dominion over you: for ye are not under law
Romans6:15 What then? shall we sin
Romans6:16 Know ye not
Romans6:17 But thanks be to God
Romans6:18 and being made free from sin
Romans6:19 I speak after the manner of men because of the infirmity of your flesh: for as ye presented your members as servants to uncleanness and to iniquity unto iniquity
Romans6:20 For when ye were servants of sin
Romans6:21 What fruit then had ye at that time in the things whereof ye are now ashamed? for the end of those things is death.
Romans6:22 But now being made free from sin and become servants to God
Romans6:23 For the wages of sin is death; but the free gift of God is eternal life in Christ Jesus our Lord.

Romans8:1 There is therefore now no condemnation to them that are in Christ Jesus.
Romans8:2 For the law of the Spirit of life in Christ Jesus made me free from the law of sin and of death.
Romans8:3 For what the law could not do
Romans8:4 that the ordinance of the law might be fulfilled in us
Romans8:5 For they that are after the flesh mind the things of the flesh; but they that are after the Spirit the things of the Spirit.
Romans8:6 For the mind of the flesh is death; but the mind of the Spirit is life and peace:
Romans8:7 because the mind of the flesh is enmity against God; for it is not subject to the law of God
Romans8:8 and they that are in the flesh cannot please God.
Romans8:9 But ye are not in the flesh but in the Spirit
Romans8:10 And if Christ is in you

Romans8:15 For ye received not the spirit of bondage again unto fear; but ye received the spirit of adoption

Romans8:17 and if children
Romans8:18 For I reckon that the sufferings of this present time are not worthy to be compared with the glory which shall be revealed to us-ward.

Romans8:20 For the creation was subjected to vanity
Romans8:21 that the creation itself also shall be delivered from the bondage of corruption into the liberty of the glory of the children of God.
Romans8:22 For we know that the whole creation groaneth and travaileth in pain together until now.
Romans8:23 And not only so
Romans8:24 For in hope were we saved: but hope that is seen is not hope: for who hopeth for that which he seeth?
Romans8:25 But if we hope for that which we see not
Romans8:26 And in like manner the Spirit also helpeth our infirmity: for we know not how to pray as we ought; but the Spirit himself maketh intercession for us with groanings which cannot be uttered;
Romans8:27 and he that searcheth the hearts knoweth what is the mind of the Spirit
Romans8:28 And we know that to them that love God all things work together for good

1Corinthians11:12 For as the woman is of the man
1Corinthians11:13 Judge ye in yourselves: is it seemly that a woman pray unto God unveiled?
1Corinthians11:14 Doth not even nature itself teach you
1Corinthians11:15 But if a woman have long hair
1Corinthians11:16 But if any man seemeth to be contentious
1Corinthians11:17 But in giving you this charge
1Corinthians11:18 For first of all
1Corinthians11:19 For there must be also factions among you
1Corinthians11:20 When therefore ye assemble yourselves together
1Corinthians11:21 for in your eating each one taketh before other his own supper; and one is hungry
1Corinthians11:22 What
1Corinthians11:23 For I received of the Lord that which also I delivered unto you
1Corinthians11:24 and when he had given thanks
1Corinthians11:25 In like manner also the cup
1Corinthians11:26 For as often as ye eat this bread
1Corinthians11:27 Wherefore whosoever shall eat the bread or drink the cup of the Lord in an unworthy manner
1Corinthians11:28 But let a man prove himself
1Corinthians11:29 For he that eateth and drinketh
1Corinthians11:30 For this cause many among you are weak and sickly
1Corinthians11:31 But if we discerned ourselves
1Corinthians11:32 But when we are judged
1Corinthians11:33 Wherefore
1Corinthians11:34 If any man is hungry
1Corinthians12:1 Now concerning spiritual gifts
1Corinthians12:2 Ye know that when ye were Gentiles ye were led away unto those dumb idols
1Corinthians12:3 Wherefore I make known unto you
1Corinthians12:4 Now there are diversities of gifts
1Corinthians12:5 And there are diversities of ministrations
1Corinthians12:6 And there are diversities of workings
1Corinthians12:7 But to each one is given the manifestation of the Spirit to profit withal.
1Corinthians12:8 For to one is given through the Spirit the word of wisdom; and to another the word of knowledge
1Corinthians12:9 to another faith
1Corinthians12:10 and to another workings of miracles; and to another prophecy; and to another discernings of spirits; to another divers kinds of tongues; and to another the interpretation of tongues:
1Corinthians12:11 but all these worketh the one and the same Spirit
1Corinthians12:12 For as the body is one
1Corinthians12:13 For in one Spirit were we all baptized into one body
1Corinthians12:14 For the body is not one member
1Corinthians12:15 If the foot shall say
1Corinthians12:16 And if the ear shall say
1Corinthians12:17 If the whole body were an eye
1Corinthians12:18 But now hath God set the members each one of them in the body
1Corinthians12:19 And if they were all one member
1Corinthians12:20 But now they are many members
1Corinthians12:21 And the eye cannot say to the hand
1Corinthians12:22 Nay
1Corinthians12:23 and those parts of the body
1Corinthians12:24 whereas our comely parts have no need: but God tempered the body together
1Corinthians12:25 that there should be no schism in the body; but that the members should have the same care one for another.
1Corinthians12:26 And whether one member suffereth
1Corinthians12:27 Now ye are the body of Christ
1Corinthians12:28 And God hath set some in the church
1Corinthians12:29 Are all apostles? are all prophets? are all teachers? are all workers of miracles?
1Corinthians12:30 have all gifts of healings? do all speak with tongues? do all interpret?
1Corinthians12:31 But desire earnestly the greater gifts. And moreover a most excellent way show I unto you.
1Corinthians13:1 If I speak with the tongues of men and of angels
1Corinthians13:2 And if I have the gift of prophecy
1Corinthians13:3 And if I bestow all my goods to feed the poor
1Corinthians13:4 Love suffereth long
1Corinthians13:5 doth not behave itself unseemly
1Corinthians13:6 rejoiceth not in unrighteousness
1Corinthians13:7 beareth all things
1Corinthians13:8 Love never faileth: but whether there be prophecies
1Corinthians13:9 For we know in part
1Corinthians13:10 but when that which is perfect is come
1Corinthians13:11 When I was a child
1Corinthians13:12 For now we see in a mirror
1Corinthians13:13 But now abideth faith
1Corinthians14:1 Follow after love; yet desire earnestly spiritual gifts
1Corinthians14:2 For he that speaketh in a tongue speaketh not unto men
1Corinthians14:3 But he that prophesieth speaketh unto men edification
1Corinthians14:4 He that speaketh in a tongue edifieth himself; but he that prophesieth edifieth the church.
1Corinthians14:5 Now I would have you all speak with tongues
1Corinthians14:6 But now
1Corinthians14:7 Even things without life
1Corinthians14:8 For if the trumpet give an uncertain voice
1Corinthians14:9 So also ye
1Corinthians14:10 There are
1Corinthians14:11 If then I know not the meaning of the voice
1Corinthians14:12 So also ye
1Corinthians14:13 Wherefore let him that speaketh in a tongue pray that he may interpret.
1Corinthians14:14 For if I pray in a tongue
1Corinthians14:15 What is it then? I will pray with the spirit
1Corinthians14:16 Else if thou bless with the spirit
1Corinthians14:17 For thou verily givest thanks well
1Corinthians14:18 I thank God
1Corinthians14:19 howbeit in the church I had rather speak five words with my understanding
1Corinthians14:20 Brethren
1Corinthians14:21 In the law it is written
1Corinthians14:22 Wherefore tongues are for a sign
1Corinthians14:23 If therefore the whole church be assembled together and all speak with tongues
1Corinthians14:24 But if all prophesy
1Corinthians14:25 the secrets of his heart are made manifest; and so he will fall down on his face and worship God
1Corinthians14:26 What is it then
1Corinthians14:27 If any man speaketh in a tongue
1Corinthians14:28 but if there be no interpreter
1Corinthians14:29 And let the prophets speak by two or three
1Corinthians14:30 But if a revelation be made to another sitting by
1Corinthians14:31 For ye all can prophesy one by one
1Corinthians14:32 and the spirits of the prophets are subject to the prophets;
1Corinthians14:33 for God is not a God of confusion
1Corinthians14:34 let the women keep silence in the churches: for it is not permitted unto them to speak; but let them be in subjection
1Corinthians14:35 And if they would learn anything
1Corinthians14:36 What? was it from you that the word of God went forth? or came it unto you alone?
1Corinthians14:37 If any man thinketh himself to be a prophet
1Corinthians14:38 But if any man is ignorant
1Corinthians14:39 Wherefore
1Corinthians14:40 But let all things be done decently and in order.
1Corinthians15:1 Now I make known unto you brethren
1Corinthians15:2 by which also ye are saved
1Corinthians15:3 For I delivered unto you first of all that which also I received: that Christ died for our sins according to the scriptures;
1Corinthians15:4 and that he was buried; and that he hath been raised on the third day according to the scriptures;
1Corinthians15:5 and that he appeared to Cephas; then to the twelve;
1Corinthians15:6 then he appeared to above five hundred brethren at once
1Corinthians15:7 then he appeared to James; then to all the apostles;
1Corinthians15:8 and last of all
1Corinthians15:9 For I am the least of the apostles
1Corinthians15:10 But by the grace of God I am what I am: and his grace which was bestowed upon me was not found vain; but I labored more abundantly than they all: yet not I
1Corinthians15:11 Whether then it be I or they
1Corinthians15:12 Now if Christ is preached that he hath been raised from the dead
1Corinthians15:13 But if there is no resurrection of the dead
1Corinthians15:14 and if Christ hath not been raised
1Corinthians15:15 Yea
1Corinthians15:16 For if the dead are not raised
1Corinthians15:17 and if Christ hath not been raised
1Corinthians15:18 Then they also that are fallen asleep in Christ have perished.
1Corinthians15:19 If we have only hoped in Christ in this life
1Corinthians15:20 But now hath Christ been raised from the dead
1Corinthians15:21 For since by man came death
1Corinthians15:22 For as in Adam all die
1Corinthians15:23 But each in his own order: Christ the firstfruits; then they that are Christs
1Corinthians15:24 Then cometh the end
1Corinthians15:25 For he must reign
1Corinthians15:26 The last enemy that shall be abolished is death.
1Corinthians15:27 For
1Corinthians15:28 And when all things have been subjected unto him
1Corinthians15:29 Else what shall they do that are baptized for the dead? If the dead are not raised at all
1Corinthians15:30 Why do we also stand in jeopardy every hour?
1Corinthians15:31 I protest by that glorifying in you
1Corinthians15:32 If after the manner of men I fought with beasts at Ephesus
1Corinthians15:33 Be not deceived: Evil companionships corrupt good morals.
1Corinthians15:34 Awake to soberness righteously
1Corinthians15:35 But some one will say
1Corinthians15:36 Thou foolish one
1Corinthians15:37 and that which thou sowest
1Corinthians15:38 but God giveth it a body even as it pleased him
1Corinthians15:39 All flesh is not the same flesh: but there is one flesh of men
1Corinthians15:40 There are also celestial bodies
1Corinthians15:41 There is one glory of the sun
1Corinthians15:42 So also is the resurrection of the dead. It is sown in corruption; it is raised in incorruption:
1Corinthians15:43 it is sown in dishonor; it is raised in glory: it is sown in weakness; it is raised in power:
1Corinthians15:44 it is sown a natural body; it is raised a spiritual body. If there is a natural body
1Corinthians15:45 So also it is written
1Corinthians15:46 Howbeit that is not first which is spiritual
1Corinthians15:47 The first man is of the earth
1Corinthians15:48 As is the earthy
1Corinthians15:49 And as we have borne the image of the earthy
1Corinthians15:50 Now this I say
1Corinthians15:51 Behold
1Corinthians15:52 in a moment
1Corinthians15:53 For this corruptible must put on incorruption
1Corinthians15:54 But when this corruptible shall have put on incorruption
1Corinthians15:55 O death
1Corinthians15:56 The sting of death is sin; and the power of sin is the law:
1Corinthians15:57 but thanks be to God
1Corinthians15:58 Wherefore
1Corinthians16:1 Now concerning the collection for the saints
1Corinthians16:2 Upon the first day of the week let each one of you lay by him in store
1Corinthians16:3 And when I arrive
1Corinthians16:4 and if it be meet for me to go also
1Corinthians16:5 But I will come unto you
1Corinthians16:6 but with you it may be that I shall abide
1Corinthians16:7 For I do not wish to see you now by the way; for I hope to tarry a while with you
1Corinthians16:8 But I will tarry at Ephesus until Pentecost;
1Corinthians16:9 for a great door and effectual is opened unto me
1Corinthians16:10 Now if Timothy come
1Corinthians16:11 let no man therefore despise him. But set him forward on his journey in peace
1Corinthians16:12 But as touching Apollos the brother
1Corinthians16:13 Watch ye
1Corinthians16:14 Let all that ye do be done in love.
1Corinthians16:15 Now I beseech you
1Corinthians16:16 that ye also be in subjection unto such
1Corinthians16:17 And I rejoice at the coming of Stephanas and Fortunatus and Achaicus: for that which was lacking on your part they supplied.
1Corinthians16:18 For they refreshed my spirit and yours: acknowledge ye therefore them that are such.
1Corinthians16:19 The churches of Asia salute you. Aquila and Prisca salute you much in the Lord
1Corinthians16:20 All the brethren salute you. Salute one another with a holy kiss.
1Corinthians16:21 The salutation of me Paul with mine own hand.
1Corinthians16:22 If any man loveth not the Lord
1Corinthians16:23 The grace of the Lord Jesus Christ be with you.
1Corinthians16:24 My love be with you all in Christ Jesus. Amen.

2Corinthians2:1 But I determined this for myself
2Corinthians2:2 For if I make you sorry
2Corinthians2:3 And I wrote this very thing
2Corinthians2:4 For out of much affliction and anguish of heart I wrote unto you with many tears; not that ye should be made sorry
2Corinthians2:5 But if any hath caused sorrow
2Corinthians2:6 Sufficient to such a one is this punishment which was inflicted by the many;
2Corinthians2:7 so that contrariwise ye should rather forgive him and comfort him
2Corinthians2:8 Wherefore I beseech you to confirm your love toward him.
2Corinthians2:9 For to this end also did I write
2Corinthians2:10 But to whom ye forgive anything
2Corinthians2:11 that no advantage may be gained over us by Satan: for we are not ignorant of his devices.
2Corinthians2:12 Now when I came to Troas for the gospel of Christ
2Corinthians2:13 I had no relief for my spirit
2Corinthians2:14 But thanks be unto God
2Corinthians2:15 For we are a sweet savor of Christ unto God
2Corinthians2:16 to the one a savor from death unto death; to the other a savor from life unto life. And who is sufficient for these things?
2Corinthians2:17 For we are not as the many

2Corinthians11:1 Would that ye could bear with me in a little foolishness: but indeed ye do bear with me.
2Corinthians11:2 For I am jealous over you with a godly jealousy: for I espoused you to one husband
2Corinthians11:3 But I fear
2Corinthians11:4 For if he that cometh preacheth another Jesus
2Corinthians11:5 For I reckon that I am not a whit behind the very chiefest apostles.
2Corinthians11:6 But though I be rude in speech
2Corinthians11:7 Or did I commit a sin in abasing myself that ye might be exalted
2Corinthians11:8 I robbed other churches
2Corinthians11:9 and when I was present with you and was in want
2Corinthians11:10 As the truth of Christ is in me
2Corinthians11:11 Wherefore? because I love you not? God knoweth.
2Corinthians11:12 But what I do
2Corinthians11:13 For such men are false apostles
2Corinthians11:14 And no marvel; for even Satan fashioneth himself into an angel of light.
2Corinthians11:15 It is no great thing therefore if his ministers also fashion themselves as ministers of righteousness
2Corinthians11:16 I say again
2Corinthians11:17 That which I speak
2Corinthians11:18 Seeing that many glory after the flesh
2Corinthians11:19 For ye bear with the foolish gladly
2Corinthians11:20 For ye bear with a man
2Corinthians11:21 I speak by way of disparagement
2Corinthians11:22 Are they Hebrews? so am I. Are they Israelites? so am I. Are they the seed of Abraham? so am I.
2Corinthians11:23 Are they ministers of Christ? (I speak as one beside himself) I more; in labors more abundantly
2Corinthians11:24 Of the Jews five times received I forty stripes save one.
2Corinthians11:25 Thrice was I beaten with rods
2Corinthians11:26 in journeyings often
2Corinthians11:27 in labor and travail

2Corinthians11:29 Who is weak

2Corinthians12:2 I know a man in Christ
2Corinthians12:3 And I know such a man (whether in the body
2Corinthians12:4 how that he was caught up into Paradise
2Corinthians12:5 On behalf of such a one will I glory: but on mine own behalf I will not glory
2Corinthians12:6 For if I should desire to glory
2Corinthians12:7 And by reason of the exceeding greatness of the revelations
2Corinthians12:8 Concerning this thing I besought the Lord thrice
2Corinthians12:9 And he hath said unto me

2Corinthians12:11 I am become foolish: ye compelled me; for I ought to have been commended of you: for in nothing was I behind the very chiefest apostles
2Corinthians12:12 Truly the signs of an apostle were wrought among you in all patience
2Corinthians12:13 For what is there wherein ye were made inferior to the rest of the churches
2Corinthians12:14 Behold
2Corinthians12:15 And I will most gladly spend and be spent for your souls. If I love you more abundantly
2Corinthians12:16 But be it so
2Corinthians12:17 Did I take advantage of you by any one of them whom I have sent unto you?
2Corinthians12:18 I exhorted Titus
2Corinthians12:19 Ye think all this time that we are excusing ourselves unto you. In the sight of God speak we in Christ. But all things
2Corinthians12:20 For I fear
2Corinthians12:21 lest again when I come my God should humble me before you
2Corinthians13:1 This is the third time I am coming to you. At the mouth of two witnesses or three shall every word established.
2Corinthians13:2 I have said beforehand
2Corinthians13:3 seeing that ye seek a proof of Christ that speaketh in me; who to you-ward is not weak
2Corinthians13:4 for he was crucified through weakness
2Corinthians13:5 Try your own selves
2Corinthians13:6 But I hope that ye shall know that we are not reprobate.
2Corinthians13:7 Now we pray to God that ye do no evil; not that we may appear approved
2Corinthians13:8 For we can do nothing against the truth
2Corinthians13:9 For we rejoice
2Corinthians13:10 For this cause I write these things while absent
2Corinthians13:11 Finally
2Corinthians13:12 Salute one another with a holy kiss.
2Corinthians13:13 All the saints salute you.
2Corinthians13:14 The grace of the Lord Jesus Christ
Galatians1:1 Paul
Galatians1:2 and all the brethren that are with me
Galatians1:3 Grace to you and peace from God the Father
Galatians1:4 who gave himself for our sins
Galatians1:5 to whom be the glory for ever and ever. Amen.
Galatians1:6 I marvel that ye are so quickly removing from him that called you in the grace of Christ unto a different gospel;
Galatians1:7 which is not another gospel only there are some that trouble you
Galatians1:8 But though we
Galatians1:9 As we have said before
Galatians1:10 For am I now seeking the favor of men
Galatians1:11 For I make known to you
Galatians1:12 For neither did I receive it from man
Galatians1:13 For ye have heard of my manner of life in time past in the Jews religion
Galatians1:14 and I advanced in the Jews religion beyond many of mine own age among my countrymen
Galatians1:15 But when it was the good pleasure of God
Galatians1:16 to reveal his Son in me
Galatians1:17 neither went I up to Jerusalem to them that were apostles before me: but I went away into Arabia; and again I returned unto Damascus.
Galatians1:18 Then after three years I went up to Jerusalem to visit Cephas
Galatians1:19 But other of the apostles saw I none
Galatians1:20 Now touching the things which I write unto you
Galatians1:21 Then I came unto the regions of Syria and Cilicia.
Galatians1:22 And I was still unknown by face unto the churches of Judaea which were in Christ:
Galatians1:23 but they only heard say
Galatians1:24 and they glorified God in me.
Galatians2:1 Then after the space of fourteen years I went up again to Jerusalem with Barnabas
Galatians2:2 And I went up by revelation; and I laid before them the gospel which I preach among the Gentiles but privately before them who were of repute
Galatians2:3 But not even Titus who was with me
Galatians2:4 and that because of the false brethren privily brought in
Galatians2:5 to whom we gave place in the way of subjection
Galatians2:6 But from those who were reputed to be somewhat (whatsoever they were
Galatians2:7 but contrariwise
Galatians2:8 (for he that wrought for Peter unto the apostleship of the circumcision wrought for me also unto the Gentiles);
Galatians2:9 and when they perceived the grace that was given unto me
Galatians2:10 only they would that we should remember the poor; which very thing I was also zealous to do.
Galatians2:11 But when Cephas came to Antioch
Galatians2:12 For before that certain came from James
Galatians2:13 And the rest of the Jews dissembled likewise with him; insomuch that even Barnabas was carried away with their dissimulation.
Galatians2:14 But when I saw that they walked not uprightly according to the truth of the gospel
Galatians2:15 We being Jews by nature
Galatians2:16 yet knowing that a man is not justified by the works of the law but through faith in Jesus Christ
Galatians2:17 But if
Galatians2:18 For if I build up again those things which I destroyed
Galatians2:19 For I through the law died unto the law
Galatians2:20 I have been crucified with Christ; and it is no longer I that live
Galatians2:21 I do not make void the grace of God: for if righteousness is through the law
Galatians3:1 O foolish Galatians
Galatians3:2 This only would I learn from you. Received ye the Spirit by the works of the law
Galatians3:3 Are ye so foolish? having begun in the Spirit
Galatians3:4 Did ye suffer so many things in vain? if it be indeed in vain.
Galatians3:5 He therefore that supplieth to you the Spirit
Galatians3:6 Even as Abraham believed God
Galatians3:7 Know therefore that they that are of faith
Galatians3:8 And the scripture
Galatians3:9 So then they that are of faith are blessed with the faithful Abraham.
Galatians3:10 For as many as are of the works of the law are under a curse: for it is written
Galatians3:11 Now that no man is justified by the law before God
Galatians3:12 and the law is not of faith; but
Galatians3:13 Christ redeemed us from the curse of the law
Galatians3:14 that upon the Gentiles might come the blessing of Abraham in Christ Jesus; that we might receive the promise of the Spirit through faith.
Galatians3:15 Brethren
Galatians3:16 Now to Abraham were the promises spoken
Galatians3:17 Now this I say: A covenant confirmed beforehand by God
Galatians3:18 For if the inheritance is of the law
Galatians3:19 What then is the law? It was added because of transgressions
Galatians3:20 Now a mediator is not a mediator of one; but God is one.
Galatians3:21 Is the law then against the promises of God? God forbid: for if there had been a law given which could make alive
Galatians3:22 But the scriptures shut up all things under sin
Galatians3:23 But before faith came
Galatians3:24 So that the law is become our tutor to bring us unto Christ
Galatians3:25 But now faith that is come
Galatians3:26 For ye are all sons of God
Galatians3:27 For as many of you as were baptized into Christ did put on Christ.
Galatians3:28 There can be neither Jew nor Greek
Galatians3:29 And if ye are Christs
Galatians4:1 But I say that so long as the heir is a child
Galatians4:2 but is under guardians and stewards until the day appointed of the father.
Galatians4:3 So we also
Galatians4:4 but when the fulness of the time came
Galatians4:5 that he might redeem them that were under the law
Galatians4:6 And because ye are sons
Galatians4:7 So that thou art no longer a bondservant
Galatians4:8 Howbeit at that time
Galatians4:9 but now that ye have come to know God
Galatians4:10 Ye observe days
Galatians4:11 I am afraid of you
Galatians4:12 I beseech you
Galatians4:13 but ye know that because of an infirmity of the flesh I preached the gospel unto you the first time:
Galatians4:14 and that which was a temptation to you in my flesh ye despised not
Galatians4:15 Where then is that gratulation of yourselves? for I bear you witness
Galatians4:16 So then am I become your enemy
Galatians4:17 They zealously seek you in no good way; nay
Galatians4:18 But it is good to be zealously sought in a good matter at all times
Galatians4:19 My little children
Galatians4:20 but I could wish to be present with you now
Galatians4:21 Tell me
Galatians4:22 For it is written
Galatians4:23 Howbeit the son by the handmaid is born after the flesh; but the son by the freewoman is born through promise.
Galatians4:24 Which things contain an allegory: for these women are two covenants; one from mount Sinai
Galatians4:25 Now this Hagar is mount Sinai in Arabia and answereth to the Jerusalem that now is: for she is in bondage with her children.
Galatians4:26 But the Jerusalem that is above is free
Galatians4:27 For it is written
Galatians4:28 Now we
Galatians4:29 But as then he that was born after the flesh persecuted him that was born after the Spirit
Galatians4:30 Howbeit what saith the scripture? Cast out the handmaid and her son: for the son of the handmaid shall not inherit with the son of the freewoman.
Galatians4:31 Wherefore
Galatians5:1 For freedom did Christ set us free: stand fast therefore
Galatians5:2 Behold
Galatians5:3 Yea
Galatians5:4 Ye are severed from Christ
Galatians5:5 For we through the Spirit by faith wait for the hope of righteousness.
Galatians5:6 For in Christ Jesus neither circumcision availeth anything
Galatians5:7 Ye were running well; who hindered you that ye should not obey the truth?
Galatians5:8 This persuasion came not of him that calleth you.
Galatians5:9 A little leaven leaveneth the whole lump.
Galatians5:10 I have confidence to you-ward in the Lord
Galatians5:11 But I
Galatians5:12 I would that they that unsettle you would even go beyond circumcision.
Galatians5:13 For ye
Galatians5:14 For the whole law is fulfilled in one word
Galatians5:15 But if ye bite and devour one another
Galatians5:16 But I say
Galatians5:17 For the flesh lusteth against the Spirit
Galatians5:18 But if ye are led by the Spirit
Galatians5:19 Now the works of the flesh are manifest
Galatians5:20 idolatry
Galatians5:21 envyings
Galatians5:22 But the fruit of the Spirit is love
Galatians5:23 meekness
Galatians5:24 And they that are of Christ Jesus have crucified the flesh with the passions and the lusts thereof.
Galatians5:25 If we live by the Spirit
Galatians5:26 Let us not become vainglorious
Galatians6:1 Brethren
Galatians6:2 Bear ye one anothers burdens
Galatians6:3 For if a man thinketh himself to be something when he is nothing
Galatians6:4 But let each man prove his own work
Galatians6:5 For each man shall bear his own burden.
Galatians6:6 But let him that is taught in the word communicate unto him that teacheth in all good things.
Galatians6:7 Be not deceived; God is not mocked: for whatsoever a man soweth
Galatians6:8 For he that soweth unto his own flesh shall of the flesh reap corruption; but he that soweth unto the Spirit shall of the Spirit reap eternal life.
Galatians6:9 And let us not be weary in well-doing: for in due season we shall reap
Galatians6:10 So then
Galatians6:11 See with how large letters I write unto you with mine own hand.
Galatians6:12 As many as desire to make a fair show in the flesh
Galatians6:13 For not even they who receive circumcision do themselves keep the law; but they desire to have you circumcised
Galatians6:14 But far be it from me to glory
Galatians6:15 For neither is circumcision anything
Galatians6:16 And as many as shall walk by this rule
Galatians6:17 Henceforth
Galatians6:18 The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with your spirit

Ephesians3:6 to wit

Ephesians3:11 according to the eternal purpose which he purposed in Christ Jesus our Lord:
Ephesians3:12 in whom we have boldness and access in confidence through our faith in him.

Ephesians3:20 Now unto him that is able to do exceeding abundantly above all that we ask or think
Ephesians3:21 unto him be the glory in the church and in Christ Jesus unto all generations for ever and ever. Amen.

Ephesians5:2 and walk in love
Ephesians5:3 But fornication
Ephesians5:4 nor filthiness
Ephesians5:5 For this ye know of a surety
Ephesians5:6 Let no man deceive you with empty words: for because of these things cometh the wrath of God upon the sons of disobedience.
Ephesians5:7 Be not ye therefore partakers with them;
Ephesians5:8 For ye were once darkness
Ephesians5:9 (for the fruit of the light is in all goodness and righteousness and truth)
Ephesians5:10 proving what is well-pleasing unto the Lord;
Ephesians5:11 and have no fellowship with the unfruitful works of darkness
Ephesians5:12 for the things which are done by them in secret it is a shame even to speak of.
Ephesians5:13 But all things when they are reproved are made manifest by the light: for everything that is made manifest is light.
Ephesians5:14 Wherefore he saith
Ephesians5:15 Look therefore carefully how ye walk
Ephesians5:16 redeeming the time
Ephesians5:17 Wherefore be ye not foolish

Ephesians5:19 speaking one to another in psalms and hymns and spiritual songs
Ephesians5:20 giving thanks always for all things in the name of our Lord Jesus Christ to God
Ephesians5:21 subjecting yourselves one to another in the fear of Christ.
Ephesians5:22 Wives
Ephesians5:23 For the husband is the head of the wife
Ephesians5:24 But as the church is subject to Christ
Ephesians5:25 Husbands
Ephesians5:26 that he might sanctify it
Ephesians5:27 that he might present the church to himself a glorious church
Ephesians5:28 Even so ought husbands also to love their own wives as their own bodies. He that loveth his own wife loveth himself:
Ephesians5:29 for no man ever hated his own flesh; but nourisheth and cherisheth it
Ephesians5:30 because we are members of his body.
Ephesians5:31 For this cause shall a man leave his father and mother
Ephesians5:32 This mystery is great: but I speak in regard of Christ and of the church.
Ephesians5:33 Nevertheless do ye also severally love each one his own wife even as himself; and let the wife see that she fear her husband.

Philippians1:1 Paul and Timothy
Philippians1:2 Grace to you and peace from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.
Philippians1:3 I thank my God upon all my remembrance of you
Philippians1:4 always in every supplication of mine on behalf of you all making my supplication with joy
Philippians1:5 for your fellowship in furtherance of the gospel from the first day until now;
Philippians1:6 being confident of this very thing
Philippians1:7 even as it is right for me to be thus minded on behalf of you all
Philippians1:8 For God is my witness
Philippians1:9 And this I pray
Philippians1:10 so that ye may approve the things that are excellent; that ye may be sincere and void of offence unto the day of Christ;
Philippians1:11 being filled with the fruits of righteousness
Philippians1:12 Now I would have you know
Philippians1:13 so that my bonds became manifest in Christ throughout the whole praetorian guard
Philippians1:14 and that most of the brethren in the Lord
Philippians1:15 Some indeed preach Christ even of envy and strife; and some also of good will:
Philippians1:16 the one do it of love
Philippians1:17 but the other proclaim Christ of faction
Philippians1:18 What then? only that in every way
Philippians1:19 For I know that this shall turn out to my salvation
Philippians1:20 according to my earnest expectation and hope
Philippians1:21 For to me to live is Christ
Philippians1:22 But if to live in the flesh
Philippians1:23 But I am in a strait betwixt the two
Philippians1:24 yet to abide in the flesh is more needful for your sake.
Philippians1:25 And having this confidence
Philippians1:26 that your glorying may abound in Christ Jesus in me through my presence with you again.
Philippians1:27 Only let your manner of life be worthy of the gospel of Christ: that
Philippians1:28 and in nothing affrighted by the adversaries: which is for them an evident token of perdition
Philippians1:29 because to you it hath been granted in the behalf of Christ
Philippians1:30 having the same conflict which ye saw in me

Philippians2:2 make full my joy
Philippians2:3 doing nothing through faction or through vainglory

Philippians2:5 Have this mind in you
Philippians2:6 who
Philippians2:7 but emptied himself
Philippians2:8 and being found in fashion as a man
Philippians2:9 Wherefore also God highly exalted him

Philippians2:11 and that every tongue should confess that Jesus Christ is Lord
Philippians2:12 So then

Philippians2:16 holding forth the word of life; that I may have whereof to glory in the day of Christ

Philippians2:18 and in the same manner do ye also joy

Philippians2:20 For I have no man likeminded
Philippians2:21 For they all seek their own
Philippians2:22 But ye know the proof of him
Philippians2:23 Him therefore I hope to send forthwith
Philippians2:24 but I trust in the Lord that I myself also shall come shortly.
Philippians2:25 But I counted it necessary to send to you Epaphroditus
Philippians2:26 since he longed after you all
Philippians2:27 for indeed he was sick nigh unto death: but God had mercy on him; and not on him only
Philippians2:28 I have sent him therefore the more diligently
Philippians2:29 Receive him therefore in the Lord with all joy; and hold such in honor:
Philippians2:30 because for the work of Christ he came nigh unto death

Colossians1:5 because of the hope which is laid up for you in the heavens
Colossians1:6 which is come unto you; even as it is also in all the world bearing fruit and increasing

Colossians1:8 who also declared unto us your love in the Spirit.
Colossians1:9 For this cause we also
Colossians1:10 to walk worthily of the Lord unto all pleasing
Colossians1:11 strengthened with all power
Colossians1:12 giving thanks unto the Father
Colossians1:13 who delivered us out of the power of darkness
Colossians1:14 in whom we have our redemption
Colossians1:15 who is the image of the invisible God
Colossians1:16 for in him were all things created
Colossians1:17 and he is before all things
Colossians1:18 And he is the head of the body
Colossians1:19 For it was the good pleasure of the Father that in him should all the fulness dwell;
Colossians1:20 and through him to reconcile all things unto himself
Colossians1:21 And you
Colossians1:22 yet now hath he reconciled in the body of his flesh through death
Colossians1:23 if so be that ye continue in the faith
Colossians1:24 Now I rejoice in my sufferings for your sake
Colossians1:25 whereof I was made a minister
Colossians1:26 even the mystery which hath been hid for ages and generations: but now hath it been manifested to his saints
Colossians1:27 to whom God was pleased to make known what is the riches of the glory of this mystery among the Gentiles
Colossians1:28 whom we proclaim
Colossians1:29 whereunto I labor also
Colossians2:1 For I would have you know how greatly I strive for you
Colossians2:2 that their hearts may be comforted
Colossians2:3 in whom are all the treasures of wisdom and knowledge hidden.
Colossians2:4 This I say
Colossians2:5 For though I am absent in the flesh
Colossians2:6 As therefore ye received Christ Jesus the Lord
Colossians2:7 rooted and builded up in him
Colossians2:8 Take heed lest there shall be any one that maketh spoil of you through his philosophy and vain deceit
Colossians2:9 for in him dwelleth all the fulness of the Godhead bodily
Colossians2:10 and in him ye are made full
Colossians2:11 in whom ye were also circumcised with a circumcision not made with hands
Colossians2:12 having been buried with him in baptism
Colossians2:13 And you
Colossians2:14 having blotted out the bond written in ordinances that was against us
Colossians2:15 having despoiled the principalities and the powers
Colossians2:16 Let no man therefore judge you in meat
Colossians2:17 which are a shadow of the things to come; but the body is Christs.
Colossians2:18 Let no man rob you of your prize by a voluntary humility and worshipping of the angels
Colossians2:19 and not holding fast the Head
Colossians2:20 If ye died with Christ from the rudiments of the world
Colossians2:21 Handle not
Colossians2:22 (all which things are to perish with the using)
Colossians2:23 Which things have indeed a show of wisdom in will-worship
Colossians3:1 If then ye were raised together with Christ
Colossians3:2 Set your mind on the things that are above
Colossians3:3 For ye died
Colossians3:4 When Christ
Colossians3:5 Put to death therefore your members which are upon the earth: fornication
Colossians3:6 for which things sake cometh the wrath of God upon the sons of disobedience:
Colossians3:7 wherein ye also once walked
Colossians3:8 but now do ye also put them all away: anger
Colossians3:9 lie not one to another; seeing that ye have put off the old man with his doings
Colossians3:10 and have put on the new man
Colossians3:11 where there cannot be Greek and Jew
Colossians3:12 Put on therefore
Colossians3:13 forbearing one another
Colossians3:14 and above all these things put on love
Colossians3:15 And let the peace of Christ rule in your hearts
Colossians3:16 Let the word of Christ dwell in you richly; in all wisdom teaching and admonishing one another with psalms and hymns and spiritual songs
Colossians3:17 And whatsoever ye do
Colossians3:18 Wives
Colossians3:19 Husbands
Colossians3:20 Children
Colossians3:21 Fathers
Colossians3:22 Servants
Colossians3:23 whatsoever ye do
Colossians3:24 knowing that from the Lord ye shall receive the recompense of the inheritance: ye serve the Lord Christ.
Colossians3:25 For he that doeth wrong shall receive again for the wrong that he hath done: and there is no respect of persons.
Colossians4:1 Masters
Colossians4:2 Continue stedfastly in prayer
Colossians4:3 withal praying for us also
Colossians4:4 that I may make it manifest
Colossians4:5 Walk in wisdom toward them that are without
Colossians4:6 Let your speech be always with grace
Colossians4:7 All my affairs shall Tychicus make known unto you
Colossians4:8 whom I have sent you for this very purpose
Colossians4:9 together with Onesimus
Colossians4:10 Aristarchus my fellow-prisoner saluteth you
Colossians4:11 and Jesus that is called Justus
Colossians4:12 Epaphras
Colossians4:13 For I bear him witness
Colossians4:14 Luke
Colossians4:15 Salute the brethren that are in Laodicea
Colossians4:16 And when this epistle hath been read among you
Colossians4:17 And say to Archippus
Colossians4:18 The salutation of me Paul with mine own hand. Remember my bonds. Grace be with you.
1Thessalonians1:1 Paul
1Thessalonians1:2 We give thanks to God always for you all
1Thessalonians1:3 remembering without ceasing your work of faith and labor of love and patience of hope in our Lord Jesus Christ
1Thessalonians1:4 knowing
1Thessalonians1:5 how that our gospel came not unto you in word only
1Thessalonians1:6 And ye became imitators of us
1Thessalonians1:7 so that ye became an ensample to all that believe in Macedonia and in Achaia.
1Thessalonians1:8 For from you hath sounded forth the word of the Lord
1Thessalonians1:9 For they themselves report concerning us what manner of entering in we had unto you; and how ye turned unto God from idols
1Thessalonians1:10 and to wait for his Son from heaven
1Thessalonians2:1 For yourselves
1Thessalonians2:2 but having suffered before and been shamefully treated
1Thessalonians2:3 For our exhortation is not of error
1Thessalonians2:4 but even as we have been approved of God to be intrusted with the gospel
1Thessalonians2:5 For neither at any time were we found using words of flattery
1Thessalonians2:6 nor seeking glory of men
1Thessalonians2:7 But we were gentle in the midst of you
1Thessalonians2:8 even so
1Thessalonians2:9 For ye remember
1Thessalonians2:10 Ye are witnesses
1Thessalonians2:11 as ye know how we dealt with each one of you
1Thessalonians2:12 to the end that ye should walk worthily of God
1Thessalonians2:13 And for this cause we also thank God without ceasing
1Thessalonians2:14 For ye
1Thessalonians2:15 who both killed the Lord Jesus and the prophets
1Thessalonians2:16 forbidding us to speak to the Gentiles that they may be saved; to fill up their sins always: but the wrath is come upon them to the uttermost.
1Thessalonians2:17 But we
1Thessalonians2:18 because we would fain have come unto you
1Thessalonians2:19 For what is our hope
1Thessalonians2:20 For ye are our glory and our joy.
1Thessalonians3:1 Wherefore when we could no longer forbear
1Thessalonians3:2 and sent Timothy
1Thessalonians3:3 that no man be moved by these afflictions; for yourselves know that hereunto we are appointed.
1Thessalonians3:4 For verily
1Thessalonians3:5 For this cause I also
1Thessalonians3:6 But when Timothy came even now unto us from you
1Thessalonians3:7 for this cause
1Thessalonians3:8 for now we live
1Thessalonians3:9 For what thanksgiving can we render again unto God for you
1Thessalonians3:10 night and day praying exceedingly that we may see your face
1Thessalonians3:11 Now may our God and Father himself
1Thessalonians3:12 and the Lord make you to increase and abound in love one toward another
1Thessalonians3:13 to the end he may establish your hearts unblameable in holiness before our God and Father
1Thessalonians4:1 Finally then
1Thessalonians4:2 For ye know what charge we gave you through the Lord Jesus.
1Thessalonians4:3 For this is the will of God
1Thessalonians4:4 that each one of you know how to possess himself of his own vessel in sanctification and honor
1Thessalonians4:5 not in the passion of lust
1Thessalonians4:6 that no man transgress
1Thessalonians4:7 For God called us not for uncleanness
1Thessalonians4:8 Therefore he that rejecteth
1Thessalonians4:9 But concerning love of the brethren ye have no need that one write unto you: for ye yourselves are taught of God to love one another;
1Thessalonians4:10 for indeed ye do it toward all the brethren that are in all Macedonia. But we exhort you
1Thessalonians4:11 and that ye study to be quiet
1Thessalonians4:12 that ye may walk becomingly toward them that are without
1Thessalonians4:13 But we would not have you ignorant
1Thessalonians4:14 For if we believe that Jesus died and rose again
1Thessalonians4:15 For this we say unto you by the word of the Lord
1Thessalonians4:16 For the Lord himself shall descend from heaven
1Thessalonians4:17 then we that are alive
1Thessalonians4:18 Wherefore comfort one another with these words.
1Thessalonians5:1 But concerning the times and the seasons
1Thessalonians5:2 For yourselves know perfectly that the day of the Lord so cometh as a thief in the night.
1Thessalonians5:3 When they are saying
1Thessalonians5:4 But ye
1Thessalonians5:5 for ye are all sons of light
1Thessalonians5:6 so then let us not sleep
1Thessalonians5:7 For they that sleep sleep in the night: and they that are drunken are drunken in the night.
1Thessalonians5:8 But let us
1Thessalonians5:9 For God appointed us not into wrath
1Thessalonians5:10 who died for us
1Thessalonians5:11 Wherefore exhort one another
1Thessalonians5:12 But we beseech you
1Thessalonians5:13 and to esteem them exceeding highly in love for their works sake. Be at peace among yourselves.
1Thessalonians5:14 And we exhort you
1Thessalonians5:15 See that none render unto any one evil for evil; but always follow after that which is good
1Thessalonians5:16 Rejoice always;
1Thessalonians5:17 pray without ceasing;
1Thessalonians5:18 in everything give thanks: for this is the will of God in Christ Jesus to you-ward.
1Thessalonians5:19 Quench not the Spirit;
1Thessalonians5:20 despise not prophesyings;
1Thessalonians5:21 prove all things; hold fast that which is good;
1Thessalonians5:22 abstain from every form of evil.
1Thessalonians5:23 And the God of peace himself sanctify you wholly; and may your spirit and soul and body be preserved entire
1Thessalonians5:24 Faithful is he that calleth you
1Thessalonians5:25 Brethren
1Thessalonians5:26 Salute all the brethren with a holy kiss.
1Thessalonians5:27 I adjure you by the Lord that this epistle be read unto all the brethren.
1Thessalonians5:28 The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with you.
2Thessalonians1:1 Paul
2Thessalonians1:2 Grace to you and peace from God the Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.
2Thessalonians1:3 We are bound to give thanks to God always to you
2Thessalonians1:4 so that we ourselves glory in you in the churches of God for your patience and faith in all your persecutions and in the afflictions which ye endure;
2Thessalonians1:5 which is a manifest token of the righteous judgment of God; to the end that ye may be counted worthy of the kingdom of God
2Thessalonians1:6 if so be that it is righteous thing with God to recompense affliction to them that afflict you
2Thessalonians1:7 and to you that are afflicted rest with us
2Thessalonians1:8 rendering vengeance to them that know not God
2Thessalonians1:9 who shall suffer punishment
2Thessalonians1:10 when he shall come to be glorified in his saints
2Thessalonians1:11 To which end we also pray always for you
2Thessalonians1:12 that the name of our Lord Jesus may be glorified in you
2Thessalonians2:1 Now we beseech you
2Thessalonians2:2 to the end that ye be not quickly shaken from your mind
2Thessalonians2:3 let no man beguile you in any wise: for it will not be
2Thessalonians2:4 he that opposeth and exalteth himself against all that is called God or that is worshipped; so that he sitteth in the temple of God
2Thessalonians2:5 Remember ye not
2Thessalonians2:6 And now ye know that which restraineth
2Thessalonians2:7 For the mystery of lawlessness doth already work: only there is one that restraineth now
2Thessalonians2:8 And then shall be revealed the lawless one
2Thessalonians2:9 even he
2Thessalonians2:10 and with all deceit of unrighteousness for them that perish; because they received not the love of the truth
2Thessalonians2:11 And for this cause God sendeth them a working of error
2Thessalonians2:12 that they all might be judged who believed not the truth
2Thessalonians2:13 But we are bound to give thanks to God always for you
2Thessalonians2:14 whereunto he called you through our gospel
2Thessalonians2:15 So then
2Thessalonians2:16 Now our Lord Jesus Christ himself
2Thessalonians2:17 comfort your hearts and establish them in every good work and word.
2Thessalonians3:1 Finally
2Thessalonians3:2 and that we may be delivered from unreasonable and evil men; for all have not faith.
2Thessalonians3:3 But the Lord is faithful
2Thessalonians3:4 And we have confidence in the Lord touching you
2Thessalonians3:5 And the Lord direct your hearts into the love of God
2Thessalonians3:6 Now we command you
2Thessalonians3:7 For yourselves know how ye ought to imitate us: for we behaved not ourselves disorderly among you;
2Thessalonians3:8 neither did we eat bread for nought at any mans hand
2Thessalonians3:9 not because we have not the right
2Thessalonians3:10 For even when we were with you
2Thessalonians3:11 For we hear of some that walk among you disorderly
2Thessalonians3:12 Now them that are such we command and exhort in the Lord Jesus Christ
2Thessalonians3:13 But ye
2Thessalonians3:14 And if any man obeyeth not our word by this epistle
2Thessalonians3:15 And yet count him not as an enemy
2Thessalonians3:16 Now the Lord of peace himself give you peace at all times in all ways. The Lord be with you all.
2Thessalonians3:17 The salutation of me Paul with mine own hand
2Thessalonians3:18 The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with you all.
1Timothy1:1 Paul
1Timothy1:2 unto Timothy
1Timothy1:3 As I exhorted thee to tarry at Ephesus
1Timothy1:4 neither to give heed to fables and endless genealogies
1Timothy1:5 But the end of the charge is love out of a pure heart and a good conscience and faith unfeigned:
1Timothy1:6 from which things some having swerved have turned aside unto vain talking;
1Timothy1:7 desiring to be teachers of the law
1Timothy1:8 But we know that the law is good
1Timothy1:9 as knowing this
1Timothy1:10 for fornicators
1Timothy1:11 according to the gospel of the glory of the blessed God
1Timothy1:12 I thank him that enabled me
1Timothy1:13 though I was before a blasphemer
1Timothy1:14 and the grace of our Lord abounded exceedingly with faith and love which is in Christ Jesus.
1Timothy1:15 Faithful is the saying
1Timothy1:16 howbeit for this cause I obtained mercy
1Timothy1:17 Now unto the King eternal
1Timothy1:18 This charge I commit unto thee
1Timothy1:19 holding faith and a good conscience; which some having thrust from them made shipwreck concerning the faith:
1Timothy1:20 of whom is Hymenaeus and Alexander; whom I delivered unto Satan
1Timothy2:1 I exhort therefore
1Timothy2:2 for kings and all that are in high place; that we may lead a tranquil and quiet life in all godliness and gravity.
1Timothy2:3 This is good and acceptable in the sight of God our Saviour;
1Timothy2:4 who would have all men to be saved
1Timothy2:5 For there is one God
1Timothy2:6 who gave himself a ransom for all; the testimony to be borne in its own times;
1Timothy2:7 whereunto I was appointed a preacher and an apostle (I speak the truth
1Timothy2:8 I desire therefore that the men pray in every place
1Timothy2:9 In like manner
1Timothy2:10 but (which becometh women professing godliness) through good works.
1Timothy2:11 Let a woman learn in quietness with all subjection.
1Timothy2:12 But I permit not a woman to teach
1Timothy2:13 For Adam was first formed
1Timothy2:14 and Adam was not beguiled
1Timothy2:15 but she shall be saved through her child-bearing
1Timothy3:1 Faithful is the saying
1Timothy3:2 The bishop therefore must be without reproach
1Timothy3:3 no brawler
1Timothy3:4 one that ruleth well his own house
1Timothy3:5 (but if a man knoweth not how to rule his own house
1Timothy3:6 not a novice
1Timothy3:7 Moreover he must have good testimony from them that are without; lest he fall into reproach and the snare of the devil.
1Timothy3:8 Deacons in like manner must be grave
1Timothy3:9 holding the mystery of the faith in a pure conscience.
1Timothy3:10 And let these also first be proved; then let them serve as deacons
1Timothy3:11 Women in like manner must be grave
1Timothy3:12 Let deacons be husbands of one wife
1Timothy3:13 For they that have served well as deacons gain to themselves a good standing
1Timothy3:14 These things write I unto thee
1Timothy3:15 but if I tarry long
1Timothy3:16 And without controversy great is the mystery of godliness; He who was manifested in the flesh
1Timothy4:1 But the Spirit saith expressly
1Timothy4:2 through the hypocrisy of men that speak lies
1Timothy4:3 forbidding to marry
1Timothy4:4 For every creature of God is good
1Timothy4:5 for it is sanctified through the word of God and prayer.
1Timothy4:6 If thou put the brethren in mind of these things
1Timothy4:7 but refuse profane and old wives fables. And exercise thyself unto godliness:
1Timothy4:8 for bodily exercise is profitable for a little; but godliness is profitable for all things
1Timothy4:9 Faithful is the saying
1Timothy4:10 For to this end we labor and strive
1Timothy4:11 These things command and teach.
1Timothy4:12 Let no man despise thy youth; but be thou an ensample to them that believe
1Timothy4:13 Till I come
1Timothy4:14 Neglect not the gift that is in thee
1Timothy4:15 Be diligent in these things; give thyself wholly to them; that thy progress may be manifest unto all.
1Timothy4:16 Take heed to thyself
1Timothy5:1 Rebuke not an elder
1Timothy5:2 the elder women as mothers; the younger as sisters
1Timothy5:3 Honor widows that are widows indeed.
1Timothy5:4 But if any widow hath children or grandchildren
1Timothy5:5 Now she that is a widow indeed
1Timothy5:6 But she that giveth herself to pleasure is dead while she liveth.
1Timothy5:7 These things also command
1Timothy5:8 But if any provideth not for his own
1Timothy5:9 Let none be enrolled as a widow under threescore years old
1Timothy5:10 well reported of for good works; if she hath brought up children
1Timothy5:11 But younger widows refuse: for when they have waxed wanton against Christ
1Timothy5:12 having condemnation
1Timothy5:13 And withal they learn also to be idle
1Timothy5:14 I desire therefore that the younger widows marry
1Timothy5:15 for already some are turned aside after Satan.
1Timothy5:16 If any woman that believeth hath widows
1Timothy5:17 Let the elders that rule well be counted worthy of double honor
1Timothy5:18 For the scripture saith
1Timothy5:19 Against an elder receive not an accusation
1Timothy5:20 Them that sin reprove in the sight of all
1Timothy5:21 I charge thee in the sight of God
1Timothy5:22 Lay hands hastily on no man
1Timothy5:23 Be no longer a drinker of water
1Timothy5:24 Some mens sins are evident
1Timothy5:25 In like manner also there are good works that are evident; and such as are otherwise cannot be hid.
1Timothy6:1 Let as many as are servants under the yoke count their own masters worthy of all honor
1Timothy6:2 And they that have believing masters
1Timothy6:3 If any man teacheth a different doctrine
1Timothy6:4 he is puffed up
1Timothy6:5 wranglings of men corrupted in mind and bereft of the truth
1Timothy6:6 But godliness with contentment is great gain:
1Timothy6:7 for we brought nothing into the world
1Timothy6:8 but having food and covering we shall be therewith content.
1Timothy6:9 But they that are minded to be rich fall into a temptation and a snare and many foolish and hurtful lusts
1Timothy6:10 For the love of money is a root of all kinds of evil: which some reaching after have been led astray from the faith
1Timothy6:11 But thou
1Timothy6:12 Fight the good fight of the faith
1Timothy6:13 I charge thee in the sight of God
1Timothy6:14 that thou keep the commandment
1Timothy6:15 which in its own times he shall show
1Timothy6:16 who only hath immortality
1Timothy6:17 Charge them that are rich in this present world
1Timothy6:18 that they do good
1Timothy6:19 laying up in store for themselves a good foundation against the time to come
1Timothy6:20 O Timothy
1Timothy6:21 which some professing have erred concerning the faith. Grace be with you.
2Timothy1:1 Paul
2Timothy1:2 to Timothy
2Timothy1:3 I thank God
2Timothy1:4 longing to see thee
2Timothy1:5 having been reminded of the unfeigned faith that is in thee; which dwelt first in thy grandmother Lois
2Timothy1:6 For which cause I put thee in remembrance that thou stir up the gift of God
2Timothy1:7 For God gave us not a spirit of fearfulness; but of power and love and discipline.
2Timothy1:8 Be not ashamed therefore of the testimony of our Lord
2Timothy1:9 who saved us
2Timothy1:10 but hath now been manifested by the appearing of our Saviour Christ Jesus
2Timothy1:11 whereunto I was appointed a preacher
2Timothy1:12 For which cause I suffer also these things: yet I am not ashamed; for I know him whom I have believed
2Timothy1:13 Hold the pattern of sound words which thou hast heard from me
2Timothy1:14 That good thing which was committed unto thee guard through the Holy Spirit which dwelleth in us.
2Timothy1:15 This thou knowest
2Timothy1:16 The Lord grant mercy unto the house of Onesiphorus: for he oft refreshed me
2Timothy1:17 but
2Timothy1:18 (the Lord grant unto him to find mercy of the Lord in that day); and in how many things he ministered at Ephesus
2Timothy2:1 Thou therefore
2Timothy2:2 And the things which thou hast heard from me among many witnesses
2Timothy2:3 Suffer hardship with me
2Timothy2:4 No soldier on service entangleth himself in the affairs of this life; that he may please him who enrolled him as a soldier.
2Timothy2:5 And if also a man contend in the games
2Timothy2:6 The husbandmen that laboreth must be the first to partake of the fruits.
2Timothy2:7 Consider what I say; for the Lord shall give thee understanding in all things.
2Timothy2:8 Remember Jesus Christ
2Timothy2:9 wherein I suffer hardship unto bonds
2Timothy2:10 Therefore I endure all things for the elects sake
2Timothy2:11 Faithful is the saying: For if we died with him
2Timothy2:12 if we endure
2Timothy2:13 if we are faithless
2Timothy2:14 Of these things put them in remembrance
2Timothy2:15 Give diligence to present thyself approved unto God
2Timothy2:16 But shun profane babblings: for they will proceed further in ungodliness
2Timothy2:17 and their word will eat as doth a gangrene: or whom is Hymenaeus an Philetus;
2Timothy2:18 men who concerning the truth have erred
2Timothy2:19 Howbeit the firm foundation of God standeth
2Timothy2:20 Now in a great house there are not only vessels of gold and of silver
2Timothy2:21 If a man therefore purge himself from these
2Timothy2:22 after righteousness
2Timothy2:23 But foolish and ignorant questionings refuse
2Timothy2:24 And the Lords servant must not strive
2Timothy2:25 in meekness correcting them that oppose themselves; if peradventure God may give them repentance unto the knowledge of the truth
2Timothy2:26 and they may recover themselves out of the snare of the devil
2Timothy3:1 But know this
2Timothy3:2 For men shall be lovers of self
2Timothy3:3 without natural affection
2Timothy3:4 traitors
2Timothy3:5 holding a form of godliness
2Timothy3:6 For of these are they that creep into houses
2Timothy3:7 ever learning
2Timothy3:8 And even as Jannes and Jambres withstood Moses
2Timothy3:9 But they shall proceed no further. For their folly shall be evident unto all men
2Timothy3:10 But thou didst follow my teaching
2Timothy3:11 persecutions
2Timothy3:12 Yea
2Timothy3:13 But evil men and impostors shall wax worse and worse
2Timothy3:14 But abide thou in the things which thou hast learned and hast been assured of
2Timothy3:15 And that from a babe thou hast known the sacred writings which are able to make thee wise unto salvation through faith which is in Christ Jesus.
2Timothy3:16 Every scripture inspired of God is also profitable for teaching
2Timothy3:17 That the man of God may be complete
2Timothy4:1 I charge thee in the sight of God
2Timothy4:2 preach the word; be urgent in season
2Timothy4:3 For the time will come when they will not endure the sound doctrine; but
2Timothy4:4 and will turn away their ears from the truth
2Timothy4:5 But be thou sober in all things
2Timothy4:6 For I am already being offered
2Timothy4:7 I have fought the good fight
2Timothy4:8 henceforth there is laid up for me the crown of righteousness
2Timothy4:9 Give diligence to come shortly unto me:
2Timothy4:10 for Demas forsook me
2Timothy4:11 Only Luke is with me. Take Mark
2Timothy4:12 But Tychicus I sent to Ephesus.
2Timothy4:13 The cloak that I left at Troas with Carpus
2Timothy4:14 Alexander the coppersmith did me much evil: the Lord will render to him according to his works:
2Timothy4:15 of whom do thou also beware; for he greatly withstood our words.
2Timothy4:16 At my first defence no one took my part
2Timothy4:17 But the Lord stood by me
2Timothy4:18 The Lord will deliver me from every evil work
2Timothy4:19 Salute Prisca and Aquila
2Timothy4:20 Erastus remained at Corinth: but Trophimus I left at Miletus sick.
2Timothy4:21 Give diligence to come before winter. Eubulus saluteth thee
2Timothy4:22 The Lord be with thy spirit. Grace be with you.
Titus1:1 Paul
Titus1:2 in hope of eternal life
Titus1:3 but in his own seasons manifested his word in the message
Titus1:4 to Titus
Titus1:5 For this cause left I thee in Crete
Titus1:6 if any man is blameless
Titus1:7 For the bishop must be blameless
Titus1:8 but given to hospitality
Titus1:9 holding to the faithful word which is according to the teaching
Titus1:10 For there are many unruly men
Titus1:11 whose mouths must be stopped; men who overthrow whole houses
Titus1:12 One of themselves
Titus1:13 This testimony is true. For which cause reprove them sharply
Titus1:14 not giving heed to Jewish fables
Titus1:15 To the pure all things are pure: but to them that are defiled and unbelieving nothing is pure; but both their mind and their conscience are defiled.
Titus1:16 They profess that they know God; but by their works they deny him
Titus2:1 But speak thou the things which befit the sound doctrine:
Titus2:2 that aged men be temperate
Titus2:3 that aged women likewise be reverent in demeanor
Titus2:4 that they may train the young women to love their husbands
Titus2:5 to be sober-minded

Titus2:7 in all things showing thyself an ensample of good works; in thy doctrine showing uncorruptness
Titus2:8 sound speech

Titus2:10 not purloining
Titus2:11 For the grace of God hath appeared
Titus2:12 instructing us

Titus2:14 who gave himself for us
Titus2:15 These things speak and exhort and reprove with all authority. Let no man despise thee.

Titus3:2 to speak evil of no man

Titus3:6 which he poured out upon us richly
Titus3:7 that

Titus3:14 And let our people also learn to maintain good works for necessary uses

Philemon1:1 Paul
Philemon1:2 and to Apphia our sister
Philemon1:3 Grace to you and peace from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.
Philemon1:4 I thank my God always
Philemon1:5 hearing of thy love
Philemon1:6 that the fellowship of thy faith may become effectual
Philemon1:7 For I had much joy and comfort in thy love
Philemon1:8 Wherefore
Philemon1:9 yet for loves sake I rather beseech
Philemon1:10 I beseech thee for my child
Philemon1:11 who once was unprofitable to thee
Philemon1:12 whom I have sent back to thee in his own person
Philemon1:13 whom I would fain have kept with me
Philemon1:14 but without thy mind I would do nothing; that thy goodness should not be as of necessity
Philemon1:15 For perhaps he was therefore parted from thee for a season
Philemon1:16 no longer as a servant
Philemon1:17 If then thou countest me a partner
Philemon1:18 But if he hath wronged the at all
Philemon1:19 I Paul write it with mine own hand
Philemon1:20 Yea
Philemon1:21 Having confidence in thine obedience I write unto thee
Philemon1:22 But withal prepare me also a lodging: for I hope that through your prayers I shall be granted unto you.
Philemon1:23 Epaphras
Philemon1:24 and so do Mark
Philemon1:25 The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with your spirit. Amen.

Hebrews9:2 For there was a tabernacle prepared
Hebrews9:3 And after the second veil
Hebrews9:4 having a golden altar of incense
Hebrews9:5 and above it cherubim of glory overshadowing the mercy-seat; of which things we cannot now speak severally.
Hebrews9:6 Now these things having been thus prepared

Hebrews9:8 the Holy Spirit this signifying
Hebrews9:9 which is a figure for the time present; according to which are offered both gifts and sacrifices that cannot
Hebrews9:10 being only (with meats and drinks and divers washings) carnal ordinances

Hebrews9:12 nor yet through the blood of goats and calves
Hebrews9:13 For if the blood of goats and bulls
Hebrews9:14 how much more shall the blood of Christ
Hebrews9:15 And for this cause he is the mediator of a new covenant
Hebrews9:16 For where a testament is
Hebrews9:17 For a testament is of force where there hath been death: for it doth never avail while he that made it liveth.
Hebrews9:18 Wherefore even the first covenant hath not been dedicated without blood.
Hebrews9:19 For when every commandment had been spoken by Moses unto all the people according to the law

Hebrews9:22 And according to the law
Hebrews9:23 It was necessary therefore that the copies of the things in the heavens should be cleansed with these; but the heavenly things themselves with better sacrifices than these.
Hebrews9:24 For Christ entered not into a holy place made with hands
Hebrews9:25 nor yet that he should offer himself often
Hebrews9:26 else must he often have suffered since the foundation of the world: but now once at the end of the ages hath he been manifested to put away sin by the sacrifice of himself.
Hebrews9:27 And inasmuch as it is appointed unto men once to die
Hebrews9:28 so Christ also

Hebrews11:1 Now faith is assurance of things hoped for
Hebrews11:2 For therein the elders had witness borne to them.
Hebrews11:3 By faith we understand that the worlds have been framed by the word of God
Hebrews11:4 By faith Abel offered unto God a more excellent sacrifice than Cain
Hebrews11:5 By faith Enoch was translated that he should not see death; and he was not found
Hebrews11:6 And without faith it is impossible to be well-pleasing unto him; for he that cometh to God must believe that he is
Hebrews11:7 By faith Noah
Hebrews11:8 By faith Abraham
Hebrews11:9 By faith he became a sojourner in the land of promise
Hebrews11:10 for he looked for the city which hath the foundations
Hebrews11:11 By faith even Sarah herself received power to conceive seed when she was past age
Hebrews11:12 wherefore also there sprang of one
Hebrews11:13 These all died in faith
Hebrews11:14 For they that say such things make it manifest that they are seeking after a country of their own.

Hebrews11:16 But now they desire a better country
Hebrews11:17 By faith Abraham
Hebrews11:18 even he to whom it was said
Hebrews11:19 accounting that God is able to raise up

Hebrews11:21 By faith Jacob
Hebrews11:22 By faith Joseph
Hebrews11:23 By faith Moses
Hebrews11:24 By faith Moses
Hebrews11:25 choosing rather to share ill treatment with the people of God

Hebrews11:27 By faith he forsook Egypt
Hebrews11:28 By faith he kept the passover
Hebrews11:29 By faith they passed through the Red sea as by dry land: which the Egyptians assaying to do were swallowed up.
Hebrews11:30 By faith the walls of Jericho fell down
Hebrews11:31 By faith Rahab the harlot perished not with them that were disobedient
Hebrews11:32 And what shall I more say? for the time will fail me if I tell of Gideon

Hebrews11:39 And these all

James3:1 Be not many of you teachers
James3:2 For in many things we all stumble. If any stumbleth not in word
James3:3 Now if we put the horses bridles into their mouths that they may obey us
James3:4 Behold
James3:5 So the tongue also is a little member
James3:6 And the tongue is a fire: the world of iniquity among our members is the tongue
James3:7 For every kind of beasts and birds
James3:8 But the tongue can no man tame; it is a restless evil
James3:9 Therewith bless we the Lord and Father; and therewith curse we men
James3:10 out of the same mouth cometh forth blessing and cursing. My brethren
James3:11 Doth the fountain send forth from the same opening sweet water and bitter?
James3:12 Can a fig tree
James3:13 Who is wise and understanding among you? let him show by his good life his works in meekness of wisdom.
James3:14 But if ye have bitter jealousy and faction in your heart
James3:15 This wisdom is not a wisdom that cometh down from above
James3:16 For where jealousy and faction are
James3:17 But the wisdom that is from above is first pure
James3:18 And the fruit of righteousness is sown in peace for them that make peace.
